this isn’t for the best, my reputation’s never been worse, so you must like me for me

122:00 | airport bombshells

A seventeen hour plane ride wasn't the most ideal scenario to be stuck in. Having so much time to yourself, unable to text or talk to anyone, left her to only do one thing - think. 

And being that it was what she had been trying to avoid doing, she wasn't thrilled about it.

Sighing, she'd practically made herself miserable by going through the photos in her camera roll. There were plenty that had been taken during her time in New York, which made her smile nonetheless. It was crazy to think she was now closer than she ever had been with her siblings and parents. She fixed the nightmare they'd been living in, her father's wrath had hopefully ended, everything was well again at the Lee residence in the Castle.

She hoped that Juyeon and Chaerin would follow their dreams. Solar was already proud of them, even if they weren't the most mature.

It was when she got to photos of Jinhwan that her heart tightened in her chest.


Exhaling, she frowns as she peers down at the photo. It didn't matter if she missed Jungkook sometimes, it didn't matter if even for a split second she felt something for Sunwoo, just one look at him and it all came back to her. All of those strong emotions would explode through her soul, and suddenly, she only wanted one thing.

Even then, she couldn't forget what happened. She couldn't forget what he'd done. There was no reason for Junhoe to lie to her, and from what he said he saw, she wasn't sure what else this situation could be about.

Sighing, she shakes her head, hoping that Junhoe had gotten the message across to Jinhwan about not picking her up from the airport. She needed some time first before she faced him, before she looked him in those shiny eyes again, and asked if it was true. Solar needed to prepare her heart for breakage, she didn't think she could take it right now.

"Wah, it's Kim Soomi!" a hushed voice had eagerly whispered somewhere behind her.

A lump forms in her throat as she continues to stare down at the picture. It wasn't that she was mad there were some fans who recognized her on the plane, she loved talking to them. She just didn't think she was in the best state to really be associating with them. The last thing she needed was for something to get out that she wasn't doing well. She didn't want to worry everyone.

"Seriously, Kim Soomi?!" another girl had gawked.

It'd definitely been a long time since she even thought about calling herself that. The name almost felt ancient at this point.

Soomi, she chuckles to herself, when a girl had suddenly gripped the back of one of the seats and propped herself up so she could see over it. Her eyes bulge out of their sockets when she makes eye contact with the superstar, and her mouth falls open as if she was a hippo.

"O-Oh my god," she stutters, covering her mouth with one hand, "I can't believe it, I thought she was joking!" she tries not to scream, but it was too late since she was practically yelling, "Soomi, I love you, I've been a neverland since debut! You're so talented and you give me so much motivation to keep going!"

The girl started boasting about how much her music spiritually healed her, and it made Solar smile as she meets her eyes. Politely nodding at each word she said, she kindly awaited for her to finish everything she wanted to say.

"That's so sweet of you," she grinned, "it's nice to meet you, what's your name?"

"Arin," she says instantly, before pulling her friend up that was beside her, "this is Kyungmi!"

"Hi," the girl nervously waves, "ah, you're so pretty - I don't know what to do!" her voice was so quiet Solar could just barely make out what she said.

She tries to ease the girl's nerves with her reassuring tone, "you guys don't have to be nervous, I'm completely harmless, really!" she jokes, and the two laugh as if it was the funniest thing they'd ever heard.

Thanks to her kind words, the two fans had calmed down the more they warmed up to her. Solar liked talking to her fans because it always seemed like they'd known each other for years. She didn't have to have any fear of saying the wrong thing, they were understanding and compassionate. It made her happy to spend some time with the two girls she'd met, and before she knew it, they'd spent hours talking.

"Do you think you could autograph this?" Arin had asked, pulling out her newest album and a sharpie. A hopeful grin is on her face as she holds it out to her.

"Sure," Solar hums, taking it carefully from her hands. She casually signs her signature across the top of the album before handing it back to the ecstatic girl, "there you go~."

"Wow," Kyungmi was gushing, "I can't believe we were lucky enough to meet you."

"Did you have fun on your break in New York?" Arin chimed in eagerly, "have you missed your friends?"

The questions made her slightly nervous, but she refused to show it as she responds brightly, "yeah, it was great, but I miss my friends in Korea a lot."

"Do you miss Jinhwan?" Kyungmi had blurted, and Solar's face fell. The girl seemed to notice how her expression changed, as she hurriedly spoke up to apologize, "sorry, I just see rumors online about how close you guys are! I didn't mean to offend you!"

Taking a deep breathe, she shakes her head, "no, no, you didn't," she tells her, and the girl's nerves seem to calm. "I, uh, I do miss Jinhwan," she finally answered after some hesitation, "he's my good friend."

The two girl's stare at her suspiciously for a moment. It was clear they were expecting to hear something else other than the fact they were good friends. 

"Oh," Arin shrugs, "I bet he misses you, too, every time I see a picture of him now he looks sad."

She wondered if there was any truth to her statement. It'd been a long time since she saw a recent photo of him. Maybe it really was a misunderstanding.

Either way, she'd find out soon enough.



+


"We have now landed in Seoul, please make sure you collect all your belongings before exiting the plane," the intercom chimes, "thank you for flying with us!"

Everyone had began to shuffle down the aisles, desperate to get off the plane after having been stuck on it for so long. Solar stays in her seat, watching them all nearly knock each other over as they push and shove. The two girls are still there behind her, waiting for the commotion to die down as well.

After a good five or so minutes, less people were crowding the plane. Figuring it was time, the brunette rises to her feet, putting her hat and the hood to her hoodie over her head. She finishes it off with her mask, taking her bags off the top rack. She only had one suitcase to get from the rest of the luggage that had been stored elsewhere on the plane, and she'd be good to go.

Look out for Jimin in his disguise, she reminds herself as she files out of her seat row. It was when one of the girls nervously clears her throat from behind her that she halts, glancing backwards.

"It was nice to meet you, Soomi!" Kyungmi had been smiling.

"I hope we can meet again!" Arin adds.

She returns their bright smiles with one of her own, "it was nice meeting you both, too," she turns to continue to way out, when she realizes. A little shaken up at the thought, she turns back to the girls, "uh, don't tell anyone you saw me here until tomorrow, alright? I don't want to bring attention to myself."

They didn't seem to find this much of an issue, which she was extremely thankful for. The two of them said, "no problem, Soomi!"

With that, she waves goodbye as she makes her way off the plane. Her eyes rake over all the people as the scent of Korean air fills her nostrils. She was home, her mind alerts her, her real home.

Exhaling, she makes her way to where the luggage was stored. It didn't take her long to pick out her bag, pulling out the handle so she could wheel it along the floor. 

She was finally back, back to face all the issues she once left behind - and new ones. 

All she had to do now was figure out where Jimin was.

With pursed lips, she begins to walk around the area. There were plenty of people who were waiting for passengers that had been on her plane, holding up signs with bright smiles on their faces. She watched as couples and family members collided, hugging each other passionately and gushing about how long it had been. Seeing things like that left a sour feeling inside of her, one that she wished she could crush like a bug. 

She really didn't want to think about Jinhwan. It hurt every time she did. 

Sighing, she continues her search, wondering if she should just ask Jimin where the hell he was. Though with all the bags in her arms, getting out her phone would be quite the hassle. She didn't need to call attention to herself, that was the plan. Surely she could find him without having to call him.

It wasn't like he was going to be holding up a sign with the letters Soomi on it, but couldn't he at least give her some sort of hint?

Cursing to herself, she awkwardly pushes through crowds of people, mumbling low excuse me's and I'm sorry's. No one seemed to really look at her, which was great. 

But Jimin, she groaned, where the hell are you?

Just when she's about to give up, tired of seemingly playing hide and seek with her best friend, Solar sets her bag on the ground about to dig her phone out, someone grabbed her forearm. She was pulled away with a gasp, and almost dropped her bag in the process.

The poor girl was too busy trying to make sure nothing fell out of it to see what was happening. It was when they stopped in front of the bathroom that she could finally zip her bag, breathing heavily from shock. Holding a hand to her heart, she slowly stands up straight, turning to the stranger who just pulled a move like that.

Brown, curly locks peek out from under the beanie they were wearing. Their eyes are a golden brown under the lights of the airport, and she would've been able to pick out that doe shape anywhere. 

It was when the boy slowly moved his mask downward to reveal his bunny teeth smile that she lost it.

"Jungkook?!" she screeches, her voice flying what seemed like eighty octaves upward. Her facial expression was like she'd just seen the most shocking thing known to man, but what could one expect? She was not supposed to see Jungkook here.

As she thought about it, she realized why he was here. She was going to kill Jimin later.

"Surprise, Mi," he giggles, adjusting the beanie on his head, hair falling in his eyes as he does so, "I missed you."

Before she could utter a word, he pulled her into a hug. Jungkook's cologne was strong like it always was, and she hadn't smelt it since that day. That day, the day she told him, the day she broke his heart, was the last time she'd seen him. It was insane to think about how long it had been since they spoke, the two who were practically inseparable at a point in time.

He was warm, and she couldn't help but wrap her arms around his torso under his leather jacket. She couldn't believe that he really wanted to see her. She knew Jimin had to do with this, but she also knew it hadn't been his idea. If Jungkook had done something like this, it was all him.

She guessed he really had missed her this entire time after all, Jimin hadn't been kidding.

"I missed you, too," she finally mumbles into his chest.

There's still that smile on his lips when he pulls away, eyes daunting down at her. She took that as the time to take in his apparel, which was all black, from his shoes to his beanie. He was something else, you couldn't deny it. Everything about him was special.

It was obvious as to why she had missed him, why he had found a way onto her mind when Jinhwan didn't. Just look at him.

"You're not disguised that great," she finally says, going on her tiptoes to brush his hair to the side so she could actually see his eyes, "how hasn't anyone recognized you yet?"

Seriously, he was probably the most famous singer in the country. The fact that no one had noticed him being here when he looked like this was just wrong.

(For reference ig)



He only shrugs, pulling his mask back on his face, "I know how to keep a low profile, Mi, it's not that hard."

She only peers up at him in disbelief. Turning to look at all the people around them, she scoffs, "no way, I can't believe this," she shakes her head, "hey people, Jungkook of BTS is right here!" she jokingly raises her voice just a little loud. It still was no where near being loud enough for anyone around them to hear.

Either way, it took him off guard. He laughs as he grabs her hand, covering her mouth with his other, "Mi, I really don't think you want to get caught."

He only took his hand off when she bit it, but was in fits of laughter instead of being mad at her. "you're right," she hums, "I really do not."

The two walk hand in hand toward the exit of the airport. Her hand always seemed to fit with his in a perfect way, one she could never explain. Those feelings she had for Jungkook that had resurfaced all that time ago when she was still in NYC, texting Jimin in complete confusion, where beginning to shine. 

But those emotions she still felt for Jinhwan weren't gone either.

Despite this, she didn't want the moment to end. She didn't want to let his hand go just yet. It was when people starting glancing over at them that she finally did, allowing her arm to fall back to her side.

Jungkook looks over to her as they walk, finally voicing, "so when are you going to tell me what's been going on? I know I've missed a lot. Jimin said something happened."

She'd been expecting the question, and there it was. 

"It's a lot," she admits after some hesitation, "I don't even know where to start."

"Well, what's it about?"

"Uh, the stalker's partner, Jinhwan, everything that happened at home," she begins to list, "you."

He raises a brow at the last part, having expected most of the first three. "I'm intrigued by the last one," he teases, before growing serious again, "but tell me about it, I suspected you were still in trouble, but you're having problems with that idiot? Can't say I'm surprised."

"Jungkook," she sighs, "don't call him an idiot."

"You have to admit he's a little slow."

"Jungkook," she warns, and he rolls his eyes. Exhaling, she thinks a bit about telling him. Did she really want him to know about everything that happened with Jinhwan before she even knew the full story herself?

Pursing her lips, she struggles to find her words as she finally blurts, "he, uh, he apparently cheated on me."

"He what?!" Jungkook deadpans, eyes widening as he turns to her. They were just by the exit now, people on top of people passing them to get where they needed to be. Jungkook didn't even care that they were blocking the walk way as he grabs her wrist so she stops, too, "Soomi, what the hell happened? How does someone apparently cheat? I'm going to fucking beat him to a pulp."

Jungkook was clearly feeling a mix of emotions. She tries her best to calm them as she shakes her head vigorously, "I don't know for sure, Junhoe just told me he saw something weird, alright? There's no need to get worked up yet."

"If he saw something weird that's probably a pretty big sign," he argues as she starts walking again, not caring that he was still holding onto her wrist.

"Jungkook, I'm going to talk to him first," she states stubbornly.

The boy sighs as he walks with her again, "it just seems pretty clear to me, what explanation would there be?"

Even though he had a point, she didn't want to believe it - not yet. Solar felt like she owed that to their relationship.

"I don't know," she mutters as they walk out the doors.

The windy air slaps her in the face, and she winces at how the weather was. Jungkook is close by her side, a dull look on his face as he thinks about what she'd told him.

"Where did you park?" she asks him, eyes raking over all the cars in the parking lot.

But before he could respond, a squeal leaves her lips as her hat flies off her head. Her green eyes widen, and she turns in every direction to try to figure out where it went. That was just her luck.

The wind calms down slightly, allowing her to actually hear. She glances around to the sound of footsteps approaching, and turns in hope that someone had picked up her lost belonging.

Though she never expected to see him.


123:00 | right here

"Soomi."

Her name left his lips like a breathe of fresh air. The sky was dark, no sun in sight, but yet his eyes sparkled when they landed on her. Seeing him was like she was in a nightmare, but yet so ecstatic. A part of her was so scared whilst the other wanted nothing more than to run to him. Even though Jungkook was right beside her, she wanted to just hug him like it was the last time she ever would.

But Jungkook...

Solar only seems to snap back into reality when he approaches her. His caramel hair blows in the wind, and her breath hitches as he reaches her. Jinhwan doesn't overstep his boundaries, though, he's careful. A small smile inches it's way on his face as he holds out her hat, eyes nervously meeting her own.

Jungkook's fists were clenched at his sides. He was on the verge of exploding after what Soomi just informed him of. It was becoming rather hard for him to keep it inside, with the way this boy had the audacity to show up here, the way he wasn't even bothering to acknowledge Jungkook's presence. He really wanted to physically give him a piece of his mind.

"Jinhwan," it felt bittersweet saying his name again. She was breathless, a mistress in distress, as she tried to figure out what to do in this situation, "why are you here? Didn't Junhoe tell you not to come?" Those were the questions she asked him, not about the cheating, not about what he'd been doing, but that.

It hurt him to hear her ask that. It hurt him to not be able to hug her. But Jinhwan knew there was a lot of fixing to do, he knew she was thinking things that weren't right.

"We need to talk," he offers, eyes diverting to the ground.

The girl stares at him for a long while. She tried her best to read his body language, to understand, to try to figure out if he was looking guilty or not. But it was unclear to her, and she was left in a daze of not knowing what to say.

Jungkook tugs on her hand, urging her to just leave with him. She'd said it herself, she wasn't ready to face him yet, but yet here he was before her. With what happened, she hadn't expected him to show.

The fact he did had to count for something.

But she couldn't, not right now, there was too much on her mind. Solar needed to settle back in, to get used to this world again, this life. She wasn't ready to face the truth, she wasn't ready to possibly hear that her boyfriend had cheated on her with someone she didn't know.

"I can't now," she finally mutters, sending him a sympathetic look whether he deserved it or not, "I-I'm just not ready to."

Jinhwan was sad, his eyes are dimmer than they once were seconds ago. Still, he finds a way to smile at her, nodding, "yeah, I understand, Soo, just call me when you're ready, okay? I promise it's not what you think."

His words made a small ping strike her heart. It gave her hope, and her mind screamed at her that it all had to be wrong. Even then, she knew she had to stay true to what she said. 

"Okay, I'll see you soon, Jinhwan."

Before she could bother to say another word, Jungkook tugs on her hand hard enough to drag her away. She squeaks from the impact of his strength, practically slamming into his chest.

"You better not forgive him like that, Mi," he warns in disappointment, "especially when I'm right here."

"Kookie," she sighs as he looks down at her with those taunting eyes of his, "I wasn't, you heard me tell him I didn't want to talk."

"Right," he hums, showing that bunny tooth smile, "just forget about him for a while, Mi, spend some time with me."

With the way he was staring down at her, how was she supposed to say no? It wasn't like she would be doing anything wrong. 

Rolling her eyes, she squeezes his hand as she responds, "well, I'm here with you, aren't I?"

But yet she was still thinking about Jinhwan.




+




"Well," Junhoe raises a brow, eyes unimpressed, "where's Soomi?"

From his monotone voice, you could tell he didn't expect the short boy to show up with her. Jinhwan only throws him a glare, a dull look on his face as he slams the front door behind him and falls onto one of the sofas in their living room. 

The dark haired boy watches his hyung carefully. He was clearly torn up about this, but he didn't know if it was out of guilt or sadness that this had happened. Though it seemed that his accusations weren't exactly true, what other explanation could there be? You didn't just allow a girl to cling to you like that, spend all this time with her drinking and doing God knows what, not without something bad happening - or at least looking like it was going to happen.

Still, whether it was true or not, at the end of the day, Jinhwan was still his eldest hyung. He was a compassionate friend who had gotten him through a lot of shit. Even though he adored Soomi as his beloved best friend, he couldn't just throw Jinhwan in the dirt, even if he was very angry at him.

Exhaling, he hesitantly sits on the sofa next to him. Jinhwan's face is buried in his hands, and he doesn't look up to the sudden presence. "So, what is the truth, hyung?"

Raising his head, there's a bitter look on his face as he sarcastically laughs, "what does it matter? You already made up the truth, anyway."

Now that made him feel bad.

"What was I supposed to think?" he stands up for himself stubbornly, "You were out with your ex all the time, who is literally a psychopath. She shows up at the studio, clinging to you like you've been together all this time, and you don't push her off, what did you expect me to think?!" he repeats his question again in disbelief. Thinking it over, he really was not wrong to make such an assumption.

"You just said it, June-ya," Jinhwan scolds him in frustration that he wasn't smart enough to put the puzzle pieces together himself, "she's a psychopath, I can't get her to leave me alone ever since we ran into each other when I went to drink alone at the bar."

The tall boy purses his lips in thought, "you could at least push her off, not make it look like you have some secret affair."

"But I didn't!" he exclaims, "I love Soomi, I'd never do that to her. Now I look like this horrible person because you told her all these things, I can't even face her!"

Junhoe's face falls as he processes it all. So Jinhwan didn't really cheat on Soomi, did he? He knew Jinhwan's ex was crazy, but could he really not get her to leave him alone? The fact she was an idol made it easy for her to get in and out of the company, but it wasn't like she even worked at YG. Why the hell did they even let her in?

"Sorry, hyung," he finally mutters after a while. 

Jinhwan doesn't say anything for a while. His head is too full of clouds for him to process anything. All he could think about was Soomi, how she was with Jungkook, smiling and laughing like she had no cares in the world. The way he'd wrap his arm around her shoulders, towering over her with his massive height, those doe eyes twinkling like stars when he looked down at her.

He hated it, he hated it because he couldn't get mad at her. He hated it because she had a right to be mad at him. Junhoe wasn't lying when he said that Jinhwan had been stuck around his ex, though it wasn't on his own terms. The girl had been clinging to him like they were together, but it wasn't like that. Still, it wasn't a good excuse.

So what could he do if she wanted to be around Jungkook? What could he do if they did something he didn't want to think about? Everyone in the world knows, Soomi and Jungkook have chemistry.

"Maybe you should both just take a break," Junhoe suggests slowly, as if he was scared for once that his hyung was going to backhand him, "I know that you know she's with Jungkook, and it's not like either of you are really ready to get all cutesy again."

He didn't want to say it, but he was right. A break from their relationship would be the best option right now. 

"Yeah," he sighs, "you're right, June-ya."



+



"SOOMI!" Jimin is screaming at the top of his lungs, and it was the first time she had ever seen her best friend so worked up. He practically pushes every one of the boys over, including Jungkook, with his tiny body before tackling the girl in one of the tightest hugs she'd ever experienced, "I missed you!"

"Chim," she breathes out as he squeezes her, only able to catch her breathe when he realizes it's too much and lets go. Able to speak normally, she sends him a smile, "I missed you, too!"

The other boys crowd around her, all hugging and welcoming her back. She was reminded of why she adored the Bangtan boys so much, they managed to make her feel right at home without even trying.

"We ordered take out a while ago," Jin shares, "feel free to have some, should be here any minute."

"Thanks, Jin," she offers, but he only responds with a wink.

Jungkook had been watching from the sidelines for a while now. He was trying not to be selfish and give her some time to talk and see everyone. It was when she and Yoongi had been talking about music, specifically about some songs she sent him weeks ago and how he liked them, that he began to grow impatient.

"Okay!" he clears his throat to interrupt their conversation. It catches the girl off guard as she looks up to him, jumping at the fact he was suddenly right beside her, "you guys can talk about this later, right?"

Yoongi blinks at him, not very amused by his disrespect, "I don't see what's wrong with right now."

"Well I do," Jungkook hums, grabbing her hand and dragging her away before his hyung can say anything else.

Soomi couldn't help but laugh out of disbelief as he drags her down the hall of their massive home. Pushing open his door with his back, he's grinning like a little kid as he pulls the girl inside, holding her bags with his other arm.

He only lets her hand go so he can place her things on the ground. "Welcome home, Mi," he announces, "assuming you want to stay here."

Her eyes widened at his sudden offer. Realizing it was probably a little quick, he goes on to explain, "you were staying with Jinhwan before, right? I know your apartment probably still isn't safe with how you mentioned there's still stuff going on with that, so why not stay here?" With that smile, he motions around his massive, darkly decorated bedroom.

She lets her eyes rake over it all, the decor a little different than when she last saw it. It was weird to think how long it had been, she wondered how much she had missed from their lives.

"Sure," she finally shrugs, "but you have to give me rides, I don't have my car."

He shrugs it off, too happy about the fact she agreed as he falls onto his mattress, "deal."

Clearing her throat, a wave of silence washes over the two. She's unsure what to do with all the emotions building up inside of her. Too much had happened at once. She was back in Korea, Jungkook - the boy she had been missing at one point - was right in front of her, she saw Jinhwan. She didn't know what to do with all the confusion building up in her. 

She hesitantly walks to sit on the bed. Jungkook was busy lounging on it, probably tired from whatever schedule he had today. His brown eyes curiously peer at the girl, and he finally asks the question she had been dreading, "so, do you wanna talk about it?"

Trying to act gullible, she looks to him, "about what?"

He rolls his eyes, shifting so he was now sitting up straight so he could fully stare at her. "I know you're thinking a lot, and we didn't get to talk much at the airport, so go. You can't tell me there's not more on your mind after that run in."

Pursing her lips, she couldn't help but break their eye contact. Solar focuses on fiddling with her thumbs in her lap, trying to figure out if she wanted to have such a conversation with Jungkook out of all people. This would be a lot more comfortable if it was with Jimin, who she didn't even get to talk to much at all yet thanks to this clingy boy.

"I was just surprised," she finally admits, "he wasn't supposed to show up, but there he was. I had it so thought out in my mind there couldn't be a good explanation, but then he said it's not what I think it is. I want to talk to him - but at the same time - I really don't, I'm scared."

Before she knew it, she poured out her entire heart to the boy. "I think we needed a break anyway, I was having a lot of trouble back home," her voice grew quieter with each word she said. It was something she was scared to say, but she would've had to eventually.

Of course this intrigued him, "what do you mean?"

"I - uh - thought about you a lot, I missed you a lot," she awkwardly mumbles, "I'm not saying I didn't miss Jinhwan but there was a part of me that almost longed for you."

She still had yet to look up at him, she was horrified to. That had to be one of the weirdest things she'd ever said. 

When she finally gets the courage, her eyes gradually glance upwards. Jungkook had been staring at her the entire time, a goofy smile slapped across his face. His cheeks looked more fluffy than ever, eyes tinted with glee as he giggled, "that's so cute, Mi."

There's a lump in her throat she didn't know was there until she nearly chokes on it. She only shakes her head in disbelief at herself, diverting her eyes again, "I'm just being honest with you, I talked to Jimin a lot about it. Now that this has happened with Jinhwan, I don't really know where to go."

Looking up again, a tiny yelp leaves her mouth at the sight of the boy abruptly right before her. He'd scooted so close their legs were grazing, faces only inches apart. Those doe eyes taunt her as he flicks his irises from her lips to her green eyes, a devious grin on his face. 

"That's an easy question to answer," he hums, "right here."

It wasn't like she could push him away. Who could push Jungkook away? No one. She was caught, caught in his trap of charm. 

This was why she could never say no, she couldn't stop his face growing closer. All she could do was meet those eyes and feel herself giving in more. 

Just as his lips gently graze hers at first, before they fully collide in a euphoric harmony. She'd lost.



jinani: soo, i think we need to take a break



124:00 | caution: danger

"Oh, Mi," Jimin sighs, a saddened look in those shiny eyes of his as he shakes his head, "I can't believe that, something isn't right there."

The brunette nods in longing agreement, a deep exhale leaving her lips as she leans back into the cushion of the sofa, "I know, I just don't feel like I'm ready to face it yet, you know? I know maybe it's not as bad as I think it is, but that doesn't mean it's still not bad, right?"

Her best friend thinks about it for a moment, before shrugging. Jimin hadn't loved her and Jinhwan, although he found it slightly amusing seeing Jungkook actually get defeated for once. On the other side, he didn't want her to be hurting, either. While he didn't like nor dislike him, he couldn't help but think this seemed weird. Everyone of the boys had seen the way he looked at Soomi, it was similar to Jungkook, and they knew how much their youngest loved her. So why would him cheating make any sense?

Even though deep down he'd always wish he was in the running, he knew that couldn't ever be true. So he had to settle with preferring Jungkook or Jinhwan, and right now, he was unsure. All he knew was he needed to be there for her and tell her the honest truth.

"I think whatever it is, it's going to be hard for you to hear," he finally explains, "I think you do need some time to process and prepare, so it's good you're on a break." The girl nods to show she was listening, when he goes on, "but that doesn't mean you should be all over Jungkook, Mi. I know that talk we had while you were in New York, but do you still feel like that now?"

She winces from the question. This was not what she had the energy to think about. "I don't know," she mutters, "this all happened just when I thought I had everything figured out. Jungkook kinda creeps up on you, you know."

He ponders for a second before humming, "I guess it's the chemistry, remember what I told you?"

"Yeah," she sighs, her mind wandering through all the years they spent together, "yeah, I know."

A few seconds pass, and a comfortable silence eases their minds. Friends like Soomi and Jimin could sit like that for hours, whether they were watching a show, talking, or doing absolutely nothing. The two were close enough to just be so comfortable in each other's presence it didn't matter what they were doing. It was why she always loved being around him, he made her feel safe.

Leaning her head back into the cushion, she closes her eyes to take a five minute nap. Jimin props his legs up on the coffee table, yawning as he does the same. He was thinking a lot not only about the Jinhwan situation, but more so about the fact she still had someone after her. After what went down at KCON, he figured they would've seriously given it up. He didn't even want to think about what Soomi told him happened.

It was when an abrupt voice snapped her out of her slumber, a presence falling onto the couch beside her, "so, been working on anything lately?"

She glances up to the expectant boy beside her. Yoongi's eyes are tinted with excitement as they meet hers. Straightening her posture, there's a certain drive that riles up her body as she ponders his question, "hm, you have no idea!"

Not wanting to waste any time, he raises a brow, "well, where's it at? Let's get to it."

She was more than thankful for this sudden opportunity. Working on music with Yoongi was more than enjoyable, it was something she had missed doing. Not to mention, it took her mind off everything. Jimin knows this, and he smiles to himself as he watches the two interact. Time like that was just what she needed, so he had no reason to be mad at his hyung for interrupting.

"Alright, let's go!" she cheerily applauds, "you're gonna like what I worked on in New York."

They rise to their feet, chatting about what she'd done while she was there. A catch up session between the two friends was desperately needed. 

"You have to come out with an album soon, don't you?" he was asking as they walked down the hall towards his studio.

"Yup, I'm on work duty again now that I'm back," she sighs, "I'm not sure what I'd do if I didn't have you, Yoongs."

"Don't worry," he shrugs as they reach the door, inputting the code, "we can get you a full album in no time."

And that they would do.


+


"Yah, Jeon Jungkook!" the petite brunette snaps as she pushes open the bedroom door, feet padding against the carpet, "I need you to be my Uber!"

Her eyes scan across the bedroom, expecting to see him playing video games or something of the sort. The Bangtan boys had been having some much needed time off recently, meaning they were finally getting to do normal things just like that. She knew Jungkook loved games after all.

What she did not expect was to see him standing there in all his glory, body bare with a mere towel wrapped around his waist. Brief droplets of water from the heated shower he presumably just took cover his toned back that was facing her, and her green eyes wander helplessly to his muscled biceps. It was a sight that always caused her breathe to hitch in her throat, and she tried to repeat Jimin's words in her head before she exploded and did something she shouldn't be doing right now.

I need to take it slow, I need to think, she tells herself, squeezing her eyes shut momentarily to regain her sanity. But when she opened him, the boy had spun around from where he was peering at himself in the mirror to look at her instead. 

"Oh, Mi," he sighs, "don't bother hiding it, I know exactly what you're thinking."

"Shut up," she immediately retorts, "I promise you right now is not the time."

Of course he didn't care for her words. Instead, he walks right up to her anyway. Every step he takes makes her heartbeat accelerate, and as much as she tried to tell herself she didn't want it, she knew she did.

But it really wasn't the time.

Just when he's right before her, hands reaching out to rest on her hips, she hurriedly opens her mouth before he could say something that would break her, "I have to go back to work today, I told CEO-nim and my producer I'd be there," she explains in a rush, "now that I'm back in Korea, I'm back to work, and they want me to work on a new album."

Jungkook doesn't say anything for a moment. He was likely thinking about what she just said, pondering what this meant for her. For once, then, he gave up his teasing antics to talk seriously, his hands rubbing calming circles on her hips, "I know that much," he hums, before raising a brow, "but doesn't this mean you have to see that toddler?"

She tries not to laugh, rolling her eyes, "did you just call him a toddler?"

He only nods.

"Okay, yeah, that is what it means," she shrugs, "but I don't have to talk to him, I'll be working in my studio anyway - not theirs."

Pursing his lips, he hesitates before slowly asking, "did you ever talk again after the airport incident?"

"No."

He exhales upon hearing this, his mind wandering about what it possibly could be. Jungkook had mixed feelings about it, he couldn't lie. If Jinhwan really did cheat on Soomi, he would be more than angry. He was already beyond pissed that he had even done something close to that. The last thing he ever wanted was to see her hurt. Jungkook wanted nothing more than to protect her. 

However, at the same time, if their relationship ended for sure thanks to Jinhwan's idiotic actions, it meant that Soomi would do one thing and one thing only - come back to him. And he knew that he could make her forget every horrible thing that dumb ass ever did to her. After all, she was meant to be with him.

So call him selfish, but he couldn't say he didn't want it to all be true. Jungkook was selfish, he was greedy for the girl he loved.

"Talk to him when you're ready," he tells her soothingly, "don't let him force you into any uncomfortable conversation."

Her eyes look up to him from her smaller height. Jungkook's irises are a light shade of brown, and they seem to be shinier the more she stares into them. It was like an ocean you find yourself lost in, one she was happy to be drowning in. 

Finally, she smiles, shaking her head as she breaks their eye contact, "I'll be okay, Kookie, the others have my back." She was in a state of disbelief at him, this silly boy who always wanted to mess with her could easily be serious and give her the best advice. He could do everything when it was the right time to. 

"But if he bothers you, call me, okay?" he puts a finger under her chin when he says this, forcing her to look at him again, "I'll be there in a second and I would be so honored to kick his ass."

"Jungkook," she sternly states, "that won't be necessary, nothing is happening today other than me making music."

He just rolls his eyes, letting go of her to go grab his keys off his dresser. They jingle in his fingers as he throws some clothes on, humming, "I'm just making sure you know, Mi."


+


Tap, tap.

Tap, tap, tap.

Tap.

Sunwoo's heel hitting the glistening wooden floors of the gang house was all anyone had heard for the last hour. It was only one day since he had said goodbye to the love of his life, allowing her to get on a plane and go across the world from him. One day and he was already worried helplessly, nonetheless, angry that this person had the audacity to pull the things they had.

It was like they didn't know who he was. He was fucking Kim Sunwoo, you didn't pull things like this with the most well known gang leader in the dark world of NYC. Either this guy was a complete imbecile, or he was so crazy he didn't care. Sunwoo wasn't sure he wanted to know which one it was.

The other members are quiet, they have been for a long time. Since Sunwoo had gotten back they could tell there was a big problem. Just from the way he walked you could see he was more than mad. Knowing he was thinking of what they should do, all the boys knew to do was wait for their leader's instructions. Even though they had no idea what exactly happened, just the fact it had to do with Solar meant it was extremely serious.

Every once in a while, they would exchange glances with each other. It was becoming worrisome Sunwoo had yet to say anything. Was he out of ideas? Did he really not know what to do for the first time? Someone like Sunwoo actually lost was the equivalent of the world ending - no one wanted to see it.

"How bad is it?" Sangyeon finally speaks up after clearing his throat. They couldn't stand the silence anymore, they had to figure this out.

However, instead of an immediate response, all the brunette boy does is heave out a sigh. He was stressed, it was clear, as he leaned his head back in the chair he was sitting in, rubbing his temples with a grimace. When he sits up straight again, his brows furrow in contemplation, trying to put together what he should tell his members.

Everyone watches him as he runs a hand through his messy hair, nearly shivering from what was to come. 

"This person that's after Solar," he begins, "they're dangerous."

"Didn't we already establish that?" Eric blinks, "did you think they were kidding?"

Sunwoo's glare made the boy sink into the sofa. Bad time for a joke.

"I took her and her siblings to a special place so she could have a memorable last day," he explains the occurrence of a few nights ago, "but it was sabotaged by that fuck, they sent shooters - but I think they were going for Chaerin. When they couldn't hit her, they just started blazing at us, it's no fucking joke."

He groans again, tapping his foot on the ground rapidly as he stresses, "the bug in her room, the fact they broke into the fucking Castle, it's something that takes practice. I thought it would be a breeze, but they were even there at the airport yesterday."

The boys peer at him in confusion. "Did they pull something there, too?"

"No," he sighs, pulling out his phone and showing them the text, "but I think they're going to - and I mean soon."


125:00 | the ex

"Mi, seriously, I swear to God if that guy tries anything I will not hesitate to go inside and beat his ass," Jungkook was saying, his toned hands gripping the steering wheel of the car. He'd taken a sharp turn into the parking lot, and the girl peers out at the building she hadn't seen in what felt like forever. She used to come here everyday without a second thought, but looking at it now left a weird feeling inside of her. 

After everything that happened with the manager and now this new stalker, it was weird to come back here casually. As far as she knew, their partner could be inside the building as they spoke. And now to wrap it all together, so was Jinhwan, who she couldn't bear to face right now.

Shaking her head, she sighs at the persistent boy beside her as the car stops in the back of the parking lot where they wouldn't be seen, "Jungkook, we already went over this."

Putting the car in park, he puts his hands up in defense, "I know, I just want you to keep it in mind."

Him messing around did always make her feel better. Something about Jungkook just made her heart skip beats, it always had. But Soomi wasn't feeling well recently, even though she was excited to get in the studio and show her producers all she had been working on. So much had happened that she felt like she still wasn't ready. She could barely process anything going on in her life, was she really ready to be back in the spotlight?

Jungkook's eyes stare at her from the driver's seat. He had known from the beginning she was off, how could she not be? As much as he didn't want to bother her about it, he couldn't help himself. He just wanted to help her as much as he could.

"Mi," he breathes, reaching out to place a hand on her thigh, "I know there's a lot happening in that head of yours, okay? If you're not ready to go back to work, you don't have to."

Exhaling, she leans her head on the cold window of the car, thinking for a mere moment, "I know I need to, but I'm just - I don't know - I don't know anything anymore."

Jungkook hesitates, wondering if it was the right time for him to ask. His eyes wander away from her, back to the company building right before them. All she had told him was that there was still someone out there for her, someone who kept doing things to spite her, but he didn't know the details. What he did realize, though, was the fact that the last person had been around Soomi, plotting the entire time, while she was just working inside that building. He couldn't help but think maybe this person was, too.

Pursing his lips, his brown irises glance at the girl again. Her head was buried in her hands, and she looked like she wanted to sink into the ground. Going back to work would not be the move for her today. Even though she was so ready this morning, now that she was actually here, everything was hitting her. It was like a rapid storm of fear that had suddenly drowned her when she least expected it.

"Do you want to go back to the house?" he finally asks, "Don't force yourself to go, Mi."

With a sigh, she shakes her head, finally sitting up straight again. "I have to, Jungkook."

+


The YG building was exactly the same as she recalled it. Modern interior, hip hop music playing from the speakers, fancily dressed employees - it was the place she learned to call home. One she felt so comfortable in.

But now she couldn't help but be paranoid. Solar strikes a smile as she waves to the workers who welcome her back, cheering that they missed her bright presence. 

It could be anyone, anywhere. Any of these people could be the one, someone who hated her with every ounce in their body.

A shiver runs up her spine, but she shakes it off. All she needed to focus on was music - not her boy issues or the stalker - just her music. Her and Yoongi had spent lots of time on it for a reason, she needed to use it.

Letting out a deep breath, she calls the elevator. Only seconds later, it dung, and the doors opened before her. She stepped inside, inputting the right floor. Unfortunately, the floor that her studio was on was also where the iKON studio was.

But surely they weren't here today, right? At least not Jinhwan, he never spent much time at the studio. 

Though he had been talking about wanting to make music more often nowadays.

Please no, she whined mentally, the doors had opened before her to the floor, and she stepped out in a rush. Soomi wanted to book it to her studio and just lock the doors, but she knew if anyone saw her they would think she was crazy. So, she tries to calmly walk down the halls as if she had no cares in the world.

You won't see him, he's busy in the studio - if he is here, she tried to give herself a pep talk. Her eyes watched her feet, horrified that if she looked up she would see Jinhwan. It was so scary to her she was nearly shaking.

She shouldn't be reacting to things so badly, and she was for only one reason.

Drug withdrawal, everything was more intense than it should be. It was making all of this ten times worse.

"Fuck!" she screeches, a body suddenly colliding with hers. The bag she had been carrying with her journals and laptop inside nearly fell to the ground when she caught it, but whoever she bumped into hadn't been so lucky. The drink they were carrying hits the wood floor with a splash, spilling in a rushed puddle on the ground. 

Solar groans at herself mentally, looking up to apologize to the person. It was when their eyes met, and she saw that it was some sort of petite girl, she paused. Not because she had something against this stranger, but because of what she did.

"Are you kidding me?!" she cries in bewilderment, her eyes on the ground where her drink spilled, "I can't believe you did that, are you not going to clean up the mess you made?!"

Pause.

Blinking, she tries to process if she just heard that correctly. Her green eyes look back down to the spilled drink, then back up to the girl before her. "Excuse me?" she responds calmly, "you could've also avoided me, there's no need to throw blame."

"There is, because you spilled his drink!" she snaps back, "he's going to be mad, I made it just how he likes it, too!"

Is this girl - mentally okay? was Solar's current thought. 

"Who the fuck even are you?" she blurts rudely, "I'm the most well known person in this building and I've never seen you here, so you should probably not speak to me like that again, you know, unless you want to get kicked out."

She was in bewilderment from the words that just came out of Solar's mouth. It wasn't her fault, she tended to accidentally get a little too extra with people. But after all, she had been rude first, that meant it was her turn.

"Right," the girl rolls her eyes, "you're Soomi or whatever your name is - I'm not really interested. You can't get me kicked out, I'm here with someone who's a well known artist here, too."

Thinking back to whatever weird shit she had been saying about making a drink for someone, she raises a brow, "okay, and who might that be?"

Solar was not in the mood to deal with whoever this suspicious girl was. People that weren't artists weren't even supposed to be on this floor, nonetheless people who didn't even work here in the first place. It was a complete mystery as to how this weirdo had gotten on the floor, and she was feeling rather annoyed with it - and the fact she somehow had the audacity to catch an attitude with her out of all people. 

Not that she wanted to throw her titles around, but she was Kim Soomi.

When the girl didn't reply for more than a few seconds, she was ready to call security. It was when the sound of rushed footsteps was heard from somewhere behind her, followed by the ding of the elevator. Solar hoped it was someone who worked here coming to get this crazy woman out of the building, but before she could turn around to check, an all too familiar voice cut through.

"Jimin, what are you - Soomi?" the tone changed as soon as her name came off his lips. Her eyes bulge out of their sockets, and her heart falls to the ground as she comprehends what she was hearing.

Jinhwan, he was there, she heard his footsteps approaching behind her. Jimin, the name he said before hers. Everything replayed in her head like a movie - every word Junhoe had told her, everything that happened, what he said at the airport - we need to take a break.

It slapped her in the face like a hurricane of trauma. This was the girl.

"Oppa," the girl cheers, running over to him as he hesitantly stops before the both of them. She stepped right inside his personal space, clinging onto his arm like she owned him. The sight of this thing grabbing onto Jinhwan - Kim fucking Jinhwan - was something she wasn't prepared to see. It was the last thing she needed right now. "Sorry, I made your coffee just how you liked it, but she ran into me and it spilled!" she cried as if she was a kid telling a teacher on another kid. 

Hearing the way she spoke made her grimace, so much so she literally facepalmed to show her massive disappointment. This had to be some sort of joke. She understood what he said back at the airport, and she tried to take it into consideration. But seeing them like this, knowing she was making herself at home at the studio, it left her with an answer to it all that was too clear.

It had to be true.

Meanwhile, the caramel boy was caught in a daze. He was in another world, eyes gazing at the only girl he ever cared about. Seeing Soomi was like a breath of fresh air to him, one he'd been missing more than anything. Typing that text he sent to her took every ounce inside of him, but the boys had told him it would be for the best until he could get Jimin off his back and Soomi had time to think and settle back into life in Korea. He felt like they were right, they needed time away. Sometimes that was what it took to fix a relationship - separation.

But how would he fix it when she just caught him with this girl clinging onto his arm?

"Jimin," he snaps under his breath, shrugging the girl off his arm, "it's fine, I didn't ask you to make it anyway."

A gasp leaves her lips as she stumbles a bit from the force he used to get her off him. He hadn't done that before, and it left her lost. She pouts as she looks at him, "what was that for?! There's no need to be mean to me just because of your drink, it was her fault!"

Solar's blood was boiling. She had no idea what was going on anymore. This wasn't what she needed today, as hard as it was for her to gather the guts to come back in this building, this was the worst welcoming. 

Before she clocked this psycho in the face, she inhaled deeply. Spinning on her heel, not another word left her lips as she headed full speed to her studio.

That was all she wanted, was to sit down and make music. Of course it wouldn't be that easy though, not after this horrid occurrence.

"Soo, wait!" Jinhwan calls, his voice cracking near the end as his feet rush after her. Hearing his calls only made her walk faster, she couldn't do this right now. As much as she was trying to calm herself down, it was becoming harder and harder. Everything was catching up to her too quickly, and the drug withdrawals were only making it that much worse on her. 

Before she could stop it, tears rushed to her eyes. She was crying, like the pathetic girl she was. How could she not? She caught him, she caught him red handed in her opinion, with the girl he was apparently trying to get away from. 

"Soomi, please!" he pleaded one last time, just when she was going to reach her studio. Even though there could be workers around that saw them, even though there were cameras in clear sight, he reaches out to grab her wrist. The brunette winces as she's spun around, forced to face her worst nightmare.

He opens his mouth to say something, probably to begin to try to explain, but as soon as he sees the fact she was crying, every word seemed to escape from his brain. Jinhwan felt like shit, he felt like the worst person to ever walk the planet. How could he do this to her? How could he do any of this to her? He hurt the only person he ever loved, and for what?

All because he couldn't push people away.

"Oppa, do you know her?" Jimin had been asking in confusion - or more like demanding, "tell me you don't know her."

Hand still wrapped around her wrist, the boy closes his eyes to hold in his anger. Turning to the girl rapidly, he practically hisses in frustration, "it's not your business, is it? Of course I fucking know her."

Jimin's mouth fell agape from what he just said. Never had he spoken to her in such a way. She tried to say something else, but he was paying no attention. Instead, he turned back to the brunette, who was just watching in disturbance as silent tears rolled down her cheeks. Jimin was confused, she was trying to put together the pieces as to what was going on here. She didn't want to think these two had some sort of thing. Just the thought of her Jinhwan being with someone else made her angry.

"Soo, I'm sorry, can we talk?" he begs, his thumb rubbing the back of her hand as he tries to calm her, "please let me explain, I know I wanted to give it time but I can't take this."

Sniffling, she snorts in disbelief, "you're just saying that because you got caught, Jinhwan."

"No," he shakes his head, "I'm fucking not, Soo, I'm saying it because I miss you."

An arrow to the heart.

She wanted to yell at him, she wanted to hit him, she wanted to try to hurt him the way he had hurt her. Soomi didn't care if she sort of deserved it, she didn't care at all. It felt like a million bombs were crashing down on her and her frail emotions, and there was nothing she could do about it but sit and watch. 

Her body was telling her to do something, telling her to run away, telling him she didn't want to talk - but she couldn't move.

Jinhwan took her silence as a sign. Not even looking back at the annoying girl behind them, he steps forward to open the door to the studio labeled SOOMI. Thankfully, it was an empty studio since she had yet to come to work and call her producers. 

Shutting the door after he pulls her inside, he locks it so Jimin didn't try to follow. Flicking the lights on, the room is illuminated to reveal her face, shimmering with tears. Soomi didn't try to talk, she only turned away so he didn't see her crying. 

"Soo," he whispers, "I'm so fucking sorry."

Silence, before she takes a deep breath, "didn't you say it was a misunderstanding? Why are you apologizing then?" Despite her crying, her tone was accusing him of something blatantly obvious to pinpoint.

And he needed her to understand it wasn't true.

"Because I can see how I've hurt you," he replies with a frown, "it is a misunderstanding, Soo, Jimin is my ex girlfriend. We broke up years ago because she's obsessive, when we fought I ran into her at a bar and she started bothering me again. She knows our address and she's in with some of the employees here at YG, they allow her to come up to this floor. I never did anything with her, Soo, I wouldn't cheat on you, okay?"

She didn't say anything. Solar didn't know what the hell to say. Her brain could barely process anything she was hearing, nonetheless begin to reply.

"Junhoe had it wrong, he saw the way she clings onto me and assumed the worst. I never let her do anything, I try to get her to leave me alone - it was never anything bad, Soo..." he went on, repeating himself what felt like a million times. He didn't know how to explain, it was like he forgot how to form sentences at the worst time possible. 

All the brunette was doing was peering at him from her stance a few feet away. Her face was a little puffy from the tears, green eyes glassy from the water in them. Her brown hair fell slightly in her face, but all she was doing was gawking up at him. She was thinking hard, hard about everything she was told. It was true that Junhoe never really had any proof, and the girl definitely did seem like the type to literally be a psycho. She just didn't get a few things, and she wasn't sure if she had the mental strength to ask him about them now. She wasn't sure what to believe even when this boy was before her begging for her understanding. After everything, Solar couldn't help but be cautious.

"I did tell her I had a girlfriend," he goes on, his voice a mutter, "but Jimin had always been like that, she disregards everything, lives in her own fantasies. I never planned to run into her, I even have her fucking number blocked."

She blinks at him in awe, this girl was seriously that crazy? 

"I'm just sorry, Soo," he finishes hesitantly, "I know this entire mess hurt you, I never wanted to be the reason you cried," he pauses again, seemingly thinking on something, before he breathes, "I love you, Soomi-ah."

She's quiet for a second. Did she believe him? It seemed to add up, meeting Jimin she could tell she had some issues. But she still needed to run over it a few times, talk to her friends so they could help her figure this out for sure.

Either way, she couldn't help but break from the way his sad brown eyes were gazing into her soul, "I believe you, Nani."

Those eyes of his light up like a storm, twinkling under the studio lights. "Really? I - I'm happy to hear that, I'm gonna figure this out, alright? I'll even look into a restraining order."

Noticing he was getting a little too ahead, she awkwardly clears her throat, turning away so she didn't have to see his face fall when she broke it to him.

"But - I still need some time away from you."



126:00 | not so glad to be back

"You're something else, Soomi!" her producer had been cheering, patting her on the back as she walked out of the recording booth, "We're glad to have you back, I have high hopes for this album."

She sends him a grateful smile, bowing to show her thanks. "I do, too, I worked hard on writing these songs, but you're who makes them come to life," she beams, and the producer only waves a hand in the air in dismissal.

"Don't be silly, you're the star here," he disagrees in a kind tone, gathering his things before heading towards the door, "I'll see you tomorrow, Soomi, take it easy, alright?"

"Of course, producer-nim," she grins, waving to him as he heads out the studio door.

Soomi turns back to her studio after the door closes, her bright expression she'd only wore a moment ago disappearing. The truth was, her heart had been hanging out of her chest this entire time. Ever since she saw Jinhwan, since she saw that girl, since he told her the "truth," she was stuck in a daze. Her producer was nice about it, but she knew she could of done better on the recordings today.

She wasn't herself, she hadn't been in a while. Too much happened in New York for her to be able to focus. It seemed it was only worsening now that she was back here.

Did she believe Jinhwan?

No idea.

Exhaling, the small girl falls onto the leather booth that outlines the room. There was no need for her to stay at the studio since her work was done for today, but she didn't feel like she could even move. 

sunu: how's korea? any suspicious things or moves at all from that piece of shit?

She stares down at the text message, blinking a few times. Like a bad habit, memories of her and Sunwoo flash through her mind. She squeezes her eyes shut vigorously to remove them so vividly from her vision, trying to think straight again. Jinhwan telling her this left a certain feeling of guilt in her bones. If it was the truth, the only one who cheated was her. And worst of all, it was with Kim Sunwoo.

"Fucking hell," she curses to herself. It was no joke she had no idea what she was going to do about any of this.

sunu: if you say so, sweetheart

sunu: keep me updated if any weird stuff happens

"Kim fucking Soomi!" a scream is heard from the doorway of her studio. It was like God had been listening to her thoughts due to the exact person she had been thinking of talking to showed up. She turns to hug Junhoe, when she's met with not just him, but five other boys, too. 

"Whoa, hold u-" she tries to say when they all jump at her full speed. The sound of a dying goat was what emerged from her lips when she was brutally tackled in affection by five boys much bigger than her.

Even though it felt like her lungs were giving out, she couldn't say she wasn't happy to see them. The boys who were suffocating her in a loving group hug finally realize what they are doing to the tiny girl, letting go and stepping back a little. 

That was, all of them that weren't Junhoe. The boy was glued to Soomi by the hip. He wasn't going to admit, but he had clearly missed his best friend.

"How are you?" Donghyuk is the first to ask in concern, "is your family alright now? Everything sorted out?"

She felt a little bad that she hadn't got to catch up with them all. The girl sighs as she recounts everything that happened to her in New York. It started off positive, that was for sure. She had fixed her family problems, reunited with them, strengthened a bond she never thought she would experience again. But this person that was after her had made moves, moves she didn't expect. Hell, she even got shot by Sunwoo's father, one of the richest men in the US, just because he hated her for being so close with his son. She found out about Jinhwan thanks to Junhoe, and it led to her doing drugs again after six years. 

And the part she didn't want to mention, her relationship that formed with Sunwoo. 

Just thinking about it all made her head hurt. She holds a hand to her head, massaging her temple as she steps back to take a seat on the booth that surrounds the walls of the studio.

The others exchange glances, realizing it must be a lot that she has to tell them. Without another word, they follow her actions. 

"Should I get popcorn?" Bobby hums into the silence.

He was trying to lighten the mood, but Soomi still wasn't looking forward to having to explain all of this. 

With a sigh, she had no choice but to begin. She'd tell them everything that wasn't about the new stalker of hers.



+



"That's a lot of good and bad in one," Hanbin mutters as soon as she's done. By this time, she was dead inside, leaning her head on Junhoe's shoulder with a dull look on her face.

She had left out more than she planned. By the time she got to parts like her hook up with Sunwoo or the fact she was doing drugs again, she realized it would be in everyone's best interest to not tell them. 

"Yeah," is all she replies, voice barely above a whisper.

The six boys frown as they see their poor friend. Soomi wasn't doing great, but yet here she was, at the studio having just recorded a new song. She had just got back from everything that they now learned happened to her in New York and was already working.

They felt bad for her.

And it wasn't like they didn't know about what happened in the hall or with Jinhwan completely. Chanwoo had briefly seen the exchange and went back to tell the others. No one knew if it was safe to ask her about their hyung. It was obviously a sore subject for the both of them right now.

"We'll always be here for you, Soo," Yunhyeong speaks up instead, laying a hand on her shoulder so she would look his way, "I know you're going through so much, but just remember you can lean on us, alright?"

The others nod or pipe up in agreement to his statement. She sends them a thankful smile, shaking her head in disbelief that she had them in her life, "thank you guys, really."


+



A deep sigh leaves her lips as she walks through the YG lobby that evening, wanting nothing more than to go home, or more like to see Jungkook. She was more than mentally exhausted after literally meeting the girl Jinhwan was suspected to have been cheating on her with. And she would never be too sure about the words he spoke. It made sense, it wasn't like she'd ever seen him as the type to cheat. He was a sweetheart, her saving grace, not a piece of shit who would do something like that behind her back.

But at the same time, it was too clear. Junhoe hadn't been so far fetched. She saw the way the girl clung onto him, the way she was dressed. He could say that he tried to get her to leave him alone, but would he not have been able to if he really wanted to? It seemed too weird to her.

All she wanted was to talk to Jungkook, and only Jungkook. Never did she think she would be here, but here she was. Maybe she really did make the wrong decision.

Sighing, she's just about to open the doors, desperate for the cold outside air to hit her skin, when a voice calls from behind her.

"Ms. Soomi-ah!" She turns to see one of the many staff workers, a polite smile on her face. Noticing she caught the girl's attention, the worker bows kindly, before clearing her throat, "CEO-nim wanted me to pass this onto you, he wants the entire family to be there to mourn a fellow YG member's passing."

A confused look is wiped over her face. Before she was able to process what this woman was talking about, she's handed a pamphlet or invitation. 

"Oh," she's muttering blankly as she takes it, her green eyes daring to glance down at what it was. In that moment, she seemed to have forgotten everything that happened, everything she went through in New York, all the trauma. For once, she had just been thinking about her childish boy problems. For once, she forgot, just for it to be thrown right back at her face.

And there it was, that woman's face plastered on the front page. There's a smile on her face in the picture, the words in memory of wrote in cursive above it. Her eyes are kind, but yet she remembered them being so evil when she'd held a gun to her very head. 

A funeral, she processes, they're having a funeral and they want me to attend. 

Swallowing the lump in her throat was like swallowing a brick. Forcing a smile on her face, she did anything but show how disturbed she was by this as she nodded, bowing gently, "I will surely be there, thank you."

Before the staff member could even bid goodbye, she turned and practically bolted out the entrance doors. As soon as cold air hit her face and she knew she wasn't in sight anymore, a deep exhale left her lips. She was in disbelief. How could she not have thought of this? 

She saw that woman die right in front of her. She'd never forget it, not after she spouted out every one of Soomi's secrets right before she did. 

Holding a hand to her head, she tries to sort her thoughts. She wanted you dead, Solar, she tells herself, it had to happen. But every time she would glance down at the pamphlet, she would feel bad all over again. She let her die, she let someone die right in front of her, she laughed as they were tortured.

What's wrong with me?

It was like her guilty conscience came back. All she could do was try to stop it, but it wasn't doing any good.

Drugs, that's what she needed. But it wasn't like she had any on her. How could she help herself? Was she really a bad person?

Soomi didn't realize she was crying until she felt droplets, thinking it was suddenly raining. It was helpless, her emotions were out of control, and all she had to thank for that was herself. She had been such an idiot when she thought nothing would come out of her doing drugs again. Look at what happened to her.

She couldn't act like everything was okay. She was a mess. A mess of confused, jumbled emotions that she wasn't sure she would ever be able to understand. The thing was, there was nothing she could do about this. She, and presumably the boys of iKON, would have to attend that funeral with their heads high like they knew nothing. She'd have to print that smile on her face and pay her respects, push anything else to the back of her head. 

Pretend like it never happened. 

But that was so hard when she saw her die right before her.

"Mi," a voice calls from somewhere in front of her, she was too busy trying to wipe away the tears to see where she was going, "Hey, Mi," it calls again. It was obvious who it was, and the sound of shoes scraping against concrete was heard. That's when she slammed right into someone's chest, Jungkook's chest, sniffling like a child. 

"Hey," he says again in a whisper, trying to comfort her, "what the hell happened?"

All she could do was exhale deeply, recollecting herself, and hand him the pamphlet. The boy takes it, eyes scanning over everything on it, and as soon as he puts two and two together, his face falls.

"Shit," he mutters in disbelief, realizing just like she had that they probably should have expected this. "Come on," he urges, his hand on her back, "we need to go."

Only then did it really sink into her troubled skin that they were indeed in the parking lot of YG. Though they were at the back where no one ever really was, it was still fucking YG Entertainment. Her breath hitches in her throat when she comprehends, looking to Jungkook, and immediately she bolts to his car.

Not her smartest move, she sighed as she was safely in the seat, slamming the door in hopes that all of this would somehow be okay, but then again, none of this was.


127:00 | sticking together

"Just breathe," Jungkook had been telling her, his doe eyes worriedly falling on her nervous frame every few seconds as he drove. One hand was gripping the steering wheel while the other was holding onto her own hand as if he was afraid she was going to fly out the window. The truth was, Jungkook was having a hard time processing this, too.

"So I guess they didn't forget about her so easily," he mumbles into the stiff air, only saying things she already knew, "did the pamphlet say anything about what happened to her or the cops being involved?"

Sighing, Soomi picks up the folded paper again. Her eyes scan over each page hesitantly, afraid of what she'll find. 

"Uh," she stutters, turning to the middle. There were a whole lot of sweet words about the woman, which she quickly threw away in her head. Nothing seemed to really spike what they were looking for, until she looked at a paragraph at the bottom of the page.

At KCON NY, she was believed to be kidnapped and eventually murdered in cold blood. For the safety of everyone and to ease the hearts of the mourning family, the police and FBI are looking into her kidnapping. Any updates/news found will be reported under YG ENT. 

"Fucking hell," she curses, almost breaking down into cries all over again. This time, she decided to be the bigger person she needed to be. Shaking her head, she informs the boy in the driver's seat, "YG has the cops and the FBI trying to figure it out, they presume it was a kidnapping, and they know it happened in New York."

His eyes widened slightly, "you've gotta be kidding."

She could only pout, "I wish."

Trying to think quickly on what to do, he clears his throat, "shouldn't you call Sunwoo? Tell him about this, they would be the prime people that all of it would lead them to, no?"

She thought for a minute, before nodding, "probably, it would lead them to Sunwoo and me."

"Which would be very bad."

"Right."

Without a doubt, she had to tell Sunwoo, he would know what to do way more than anyone else. Soomi tries not to freak out, containing her emotions the best she could, and dials his number. Putting it to her ear, she listens to the sound of the ringing.

Though it only rang twice before his silky voice burst through her ears, "sweetheart, to what do I owe the pleasure?"

Typical Sunwoo, he never really got old.

"Sunu," she breathes, "I have bad news."

There's a bit of shuffling on the other line. He was probably moving to a quieter place to speak, as she could hear the screams of the boys. After a moment, he speaks, in a much more serious tone compared to the one he was just using when he answered, "Alright, nothing your favorite crime boss can't take care of, what is it?"

"I got a pamphlet from one of the staff today," she begins, looking down at the paper she held in her lap, "they're having a funeral for the woman that was stalking me. My CEO wants me to attend," she pauses for a second, breathing deeply, "and they've got the FBI and the police on the case, they know it happened in New York."

There's a long silence. 

Soomi had to make sure the call didn't end or she didn't have service. Neither of the two were the case, he really just wasn't saying anything. 

Jungkook glances over to her in the passenger seat, confusion written all over his face. "What's happening?" he mouths, but she only shrugs. 

When she's about to call his name, that's when the sound of a light chuckle is heard. It's faint at first, until it became prominent enough to tell that it was no other than Sunwoo. Soomi is bewildered, not understanding why he would laugh now of all times.

"That's all, sweetheart?" he sighs, and the sound of his exhaling air was heard loudly, presumably him smoking, "I thought it was actually serious, like another word from the new stalker, not about the one that's dead."

"Huh?" she blinks, "that's the thing, though, what do you mean that's all?! Aren't you worried they're gonna find you?!"

The boy giggles on the other line, except it was more menacing than it was cute. "Sol, how many times do you think the cops and the FBI have tried to find the most dangerous gang in the States, seriously?" he hums, humor tinted in his tone, "Now, don't think too hard on it, it might wear your brain out."

Her mouth is agape as she processes. 

He had a point.

"Shut up!" she cried in annoyance as soon as he started laughing, "I get that they might not be able to track you, but what if they figure out it's me?!"

His laughter dies down after a second. The sound of him taking another whiff of his cigarette's heard, before he exhales, "unlikely, Solar, don't think I was dumb enough to leave a trace of me or you. It's possible they could figure out that you were both MIA from the event at the same time, but they still wouldn't be able to trace you. I wiped out any camera that was on the outside of that venue, you're fine."

She's silent for a while. Sunwoo really was smart.

"If they do decide to call you in for questioning because you were MIA at the same time, you can just play it off, you've done this before, remember? Don't act like this is new to you," he continues, "you and your little friends can stick up for each other. They won't be able to figure anything out more than the fact she's dead and not coming back. The police and the FBI are as dumb as the normal townsfolk, they just get a fancy name to scare you."

"You're right," she agrees after a moment.

"Just keep your head up, sweetheart. Go to that funeral and make it look like you're really paying your respects, pass the message on to your little friends, too. It might be harder for them being that they were closer to her."

"Okay, Sunwoo."

"And if you need me, just call, okay?"

"Yeah, always."



+
