128:00 | his terms

The funeral was this Saturday. The current day was Thursday. Soomi had talked about it with the boys of iKON, and they all agreed upon the same thing. All they could do was believe Sunwoo when he said it wasn't possible for them to get caught. They had to put up their best acting and pay their respects, be careful to raise any suspicion. The funeral would pass and so would the memory of the boys' manager. It would all be okay soon.

This second stalker that she had been working with had yet to make any move since she returned to Korea. You would think they would be eager to since she was away from the protection of Sunwoo. But she hadn't heard a word. Her days were spent stalker-free with Jungkook and the other Bangtan boys. It was seemingly peaceful if you removed the thought of the funeral from the equation.

That talk she had with Junhoe repeated in her brain. She was busy staring down at her phone screen, the light being the only thing illuminating her shocked expression as she was laying in Jungkook's king sized bed. The boys had gone to Big Hit today for work, leaving her here at this massive mansion to do whatever she pleased. It really wasn't so bad, as the security in this place was pretty scary and it was a hard location to get to. 

She would be fine by herself, right? Plus, it was nice to finally get to be alone. 

Soomi had just been trying to enjoy her day thus far, but she had been getting tagged a scary amount of times in these articles and posts. Eventually, she got tired of the constant notifications and just clicked on one of them. She had a bad feeling about it, last time she looked at an article of herself was when her entire career was almost destroyed.

That was until she saw the headline.

BTS Jungkook's New Song Raises Questions On His Relationship Status
Could it be about his previous girlfriend, singer-rapper Kim Soomi?

She giggled as she looked at the article. These were pretty hopeless since no one had proof of it being about her. It still left a weird feeling inside of her, though, as she thought back to that day at KCON. 

It was such a pretty song, all for her, all about how he would always be there to protect her even if she didn't want him in the end. If she really sat and thought about it, Jungkook had never been the bad one in the equation, not anymore. Not after she found out the scandal wasn't his fault, that he loved her the entire time, not after he had done so much to help her since that very day in her dressing room. 

She knew that there was a reason he wouldn't leave her head at the weirdest times. There was a reason that she kept thinking about him back in New York when she was alone in her room. There was a reason.

Just do it, Soo, Jinhwan will have to understand that what he did has consequences. There's nothing wrong with testing the waters with someone else, look at him, after all, Junhoe's text repeats in her head like a broken record.

Yet she knew she was acting on a limb. The thing was, she was never so sure of who it was for her. Just when she believed it was Jinhwan, her heart would call to Jungkook. The answer was never clear. Who did she really need? 

She already tried with Jinhwan, she saw his presumable true colors. He could say he was sorry, but at the end of the day he was still promoting that girl's behavior. There wasn't an excuse for that.

Maybe this is what she had to do. Sure, it was stupid, sure, it was completely on a limb, and sure, she was walking on egg shells here. 

But she knew there was something inside of her that kept calling for this boy. She was tired of ignoring it.

After all, there was a reason she almost married him.

Her eyes peer down at the articles, skimming over the comments on them. Most of them were about the same thing, crying about how they wanted to know if his song was about her or not, asking if they were still together, still talking, or going through a rough patch. Every comment seemed to have to do with her, even though the song didn't directly mention her, they just seemed to know.

Thinking about that song he wrote seemed to hammer it into her skull. 

Nothing wrong with second chances, right?




+


"Wait, what, you're going out where?" 

"I'm going to see Soomi, idiot, I know she's with him."

"What's that got to do with you? Just leave her alone," Junhoe groans, holding a hand to his head. Thinking back to the conversation he had with her through text, he knew he couldn't let him go. Jinhwan showing up at their place would ruin the moment she was having. He meant the advice he gave her, it really was about time she gave Jungkook a chance. The poor boy had been trying for so long, and Jinhwan needed to learn a lesson after whatever he thinks he's pulling. "She doesn't want to see you, she told you that herself, pushing her is going to make it that much worse for you."

The caramel haired boy pauses, having had just about to of opened the front door to their apartment. His eyes trail to the tall boy across the room, and his face seems to fall when he realizes that he was right. For once in his life, Junhoe actually made sense. 

"It irks me knowing she's been with him, though," he mutters, letting his hand fall from the door knob.

"Who's fucking fault is that?" he retorts instantly, crossing his arms over his chest and shaking his head in disappointment, "you should've thought of that before you fucking pulled this. You spend all this time with Jimin, don't get mad because she wants to be with Jungkook. You aren't together anymore."

His words were a cut in the heart. Jinhwan winced at the sound of them coming out of his mouth. It was a lot to take in, but he knew he was right.

Still, he hated looking like the bad guy. There was a reason all of it happened.

"Don't act like it's all on me," he snaps, finding his courage, "I know my actions aren't justified, but there's still a reason behind them."

The tall boy looks intrigued. But just as he opened his mouth to probably ask about what the hell Jinhwan was saying, the short boy spins on his heel, and bolts out the door. 

Just when he thought he changed his mind.


+


Soomi's heart was on her sleeve. She was wearing it loud and bright, practically waving it around in the open. She'd been glancing out the window of Jungkook's bedroom for the last hour since she told herself she would do it. As soon as she saw the cars pull up, she practically sprinted out of the room and down the steps like a psychopath.

The boys were walking in the house already, but she bolted right past them. Some of them peered at her in confusion and others tried to greet her before seeing she was clearly distracted. It was true, her thoughts were set on only one thing and one person alone.

Jungkook had been intently reading something on his phone. He was so focused on whatever it was that he was still standing on the sidewalk, many feet away from the porch. Everyone else was already inside, and she closes the door behind them all. 

The sound of rushed footsteps approaching him caused the long haired brunette boy to glance up, doe eyes full of wonder until they land on the petite girl running at him full speed. Jungkook blinks a few times, trying to figure out what was up with her and her demeanor. 

"Mi-" he starts to say, about to make one of his many cocky comments about how she must've missed him to come running outside in her PJs like this. 

But she cut him off by breathlessly crying out his name as if he wasn't right in front of her, "Jungkook!"

He blinks in confusion, looking down at her as she was right before him now, "are you feeling okay, Mi?"

"Yeah, but I have to talk to you," she insists eagerly. 

He stared at her for another moment, before looking back down to his phone and what he had been so invested in, "wait, I have to tell you, did you see the articles about my song? Everyone's talking about it."

Soomi is quiet for a second, thinking back to the article and topic that had made headlines everywhere you looked. Indeed, you couldn't miss it. It was like the entire country knew of his song and was speculating it was about none other than Soomi. 

"Yeah!" she nods cheerily, "everyone loves it, it's a great song, Kookie."

The boy grins from ear to ear, those bunny teeth making an appearance. Jungkook could sense something was going on with the girl before him. She was never this bright, especially after all the drama that happened. Soomi usually had the darkest cloud above her head, but now it was like she was the shining star in her own sky. 

"Well, of course it's great," he shrugs a moment later, "it was about you. The media sure seems to know that, too."

"It seems there's more support for us now, though," she responds, thinking back to what happened at the award shows, "because of that, our company's would probably use us for positive press."

Jungkook is blinking at her in obvious confusion. Now he knew there was really something going on. Kim Soomi was the last person to ever talk about that. 

"Hm, that's true," he hesitantly nods, eyes narrowed at her, "I'm glad that the song is received so well, though, and I hope you can understand the meaning behind it."

Pursing her lips, her heart seemed to be racing, "well, you did tell me."

"It'll always be you, Mi," he blurts before he could stop himself, too caught up in the tension in the atmosphere, "remember that."

Solar is quiet for a long time. Her thoughts were jumbled, heart beating out of her chest, and she couldn't seem to maintain eye contact with the glorious boy anymore. Everything was out of the ordinary to her, even though she had lived through this before. It was Jungkook, the boy she was engaged to, the boy that helped her when she had nobody in Korea, the boy that gave her wings when she wanted to fly.

There was something she would always have for him. A flame that she would ignite then burn out, she did it over and over until the decided it was Jinhwan. But could it be the entire time it was him?

There was only one way to find out, and she prayed it wouldn't end like the last time.

"Jungkook, I think," she pauses, biting down on her bottom lip as she tried to make up her mind, "I think back at KCON I might've made the wrong decision."

His eyes widen, nearly falling out of their sockets. It was clear this shocked him at first, before he cooled down back to his cocky, mellow state. "I could've told you that, Mi, look at what that asshole did to you."

"Look, I'm not for sure what I'm doing, okay?" she breathes, "but I think - I think whatever it is, I want to do it with you."

Jungkook's eyes dart from side to side, full of shock. Sure, he expected Soomi to tell him stuff like she made a bad decision when she chose Jinhwan, because she did. But he didn't expect her to straight up want to be with him again. It was not like her at all.

It takes him a minute to process. Once he does, he burst into giggles, high pitched giggles sounding much as if he was a little kid. Soomi, who had been worried about saying what she said, seems to drip of all worry when she witnesses his reaction. It seemed to sink into her brain that this was Jungkook she was talking to, not a complete stranger. She could've approached it a lot easier than that.

"Ugh, stop laughing at me!" she whines, hitting his shoulder with all of her might. It didn't affect someone like Jungkook in the slightest, not with how much he worked out these days. "I was nervous, okay? You don't have to be mean about it."

"No, Mi, you're just precious," he insists with that stupid smile, "I didn't expect that from you. What about Jinhwan?"

Her excited expression seems to disintegrate right before him. It made Jungkook feel bad, but he knew it had to be talked about. "I don't know," he mutters a moment later, "but I feel like if I'm ever going to figure out how I really feel about the both of you, that includes giving you a chance, too."

The boy before her is more than giddy. He felt like everything in his life was coming into place, but it wasn't exactly how he planned. His dark, long hair falls in his face when he looks down to grab her hands in his own. Solar purses her lips as she lets him do so, wondering what words he was going to say next. 

A twinkle is evident in those doe eyes of his as he meets her own. "You're right, Mi," he finally clears his throat, "but I want us getting back together officially to be more special than this."

Almost instantly, a groan leaves her lips. 

"Seriously?" she whines.

Giggling at her upset reaction, he pinches her cheek with two fingers. The girl only glares at him in clear disappointment, "sorry, Mi, it's gotta be on my terms. You're just gonna have to wait."

"Ugh, you'd think you would be excited."

"Oh, Mi, as if I've ever actually thought you slipped out of my hands."

"You suck."

Rolling his eyes, Jungkook only pulls her forward, causing the girl to fall face first into his chest. She was trying to be annoyed with him, but he had no intention of allowing her to do so. "Yah, get a grip!" she's complaining as he's busy squeezing the life out of her.

Eventually, she gave up, just accepting that she wasn't getting out of this one. Solar would have to face the fact that Jungkook always wanted to do things his way. With that, she shrugs it off, wrapping her frail arms around his torso. 

"I still hate you," she mumbles.

"I'm sure you do."

And there the two stood, in the yard of the Bangtan mansion. Located in the middle of nowhere, they felt free. No one would ever find them here, no one knew where it was. They were safe, free to be whatever they wanted to be. 

Except, not quite. Because someone did know where it was located.

Jinhwan parks the car a bit down the road, hoping that they wouldn't be able to see him from such a distance. Turning the vehicle off, his eyes wander to the front yard of the estate. The caramel boy's face falls when he sees the scene, biting his lip to attempt to suppress any emotion from leaving him.

It sucked, it sucked losing the only person you ever seemed to click with, the only person you ever felt genuine love for. So why did he ruin it? Why did he allow the one who meant the most to him to slip out of his fingers?

All because of a stupid incident that she didn't even remember, that's why. He sought to seek revenge for something that wasn't technically her fault. Sure, he would be mad about it, but doing what he did was never the right way to approach such a situation. He knew that now.

But as he watched the girl he loved embrace the likes of Jeon Jungkook, he had a feeling it was too late.

Maybe that was really it for them.


129:00 | shocking veracity

"Look who's home," Donghyuk offers in a cheery tone when the eldest enters the apartment. He didn't offer anything in return to his friend's words. Jinhwan only slams the front door, kicking his shoes off, and begins to walk bitterly towards his bedroom.

The boys, who had all been lounging in the living area, exchange glances with one another. Bobby mouths to everyone, "think he's a bit of a sour puss."

"No shit," Chanwoo mouths back to him.

No one really knew what they could do for him. The last time Soomi and Jinhwan had a fight it was all the way back when he caught her kissing Jungkook. But that fight was nothing compared to this. This time, Jinhwan was completely in the wrong. They really couldn't blame Soo for going back to Jungkook after he basically cheated on her endlessly. 

They knew he just went and probably saw them together. What could they really tell him other than he should've known better?

"What do we do?" Yunhyeong whispers.

"Sometimes you have to let him figure it out on his own," Hanbin advises.

But they should've known a certain idiot would have trouble with that. Before anyone could stop him, Junhoe rises from his feet, walking off to follow the eldest to his bedroom.

"And don't do what that idiot is probably about to," the leader finishes, shaking his head in disappointment.

Jinhwan had already closed the door, but Junhoe opens it without knocking. This causes the short boy, who was in the middle of changing, to turn around with annoyance written all over his face. Once he sees who it was, he exhales deeply, turning away to finish putting a shirt on.

"Yah, hyung," Junhoe begins as he closes the door behind him, "so what happened? You saw them together, right? I told you."

Rolling his eyes, the caramel boy finishes changing. Grabbing his phone, he doesn't say anything as he walks to fall onto his large king sized bed. Sinking into the wine colored sheets, only then does he groan, "it's really not the time for the I told you so's, June-ya."

The tall boy purses his lips, thinking carefully of his next words. It was obvious Jinhwan didn't feel like talking about it, though it didn't matter since he had a good feeling of what happened anyway. 

However, there was one thing that he had been thinking about. 

"But what exactly made you start all this in the first place?"

Poor Junhoe was the only one in the household who didn't know. Jinhwan had been holding off on telling him, but it was taking all of his nerve. Especially when Junhoe was constantly here bothering him about Solar, making him out to be the shittiest person. He guessed all of his hints had finally nailed something into the tall boy's brain that there was a motive behind his actions. 

Now that Junhoe realized such, though, it meant he was going to want to know what it was. Jinhwan wasn't sure if it was the best time to tell him. He wasn't sure what it would do to the friend group, and he wasn't sure if he wanted Solar to know because no doubt it would lead right back to her. 

But he had to. He had to know. 

Junhoe knew there was something up, and he knew it had to do with him. Back when Jinhwan and him had their first argument about this entire thing, Jinhwan had said words that Junhoe seemed to push to the back of his brain like nothing. But after today, after Jinhwan said there was a reason, he was beginning to think that there was something he was missing in this equation.

"You don't want to get into it," Jinhwan finally warns him. 

"What happened with me and Soomi that I don't know about?" the tall boy blurts, not holding anything back anymore. He had a lot of time to think about this, and he couldn't take it anymore. Nothing was adding up in his head. "How the hell would I not have known something happened? This doesn't make any sense, is that what your motive was?"

A deep sigh leaves the caramel boy's lips. He sits up from his bed, setting his phone aside that he had been scrolling through. Flinging his legs over the side, Jinhwan rises to his feet, running a hand through his hair as he walks to distract himself. It was a habit he had when he was worried or nervous, he would start pacing. 

"Do you remember the celebratory night we had?" he clears his throat, back to Junhoe. He was afraid of what his reaction would be to this news. "That night, we were all drunk except for Chanwoo."

"Okay, well no shit I don't remember," he shrugs, not understanding in the slightest, "what the hell does that have to do with anything?"

Tired of his sassy attitude that he never dropped, Jinhwan spins on his heel to face him. "Because, idiot, that night you hooked up with my fucking girlfriend."

The bomb had been dropped. It flew through the air at mach speeds, slamming into Junhoe's brain and exploding the pieces of everything he thought he knew everywhere. It was the most confused and bewildered he had ever been in his life.

So much so, he couldn't begin to believe a word of what his hyung just told him. He had to be fucking crazy.

"What?" he blinks, shaking his head after a second of thought, "hyung, are you feeling okay? You might have a fever."

Jinhwan scoffs in disbelief, crossing his arms over his chest, "Chanwoo confessed it to the other members, I walked in to hear it."

Junhoe is just standing there gawking at him. He was waiting for Jinhwan to start laughing, to say it was a joke, an early April fools. When he doesn't, the boy could only awkwardly laugh, unable to process it in his head, "yeah, I really don't think I can believe that I drunkenly hooked up with my best friend, which lead you to start cheating on her. What kind of story is that?"

"Look, June-ya, I'm not trying to justify hanging around Jimin," he sighs, "but I never cheated on Soomi, not in the beginning. You know how Jimin is, she's crazy, she forced me to do things I didn't want to, but that was after me and Soomi broke up," he pauses to run a hand through her hair, "but I love Soomi, I just went out to a bar and unintentionally ran into Jimin out of anger. I knew I couldn't be mad at her, I couldn't be mad at you, because you guys were drunk. In a way, I was just mad at myself."

"Yah," Junhoe mutters under his breathe, "I don't - I don't know how to fucking process this."

"I figured you'd have a hard time taking it," he breathes, "but just lay back on calling me a cheater and bothering me about Soomi, yeah? I know the way I acted wasn't the best, but I do know I'm not completely accountable."

"And I'm going to get her back," he finishes matter-of-factly, "one way or a fucking nother."


+



The day of the funeral had finally rolled around. Well, wasn't like it was ever far in the first place. All the workers of YG were required to attend to "pay respects." This would be a simple task for anyone - anyone who didn't know what happened to the woman who was deemed dead. 

"We know you're worried, Mi," Jimin had been telling her reassuringly, putting a hand on her shoulder from his spot in the back seat, "it'll be okay, just keep a smile on your face and don't say anything out of the ordinary. You have to trust Sunwoo, remember?"

With a shaky deep breath, the brunette girl nods in understanding to her best friend. She only  stares out the window from where she sat in Jungkook's car, watching the scenery as they passed. Soomi didn't have any words, she didn't know what she could say anymore. All she could think about was how she was going to hear about the woman, the woman who tried to kill her, the woman who tried to ruin her career. She literally held a gun to her head. Now she had to go sit and smile, act like she was an amazing person.

As much as she didn't want to, it wasn't like she could just not show. Her CEO would likely not like that, and it could lead to the police being more suspicious. They were already investigating her case, God knew what they would find.

But Jimin was right, trust Sunwoo. A gang like his never got caught in the act.

"You'll be alright, you can call me to come get you at any time," Jungkook tells her soothingly, placing a hand on her thigh. He rubs his thumb in circles on her skin, a motion that eased her worries in the weirdest way.

"Thanks guys," she sighs, "I can do this."

"Good, because we happen to be at the venue," Jungkook then announces. Soomi dares to turn and look out the window once again, only this time to see they were indeed located at the graveyard where she would be buried. 

The parking lot was full of other vans and cars. Jungkook made sure to park in a place that wouldn't be noticeable. The last thing they needed was for the press to get a hold of them all together.

"Alright," she clears her throat, grabbing her purse and throwing it over her shoulder, "I guess I should go."

"Don't be afraid to text us, Mi!" Jimin calls as she's climbing out of the car.

"I will!" she returns, waving to them as she shuts the door.

Jungkook watches the girl walk away, hands resting on the steering wheel. He sighs as she eventually disappears somewhere behind the cars and vans parked in the lot. He wished he could go with her, comfort her, keep her away from Jinhwan. But it was the last thing he could do for the safety of them both.

"We should go, Kook," Jimin reminds him, jumping into the front passenger seat where Soomi had just sat, "we can go drive around the area, but we can't afford to be spotted here."

"Yeah," he nods, starting the car again and backing out of the spot. In a few seconds, he was back on the main road, driving at a slow pace to pass time. Jungkook was still thinking about Mi, wondering if she was doing okay so far even though it had only been literally like three minutes. However, there was something else particularly on his mind. 

"Chim," he breaks the silence that had been in the vehicle, other than the sound of the radio, "I've been thinking about something."

The mochi boy raises a brow as he looks to the youngest, "what might that be? Something about Mi's birthday?"

He chuckles in surprise, "how'd you know?"

"Well, it's getting close, if it wasn't what you were thinking about that'd be sort of messed up."

"Yeah, well, I want to throw her a party," he explains, "at a nice venue, you know? Invite all her close friends, people we can trust, and I'll ask her to be my girlfriend exclusively when it's just our friend group."

Jimin nods as he takes in the idea. "That sounds great, Jungkook," he hums, "Mi would love that, but would you invite her other friends?"

"Sure, even that Jinhwan fuck just to spite him," he snorts.

The boy rolls his eyes, "figured as much. Minus that part, that would be a great idea, I can help you plan it."

And that he would. Soomi would have the best birthday this year, Jungkook would make sure of it. She needed some time to let go and forget about everything that kept bringing her down, after all.

Then she would be his again, everything would finally fall back into place. Just how it's supposed to be.

Meanwhile, Soomi had walked up to the group of people that were attending the funeral. She recognized the majority as workers at the company, and they greeted her, hugging her and asking how she was doing. She put the biggest smile she could on her face, making sure she said hello to everyone. 

Her eyes rake over all the people, trying not to let anything get to her head. It was when her green eyes land on a group, located towards the front where a closed casket was (empty since her body was never found for good reason.) They were huddled up, clothes in hand as they dabbed their eyes. She noticed then it must be the family, there was even a few children there, tears in their eyes as they look to the big picture of the woman being displayed. 

That's when it hit her.

"Oh my god," she whispers to herself, feeling her heart sink in her chest. What kind of monster was she? How could she let that happen to someone? She never stopped to think about how it would affect the loved ones. 

Before she knew it, her remorse was getting the best of her all over again. She couldn't think rationally anymore.

That's when someone grabs her wrist harshly. Their hand grips it like it was the most important thing they would ever do. Due to the negative thoughts clouding her brain, the girl squeaks in fear as she was being dragged away.

"I didn't do-," she starts to cry, when her mouth is covered by a hand. Thankfully, she had been dragged behind some trees to a save spot where no one would've heard her nearly say something like that.

Only then did she look up.

"Jinhwan-ah..." she mutters. As soon as she meets his eyes, she bursts into uncontrollable tears. All of the guilt she felt was catching up to her, she felt like the worst person to ever roam the Earth after seeing the family. She couldn't contain it anymore.

"Soo," he breathes as if he'd been suffocating, frowning at the sight of her breaking before him. He didn't have to ask, he knew it was a lot for her. Seeing him probably wasn't helping her case, but he couldn't help it. He knew she was going to have trouble at this funeral, he couldn't leave her all alone. 

With that, he pulls her into a tight hug, the tightest embrace he could manage. The girl sniffles into his chest, burying her face in his suit jacket. 

The girl was so shaken up she didn't realize what was even happening. Only after a while of him caressing her back and hugging her with all his might did she come back down to Earth. That's when she realized she was embracing Jinhwan. 

But for some reason, she didn't feel bothered anymore. She knew what he did was wrong, she knew she still had suspicion, she knew she couldn't forgive him already. 

However, at the end of the day, it was still Kim Jinhwan. It was still the boy she fell for at first sight, the boy she chose for a reason. 

Even if she was about to get back with Jungkook.

She wouldn't be Kim Soomi without the best of both worlds.

"Nani," she breathes, finally pulling away to get a good look at him. His caramel hair was slicked over to one side, fluffier than ever. Those brown eyes twinkle under the sunlight as they peer down at her, earrings dangling from the slight breeze. And he was dressed formally for the occasion in a suit, one that seemed to make her double take.



PEEP THE LEWK!



"I didn't mean to pull you away so suddenly," he explains, scratching his neck as he diverts his eyes for a second, "I could tell you were breaking though. It wouldn't have been good to do in front of all those people."

Pursing her lips, she stares at him for a second. It was hard to resist the likes of Kim Jinhwan, it always would be. 

"It's okay," she shakes her head, "I should thank you for it."

"I know this is scary," he tells her, "but you can't let yourself break, okay? You have to be strong for you, for all of us."

His works sunk into her skull, and she couldn't help but smile as she looks to him. "I can stick by your side if you like, there's no use trying to go through this alone, Soo."

"Yeah," she nods, "that would be good."

It was clear there was tension in the air. They both felt too awkward to say much. 

Until Jinhwan clears his throat, "have you thought about what I said?"

"Um," she stutters, "sure, yeah." Solar was clearly having a bit of an issue with the way his eyes were staring at her. It wasn't like Jinhwan was intimidating, but for some reason she felt like she was melting under his gaze.

She wasn't nervous or anything. It was the fact that this was her now ex, the boy she had been so in love with, the boy she spent every waking minute with for over a year. He was her entire world for what maybe wasn't that long - but felt like a decade. She was used to him whether she wanted to be or not. She was comfortable with Jinhwan in a way she couldn't explain. Soomi was having a problem because she didn't want herself to be so instantly comfortable with him. He didn't deserve it.

"Listen, Soo," he hesitates, "there's something else you need to know about all of this."

The girl's curiosity is spiked from his words. Blankly, she meets his eyes once again, thinking a second before she cautiously asks, "what might that be?"

Jinhwan is in a deep debate with himself. He knew that if she was ever going to find out, it needed to be from him, she needed to understand his perspective. 

"Soo, there's a reason I started going out to bars, there's a reason I unintentionally started hanging out with Jimin," he begins, trying to keep his composure as he tries to tell her the truth, "when we celebrated that night and you drank for the first time, you did something that you didn't know."

"Huh?" she's more than confused, but nonetheless vacillates, "well, okay, what did I do?"

Surprised she was so open, he pauses for a second, before faltering, "you hooked up with Junhoe. I found out through Chanwoo, he's the only one who saw it happen because he was still sober. I became really angry because it was my girlfriend and my best friend, but I knew I couldn't be mad at you or Junhoe because you guys were drunk. I was just in a state of confusion that turned into anger and it got the best of me. So I started going to bars and ran into Jimin, then we went on a break, and I just let her bother me because I felt like I lost everything-"

"Yah, Kim Jinhwan," she cuts him off by placing a hand over his mouth, "you're rambling, you ramble when you're nervous," she recites, a giggle leaving her lips. It was a giggle she only ever had when she was with him. He seemed to bring out a different side of her, a giddier side she would never acknowledge.

Her hand falls back to her side, and the boy is free to speak again, "Soomi, Solar, I fucking love you," he states confidently, "I know you've been with Jungkook and it kills me even though I can't really blame you."

"Well, he's kind of a safe place for me," she shrugs, rubbing her forearm awkwardly. The mention of the particular boy she was tempted to get back together with did make it sort of weird.

"I know," he nods, "I know you need time, I know you might want to try it out with Jungkook or whatever, but remember I'm right here, and I hope now that you know the truth you can understand my side a little better."

"Yeah," she agrees, "I can."

Jinhwan seems to be staring into her soul, observing her, "you're taking the fact you hooked up with your best friend oddly well."

Solar only brushes it off, not seeming to care too much, "I have a tendency to do stupid things when drunk that I never remember," she informs, "it was kinda my thing."

"Right," he chuckles, "I forget where you came from sometimes. Speaking of, how are you feeling about this whole funeral now?"

The girl exhales, looking back to the crowd of people that were still in the graveyard. It looked like the service would officially be starting soon. 

"Not so great, but I don't have a choice but to get this over with," she murmured.

"I'll be here with you through it, okay?" he hums from behind her. She could feel his presence directly behind her, that familiar scent of his cologne sinking through her nostrils.

She knew she should say no. Soomi still had to think about everything he said. 

But she couldn't.

"Okay, Jinhwan-ah."



130:00 | the funeral

"We're here today to celebrate a beautiful soul, a strong woman, a woman that represented power, courage, and individuality," the priest had been saying, a book in hand, standing at the front of the many people that were seated across the grass of the graveyard area, "Kang Jisoo (I DONT REMEMBER HER NAME) was an empowering, independent woman who loved those around her. She was an outstanding, long time staff member of YG Entertainment."

Solar winces with each word that leaves the man's mouth. Each one was as if she was being shot, stabbed with tacks, all in her heart. She could feel herself breaking with each sentence he spoke. It all made her feel so bad, but she couldn't help but feel conflicted. Was she not a horrible person? She tried to kill her, she held a gun to her head. How did she love those around her?

Just when she grew closer to convincing herself that what happened was for the best, her eyes would land on the children, the family, seated at the front. The kids would hug the adults beside them, burying their faces in their jackets. The adults patted their faces with a tissue, shaking their heads every once in a while in disbelief.

There was so much in her head, too much for her to think straight. So much so that seeing the family members, seeing people so hurt by something she felt like she caused, made her feel like garbage nonetheless.

"She was a loyal member of the YG family, and a wonderful mother to her beloved children," the priest goes on, "a scar has clearly been left on the hearts of those who were lucky enough to know a woman like her."

Without meaning to, Solar chokes on her tears. She didn't realize she was going to cry again until she felt it in her throat. Jinhwan, who was sitting beside her, looks to her in worry. Due to the fact he was also an evident thing on her mind, she couldn't bring herself to meet his eyes. 

The boy was longing to touch her, to embrace her again, comfort her in a clear time of need. He knew he couldn't do anything like that, though, not around the entirety of their company staff. All he could do was whisper to her, "are you okay? Do you want to step out?"

Unable to say a word, she could only shake her head. Solar had to fight this, she had to get over it and push through. This should be a walk in the park for her, she'd seen people die before, right? Just like Sunwoo said.

You're not new to this, Solar.

He was right, she wasn't. 

With a deep breath, she tries to focus on the service. She had to remind herself that this was like a mission to her and the boys. Despite what was going on - like the fact she hooked up with her best friend and she was suddenly talking to Jinhwan - they had to all stick together and be a team. The events that happened would have to wait, this was their safety on the line. 

As if on queue to throw that thought in her face, she hears footsteps from the area behind them where the parking lot was. She tells herself not to turn around, but her curiosity got the best of her.

Glancing back quickly, her breath nearly hitches in her throat when she catches herself.

Police investigators.

Cursing to herself, she faces the service again. She knew it was rude to take out your phone during a funeral, and it would likely raise suspicion. She found herself wondering if the police had come to realize that whoever was involved with her murder were a coworker or under the company at least. 

If they'd gotten that far, she was worried about what else they would find out. How clean could it be? Was Sunwoo really that good that he could cover every single track in something like this? She couldn't help but get a bad feeling.

Though it was likely her being paranoid. Solar had to trust Sunwoo, he was a pro at this. It would be okay.

He did say that there was a chance they might get questioned. If they did, they would just have to play it off. She knew this wouldn't be too hard for her, she just had to remind herself of the goal. The boys, though, was a different story.

Just breathe, it'll be over soon enough.


+


After a scary amount of acting and sending her condolences, Solar was free. Her eyes found Jinhwan and she practically bolted to him. The service was over by now, and people were either leaving or busy chatting. This gave her the time to talk to him and find where the others were. As much as she wasn't trying to have a get together today, she didn't have much of a choice.

She had to push everything about Jinhwan and Junhoe to the back of her head, pretending like it wasn't there.

"Soo," Jinhwan greets her, eyes glancing around every which way behind her as if he was looking for something, before he lowers his voice, "did you see the police, too?"

"Sure did," she breathes, "we have to find the others and tell them. We have to stick together if we get questioned or this will get messy real fast."

"Yeah, okay," he nods, thinking quickly, "you're right, I'll text them to meet in the car."

"Okay, sounds good," she agrees hurriedly, "let's go."

With that, the two of them walk casually yet fast towards the parking lot. You'd think they would look awkward, but the two pulled off a normal appeal rather well. Despite them not talking, there wasn't a weird feeling between them. It was clear they were just too used to each other to ever be bothered. 

It left a conflicted feeling inside of her. Solar knew she was trying not to think about it, but even then, Jinhwan was still an evident thing on her mind. A lot of this was too normal to her, she'd been through it many times before. When the service ended, it kept slowly sinking in that this was just another thing that had to happen. She would've died if the woman didn't, after all. Was she supposed to wish that it was her instead?

The only thing that wasn't normal was the strong feelings she would always seem to have for this boy. They seemed to mess with her at the worst times. As she glanced to him as they walked through the grass of the graveyard, her mind kept replaying everything. That day at the studio, everything he told her, the airport - she hadn't really had time to think about it. Yet, she still felt like he wasn't lying. Now that he'd told her the reason behind it, she found herself understanding. 

She was sure she would've done something stupid like that, too, if she ever found him having a drunk hookup. It wasn't something you ever wanted to find your significant other doing, especially not with their best friend. Solar could understand it was probably hard on him.

And she did understand that this ex of his was clearly obsessed and psychotic. Could she really blame him for that? No, of course not, but she just hated the thought of how she was clinging to him while she was in New York.

Still, at the end of the day, Solar wasn't innocent, either.

She just had to face the fact that she wasn't the only one who made mistakes. After all, the majority of her decisions were made on a limb without her thinking.

Like getting back with Jungkook.

Though it hadn't happened yet, she had technically told him she wanted it to. Could she go back on that? 

Not without breaking his heart all over again. 

She was an idiot, an idiot who could never understand what she wanted.

"Excuse me, Kim Soomi," a loud, booming voice seemed to catch her off guard. The girl jumps in shock from it, holding a hand to her heart in surprise as she turns to meet the eyes of her worst nightmare - a detective.

Jinhwan is before her, standing between the officer and her, as if he was ready to fight someone. The brunette clears her throat, knowing this was inevitable, and steps forward, "yes, that's me?"

"Hello, I'm Chae Ryujeong of the Seoul Police Department," the officer displays a badge, before clearing her throat, "I'm conducting an investigation on behalf of Kang Jisoo and was hoping to ask you a few questions."

Her expression doesn't falter. That was the first rule of this all, don't show that you're guilty, obviously. 

"Sure," she nods, "that's fine."

With a nod, the detective shifts to pull out a notepad and pen. Exhaling, she opens her mouth to voice the first question. 

Here goes.



+


"Are you okay? Did it go alright?" Jinhwan is parading her with questions as the girl makes her way across the parking lot to meet with him near the boys vehicle. They'd told him to just wait inside the car with them, but Jinhwan had been too worried. The other members didn't know the events that had happened between the two that day at the service. This was why it was more than shocking to see them associate so casually.

When Solar finally stops before him, Jinhwan brings her to the side so they're covered by cars and free to talk openly without anyone seeing. His hands rest on her forearms, and he's bending down slightly so he can peer into those green irises of hers. Solar, who one who assumed was shaken up, weirdly enough appeared unbothered. She wasn't tainted in the slightest by being questioned. No, it had brought back too many memories to her.

After she was questioned, she texted Sunwoo to update him. Talking to him, like it always did, brought her back down to Earth. He always reminded her that this wasn't something new to her, she wasn't truly the innocent girl she forced herself to believe she was. Solar had seen people die, too many times to count, and this was someone who deserved what came to her. She couldn't let herself feel bad for that.

Just be glad the family don't know of the kind of person she was, in some ways, it makes it worse that way, he'd told her. It was weird to think about, but he did have a point. It would probably hurt them more to know what kind of monster that woman was, it was easier for them to just mourn her how they saw her. Solar would have to leave it at that, and now that she was been questioned and passed with flying colors, there was nothing more they could do to her.

It should be over now. 

"It went fine, Jinhwan," she clears her throat, eyes lifelessly meeting his. Jinhwan was no doubt confused by her sudden change in attitude, but he seemed to get the message. "They don't suspect a thing."

His hands fall back to his side, and he diverts his eyes elsewhere, "that's good, Soo, you should probably tell the others that if you're comfortable, they've been worried."

"Yeah," she nods, not knowing what more to say to him. It was clear there was a lot Solar needed to fix in her life, and a new one had been added today. Her relationship with her best friend was possibly on the line here.

But Jinhwan, Jinhwan was one of those things, too.

Still, she didn't know where to start. She didn't know what to do from there. All she could do was slowly walk away from him towards the car door so she could talk to the others, when his hand reaches out and stops her by grabbing her wrist.

"Wait," Jinhwan starts, hesitancy evident in his tone, "Soo, where do we stand? What's going on in your head?"

She purses her lips, not moving from her halted stance even when his hand leaves her wrist. Solar doesn't turn around to face him, knowing it would create a much unneeded tension between them. Her mind was searching for the words to say, but what were they?

"I love you," she abruptly blurts before she could stop herself, cringing on the inside when she let the words escape her mouth, "I understand why you did what you did, and I believe that you're not the kind of person to ever cheat."

Jinhwan opens his mouth to speak but closes it again, because Solar turned to face him. 

"But we can't get back together, Nani," she breathes. The words cut into his heart like glass, and his eyes could only stare back at her whilst his heart ached. "Not because of this, but because I still have feelings for Jungkook, too. It's become more clear to me since I've been around him," Jinhwan tries to speak again, but she cuts him off, "and I know you're gonna say that it's always been like that and that we worked fine - and while that might be true, I can't go back on the things I've already started."

His eyes narrow a bit, and he starts to put the pieces together in his head. "You're getting back together with him, aren't you?"

"I don't know," she answers instantly, "but it's definitely there. We had our run, Jinhwan, and I think that a part of me needs to figure out if that's what I want to continue."

"So it's his turn, huh?"

"Sure," she sighs, "I guess if that's how you want to put it."

He doesn't say anything after that, he just stares at his feet. Jinhwan was obviously hurt by what he learned, but he had to admit he saw it coming. Junhoe, everyone predicted it, everyone told him the consequences of it all. Though Soo said she understood why he did it, they both knew the way he handled it wasn't okay, it was wrong. 

So, as hard as it was for him and as much as he hated the thought of seeing her with Jungkook, he had to let it happen. He had to let her figure it out, and hope that it lead her right back to him. 

Jinhwan knew that, but it still hurt. Of course it still hurts.

Solar, not knowing what more she could say to ease this conversation, spins on her heel, figuring it was about time she went and talked to the others. Knowing they were just watching this interaction between her and Jinhwan made it that much worse.

Just when she took her first step towards the vehicle, assuming that was it between them, for the hundredth time today, she was stopped. 

But this time, it wasn't just him grabbing her wrist. The girl was spun around to face him again, but he didn't back up that time. His eyes only peered into hers for a harsh second, before he did something she should've guessed he'd do.

He kissed her.


131:00 | not the best way to receive an invitation

"Yah, Jinhwan," she gawks as he'd pulled away, his eyes fluttering open. Solar is in a daze, a daze of not understanding the fact he just did that. Most of all, she didn't understand what she was feeling. She was a jumble of longing, lust, and maybe even resentment. The girl was a mess, a mess who didn't know how to face the boy before her.

"I needed that, one last time," the caramel boy breathes, taking a step back to distance himself again. Solar feels the cold air hit her skin as soon as he was gone, the warmth of his presence leaving goosebumps on her skin. A part of her was aching, a part of her wanted to just pull him back and cling onto him forever.

It wasn't like they couldn't get passed it. They'd fought before, they'd had rough spots before, they could get passed this one. Jinhwan was still special to her as much as she wanted to pretend he wasn't. Could she really get back with Jungkook after all of this? Solar felt so sure of her in-the-moment decision, but rationally thinking about it, it didn't seem like it would make sense, would it?

Her and Jinhwan had been through too much, too much to just put behind her. But something inside of Solar was telling her she had to let this play out, she had to give herself time to fully understand everything she felt and had been feeling for the last year and a half.

And to do that, she knew she had to figure out what her emotions were towards Jungkook.

So, as much as it still hurt, she turned away and finally headed towards the vehicle the boys were sitting in, probably watching the entire scene in bewilderment. Though it wasn't a huge deal to her that she hooked up with her best friend that night unknowingly, she knew that it was a massive deal to everyone else. That entire thing was practically what broke her and Jinhwan up, and now it would put a wall between her and Junhoe due to how weird he was probably feeling.

Personally, Solar didn't really care. She had a history of doing idiotic things she would never be able to remember when she was drunk. Even now that she was told what happened, she didn't really remember, but it sounded like something she would do. The two of them were idiots when they were drunk, they probably got carried away. In her eyes, it was still nothing she hadn't already done before in her life.

Though she could admit it was a little odd to imagine herself with her best friend.

With a sigh, she swings open the van door. The boys of iKON, who had presumably been watching her every move, eyes are on her immediately.

"Hey losers!" she yells at the top of her lungs, throwing her arms in the air and catapulting into the vehicle.

The others start yelling greetings back at her, and it was bound to give anyone a headache from how loud they were. The thing was, when she jumped into the vehicle, she had jumped onto whoever was sitting in the back seat's laps.

The boys scoot over as she gains her composure, making room for her on the seats. Solar takes the time, as the van finally calms down, to look around her, greeting everyone individually and hugging who was next to her. She had to admit it was weird not being around them all the time now.

It was when she got to Junhoe, who was sitting next to her, that there was an unknown tension in the air. Since everyone knew what happened, they didn't know what would happen between them.

They also didn't know that Jinhwan told Solar.

Until, to break the air, Solar giggles giddily, wrapping her arms around Junhoe's neck and pulling him in for one of the tightest hugs of the century. The boys eyes are bulging out of their sockets, and the others are bursting into silent snickers at how bewildered he was. Junhoe still was unsure how he felt about what happened or how to approach it, but he was still used to his best friend. That's what inclined him to wrap his arms around her torso, hugging her back.

"I missed you, you tree fuck," she's sighing in awe as she pulls away. Junhoe looks hesitant to speak, so Solar does it for him, as blunt as ever, "I know what happened, alright? You can all stop acting so weird."

Silence falls over the six boys. Some of their mouths fall slightly agape, their eyes exchanging glances of awe. It was like none of them could believe the words she just uttered. Solar understood it was a little crazy to them, they'd never dealt with anything like this before. Personally, she believed they had bigger worries on their hands.

"Look," she breathes, "there's no point on dwelling on it, okay? It happened, and it was meaningless. Don't we have bigger issues at hand?"

Before any of them could finally speak, the door was opened. The sight of the caramel boy sent everyone into more deep thought. He offers a nod of greeting, and the boys move so he could slide inside the vehicle to join them, right next to Solar.

Already seeming to know what the topic was, Jinhwan clears his throat, "Soo is right, don't be childish about it and move forward," he glances at the tall boy, "right, June-ya?"

Seeming to snap out of his daze, Junhoe shrugs, "that's fine, I still don't remember a thing."

"That's what I'm saying," Solar agrees.

The boy starts giggling in response, seeming to not be so worried about it anymore.

It was clear the boys were still confused about what they witnessed between the two exes who were supposed to have been in a fight, but they didn't dare to question it. Whatever was going on between them, they needed to sort it out themselves.

So it was back to the matter at hand, as Solar sighs, "so, I got questioned by the police."


+


A deep sigh leaves the lips of Solar Lee, her eyes rather dull that evening as she glances again to the road, waiting for that familiar car that belonged to Jeon Jungkook to pull in the driveway to pick her up. Her head was all kinds of mixed up. She assured them in the end it would be fine, that she and they should be off the hook. With the detectives off her case, they wouldn't place her as a suspect. She was safe, Sunwoo was right, he would never dare leave a track. All that was left was to find whoever was tormenting her now.

The fact she hadn't heard from them in so long left an ill feeling inside of her. What did that mean exactly? From what she knew, that they were planning something big.

That was Sol's best guess, and little did she know how close she was. Maybe Sunwoo should've told her about that text he got at the airport that day.

Though she really should be focusing on the fact there was still someone after her, her head couldn't help but wander back to everything else happening. Jungkook, Jinhwan, her and Jungkook getting back together soon, that kiss with Jinhwan, everything he told her. Truthfully, she wasn't mad at Jinhwan anymore. She came to understand what really happened, and she couldn't blame him. But it didn't mean that just because they made up they could magically be back together. It was a little late for that.

"But, I've never seen you as happy as you are with him, I've known since day one, I just wanted more time with you," Jungkook's previous words repeat in her head from that day in New York, "I know you love him, he's who you should be with. This is me telling you it's okay to let go now."

That talk they had was one of the most heartbreaking things she'd ever experienced. Jungkook was not the kind of guy to admit to losing, she knew that better than anyone. It was clear now he never seemed to mean those words. She guessed he was just saying them because he felt obligated.

Yet, the words he spoke all that time ago weren't so far fetched. Solar couldn't act as if there was no truth to them. She knew there was a lot of truth to them. But she had to think about it rationally, she had to think about what had happened, she had to think about who she really saw herself with. Solar felt like this was what she had to do to figure that out. In the end, she was hoping fate would drag her whichever way was right.

She hoped.

Though, just like every other time, Solar Lee didn't have the time of day to truly focus on just her ridiculous love life. She had a job, fame, family, and the fact there was someone trying to kill her out there. Her complicated love life never seemed to fit in the schedule.

But it definitely continued to torture her.

The sound of a roaring engine pulls her out of her head. Turning back towards the road, that jet black Ferrari he just bought sparkling under the sunlight. Due to the fact that, by now, there was basically no one in the parking lot, it was safe for him to get out and show himself. The only people left at the graveyard were Solar and the boys of iKON who insisted they stayed until her ride got here - or until she hopefully changed her mind.

She's about to run to the car as soon as she sees Jimin's grinning face, but she couldn't help but glance back for a second at Jinhwan. There he was still, an emotionless look on his face, eyes narrowed, caramel hair messy from the wind, leaning against the side of the van the boys were seated in. His hands were in his pocket, and he was in clear deep thought. 

That was, until Jeon Jungkook swung open his car door. Solar jumps at this, knowing there was absolutely nothing positive that could come out of this action. 

"Hi, Mi, I missed you," he smiles to her first, those bunny teeth almost convincing her he could never cause any harm - almost. 

Before he tried to just slip right past her to go Jinhwan's way, of course. Solar didn't want to witness another male domination battle, so she exhales deeply, following the few steps he had taken towards the boy and grabbing onto his arm to stop him.

As if he knew this would happen, he turns with a pout, "what~? I'm just going to give him an invitation," he tells her all-so-innocently. The brunette boy holds up a fancily made piece of paper, the gold lining on it nearly blinding her as she squinted to try and see what the hell it was. 

However, Jungkook didn't really allow her as his hand falls back to his side. "Nothing more, Mi," he hums to her, and before she could stop him, he spun on his heel and walked away.

Well, she could've stopped him, but she knew there was no point. She was more focused on what the hell that invitation was for?

Jungkook halts right before Jinhwan, a little smirk on his face as if he was planning something evil. At the sight, Jinhwan immediately stands up straight, turning to walk away and get into the van. Whatever he was trying to start, Jinhwan wasn't in the mood anymore. A day ago, he would've fought Jungkook with every ounce of strength in his body. Now, he didn't have the energy. He was too busy dwelling on what happened, on the fact he really lost the girl he loved, on the fact the guy he hated was getting a chance he (maybe did, but Jinhwan would not admit that) was getting a chance he didn't deserve with Solar. And nothing he did could ease the hurt he felt, even though her knew that a part of him deserved it for what he did.

It hurt, it hurt like hell.

So the last thing he wanted to do was face Jeon Jungkook. Until that menacing voice calls out to him, "whoa, hold up, tough guy. I think you'll want to attend this for Soomi. If not, at least give it to your friends."

The way he talked to Jinhwan clearly showed Jungkook saw him as inferior. It made his blood boil. But his interest had been spiked enough for him to turn and snatch whatever the paper was out of his hands. Jungkook is giggling as he witnesses his demeanor, "not so big now that you're on the losing side, huh?"

That was it. 

With one turn, Jinhwan's fist flies into Jungkook's face. It hit him directly in the nose, a crack sounding the air. Never had any of them seen Jinhwan out of all people fight someone before, and Jungkook had no doubt not expected it. It seemed to happen so fast he didn't see it coming. 

A curse leaves his lips from the impact, knocking him back a bit but he refused to fall. His hand covers his nose as he regains his composure, eyes flaming as he looks back up to the culprit. 

"Watch your mouth, Jungkook," Jinhwan hisses, "don't act like you're anything special."

"You fucking-" he was ready to lunge straight at him, to destroy all of his hopes and dreams. But just as he goes forward, someone stops him. Out of anger, he nearly hit those people two, only until he saw Jimin and Solar at each of his sides.

"Don't, we need to go, Kook," Jimin advises him in that stern voice of his.

Meanwhile, Solar sighed as she walked to the front of him, in the middle of him and Jinhwan. Her hands cup his cheeks, and she leans in closely to examine what kind of condition his nose was in. 

After her time in New York, though, she was used to it and it wasn't anything she hadn't seen before. She liked to imagine she could hear Sangyeon's knowing voice in her head in those moments, telling her what to do to nurse a wound. 

You know, she sort of missed them. 

"Okay, let's go, Jimin's right," she breathes, "I can clean your wound when we get home."

"Here, give me the keys," Jimin practically demands as he forces him to go back to the car, "I'll drive, you can rest."

"No," Jungkook snaps at he kept pestering him, "stop acting like I'm fucking disabled, I barely felt that twirp's punch."

Solar sighs as she watches Jimin take the keys from him anyway, pushing him into the backseat. Jungkook, in those moments, was basically a baby. It was weird to see him so angry and vulgar, really, because back then, that was all anyone ever saw him as. A helpless, kind, baby that didn't want to hurt anyone.

"I'm sorry, Mi," Jinhwan says into the quiet, only meaning it to her, "but he was clearly asking for it, did you hear what he was saying?!"

"No," she sighs, "and I don't really want to know what it was to get even you to hit him." The girl shakes her head, hair gently blowing in the wind as she refuses to make eye contact, "Jungkook did it on purpose, you know. He wanted you to hit him."

"Yeah," the short boy breathes, "so I've realized."

"Hey, what did he even give you?" she suddenly recalls, turning to him fully.

Jinhwan pulls the paper out of his pocket, unfolding it so he could read aloud to her, "singer-songwriter-rapper Kim Soomi's 24th Birthday Extravaganza," he reads the title, giggling a bit at how stupid it sounded. There's a bitter taste in Jinhwan's voice when he hums, "wow, guess he's throwing you a party, at a nice venue. Of course he wanted to give it directly to me, huh?"

In a bit of shock from what she heard, she could only shrug, "Yeah, that sounds about Jungkook. He does like to be flashy."

With a sigh, he sends her a small smile, as if reminding her with that grin of his that he was still there for her. "I'll be there, Soo, just because I love you."


132:00 | flights to korea

"A birthday party, really?" Solar is quizzically asking as she climbs into the backseat of the Ferrari. As soon as she closes the door, Jimin speeds off down the road without another thought. He was tired of everything that had happened here today, and the vibe of the graveyard they were by was starting to creep him out.

At the sight of Solar, Jungkook grins from ear to ear like a child. Scooting over in his seat, he leans his head on her shoulder as he hums, "yep, I planned it all myself - oh, and Jimin - but it was my idea. It's at the prettiest, most expensive club you can rent in Seoul, and I have even more surprises for you that I, of course, can't tell you."

The girl laughs in disbelief, shaking her head as she raises a hand to mess with his hair. His brown locks are soft under the touch of her fingertips, and she couldn't help but sigh as she thinks about the words he spoke, "I didn't think anyone really remembered my birthday."

"Of course we do, Mi," Jimin gawks from the driver's seat, "we would never forget such a special day."

"Yeah, I know," she nods, "just with everything going on, I honestly forgot about it."

"You always have too much on your plate," Jungkook groans in annoyance, vividly upset with how much she had to go through, "whoever this is that's doing this shit to you, the police bothering you, your family, your job - no one should have to handle all of that. You deserve a night to be free and happy."

"Oh, Kookie," she breathes, poking his nose with a chuckle, "you're a sweetheart."

"Anything for my Soomi," he gushes, and Jimin winces from the front of the car. It was hard to believe it was really happening. Jungkook and Soomi were getting back together. He'd been thinking and thinking about it, but he still couldn't decide if he was happy about it or not. Was Soomi really better off with Jungkook? Was she really happier? Jimin wasn't even sure, how could he be?

All he could do was support her and hope she found the right road.

"How's your wound?" Solar is raising a brow as Jungkook sits up from leaning on her shoulder so he could make eye contact.

The boy shrugs, "for someone so small, he can hit harder than I thought."

She didn't mean to laugh.

"Shut up, he's not that small."

"He's a dwarf, Mi, kinda like you."

"Jungkook, please stop talking."

But he only bursts into fits of laughter. Solar groans at how rude he was to Jinhwan, it really drove her crazy. All of the other boys got along just fine, but those two literally just nearly tried to kill each other.

While she understood why, she still hated it.

And even though Solar Lee would never make any sense, even though a part of her was still thinking about Jinhwan, she still found a way to smile. A part of her was still happy with this life she was forming, and she thought that then, maybe it wouldn't be so bad.


+


"you're what?!" Eric is the first to blurt in absolute shock, "yo, someone tell me if I heard this dude correctly," he points to the leader before looking to the other members in hope for some assistance.

Though the others looked either confused or just unphased, with no in-between. It wasn't like they had a right to get mad at their leader, he was the one who got to make decisions and no one was allowed to disagree. However, Eric felt like this was a special time in which it was okay to question Sunwoo's decisions - just this once.

"I'm not gonna stand around here while Solar gets hurt and possibly killed," the gang leader hisses, eyes full of fire. Heaving his bag further up his shoulder, the clinking sound of the guns that were inside it rings through the silent living room. Sunwoo is clearly distressed as he chunks his cigarette in the ashtray, exhaling, "do you not remember what I said? They're making a move, fucking soon. I have their location, I have their license plate, I have their identity. I know who they fucking are, and I'm not going to stand here and let her die when I can go kill that motherfucker myself."

There's a long pause as everyone thought about what he was saying.

"Well, he has a point," Hyunjae speaks up, "it would be wrong to let Solar die."

"Are they even planning to kill her?" Eric scoffs, "wouldn't she be dead already? I thought they just wanted to ruin her life or something."

There's disgust written all over Sunwoo's face as he looks to the youngest, "God, you're a fucking idiot."

With a sigh, Younghoon clears his throat so he could kindly explain to the maknae before Sunwoo busted a bullet through his head instead. "From what we have gathered, we know that this person is rather smart, they don't intend to do what the last did. They had close relations with the woman we killed, so they likely intend to seek revenge in a much dirtier way on Solar. Sunwoo used the license plate and the hotel they were staying at from when they bugged Solar's room here in the Castle to track them back to Korea. He knows they're going to do something soon, so do you expect him to allow her to get injured and likely killed based off their presumed motives?"

Eric blinks rapidly at the boy who was obviously too intelligent for him, "look, you're gonna have to use some smaller words if you want me to comprehend whatever the fuck you just said."

"Eric, honey," Hyunjoon sighs in disappointment from beside him on the sofa, "just shut the fuck up, kay?" The boy only blinks at him in continuous confusion, but he ignores it. Instead, the glorious boy looks to Sunwoo, "go save my queen, okay?"

Sunwoo's face is blank as he just glares at the particularly odd human. "Right," he deadpans after a moment, "I think they must know I'm onto them. They definitely are aware of my existence, it's not like they think Solar killed their partner on her own. I believe they're likely moving locations often because of that," he explains, the other nodding in understanding.

"Keep an eye on there whereabouts and keep me updated on the changes," he orders, "half of you also need to spend nights and days supervising the outside of the Lee mansion. I don't know what they're planning, but I do not want her family involved. The Lee siblings know of what's going on because of that night at the park, so alert them of what's happening so they don't freak out."

"No problem, Sunwoo," Sangyeon nods, "we'll take care of it."

Sunwoo doesn't respond, his facial expression doesn't even change. All he does is adjust the bag on his shoulder before turning and heading towards the front door.

"Hey, don't get yourself killed out there!" Haknyeon yells out as his hand was on the door handle.

Sunwoo only snorts, "don't insult me."

"Wait, Sunwoo, what if your father comes back or calls?" Younghoon asked just before he was going to step outside.

This was a good question, Sunwoo realized. Pursing his lips, he brushes it off casually, "tell him I'm on a little business trip, tell everyone that who asks, refuse to share anymore details."

Though doubtful, he nods in response, "will do."

With that, he left, he left to catch the earliest flight to nowhere other than beloved South Korea.

The other members only exchanged glances of disbelief as he closed the door behind him. No one really knew what to do without Sunwoo here, it left a weird feeling in the air. But they knew what he had to do, and they knew he could do it. Sunwoo was smart and powerful, he'd been born to do this, after all. As sad as it was, it was his life calling.

No one was better at it than him.

"Wait, can I tell Chanhee?" Eric suddenly asks, "what if he asks?"

"No, dumb ass," Haknyeon responds instantly.

"But he's gonna know," Eric whines, "he always asks me too! The dude is so persistent!"

"Eric, honey," Hyunjoon says again, "just close your mouth."

After the idiotic bickering had ended, some of the boys would tend to keeping an eye on the "stalker's" location like Sunwoo ordered. The others would head to the Lee household, after sending Chaerin and the other siblings a very vague text that ordered them to come outside to the park that was located in the middle of the Castle. It was the same park Solar and Sunwoo met at that night, a park that had a lot of significant memories attached to it.

And sure enough, that evening would become on in the brains of the Lee siblings.

The few members were left waiting at the park for a while. Cigarettes in between their fingers, smoke fluttering through the air. The first one's to arrive were Juyeon and Jaehyun, who weren't too shocked at the sudden meeting since they were okay acquaintances. They were only confused at the fact that Sunwoo was absent. It didn't take them long to figure it was about their big sister, so lack of Sunwoo was weird in that matter.

Chaerin, however, was as late as ever. Not that it was shocking to anyone.

Until finally, the girl came pouncing down the sidewalk, dark brown hair blowing in the wind. Her same green eyes sparkled under the sunlight, and it was a daring sight for anyone to witness. The only thing was - she had Chanhee with her.

"Oh, you've gotta be kidding me," Haknyeon mutters at the sight.

As soon as they stop before them, Chaerin crosses her arms over her chest, unimpressed at the sight of the group. "Where's Sunwoo?" is the first thing she demands more than asks.

"Why is he here?!' Eric cries instead, motioning to the pink haired boy beside her, "I'm pretty sure the message said Lee siblings only."

"I'm pretty sure you don't need to be talking right now," Chanhee retorts with a sarcastic smile, "if it has to do with Solar, I have every right to be here as anyone else, so spit it out. Do I need to go save her? Did she do something stupid - again?"

"No," Younghoon is the first to say, "well, not quite. Sunwoo wanted us to update you on the fact we'll be keeping an eye on your house in case whoever is after Solar tries to show up."

They all nod slowly, taking in the information. "What's with the sudden update?" Jaehyun questions, "Is there something new going on?"

"Sunwoo's been tracking them," Younghoon goes on, "he anticipates they're going to try something soon but he doesn't know what. He's been on edge ever since the incident at the park you guys went to. Then when he dropped Solar off at the airport, he got a text from them, too, something about how he won't be able to protect her if she's in Korea."

"I think I know where this one's going," Chaerin mutters, "you're telling me Sunwoo went to Korea?!"

"Bingo!" Eric shouts, causing everyone to glare at him.

Sighing, Younghoon nods, "indeed. He's going to protect her from whatever is going to go down. Here, we're keeping check on their location."

"Is she going to be okay?" Juyeon hesitantly speaks up.

"Probably," Younghoon responds blankly, "Sunwoo is not one to make mistakes, I think we all know that."

Chanhee wrinkles his nose, disgusted at how fond the members spoke of their leader, "at least not when it comes to his infatuation with my best friend."

"oh, but one last thing," the member raises his voice just when they were about to turn around and leave.

"Don't tell Solar about Sunwoo going to Korea."


133:00 | solar's gay lover

Soomi was tired.

Exhaustion is written all over her face as she looks up to her manager. There's a grin on his face as he announces, doing some jazz hands, "Congratulations, CEO-nim loved your new songs. Your album is set to be releasing in April!"

She smiles at this despite how she felt. "That's great news," she nods cheerily, "I hope it's received well."

"Of course, Soomi," he hums, "it always is."

She only sends him a thankful glance before looking back down to her phone. The poor girl didn't notice the glare she was given by her own manager. Why would she ever suspect such a thing from someone she thought she knew so well?

It wasn't like she would ever think to. There were bigger matters at hand, after all.
