"I'm fucking sorry, Sol," he spills, "I'm really fucking sorry for failing, lil' sister."

To say she was caught off guard was an understatement. All she could do was blink at him in confusion, trying to figure out why the hell he thought he had to apologize to her. "What are you sorry for?" 

That would be one of the only times in her life she ever saw him so vulnerable. "Sunwoo had told me that he figured there were a lot of staff working with them. I also knew that Sunwoo was here the entire time - and I should have listened to my gut and told you," he heaves out a frustrated sigh towards himself, gripping onto the railing after he throws his cig to the ground and steps on it, "the one time I don't listen to my gut, and look what happens. Tough boy Sunwoo wanted to handle it all on his own, and I was dumb enough to let him."

"Whoa, none of that is your fault," she asserts before he could say anymore, "I don't blame you for any of it. I know how persistent Sunwoo is, and I know he wanted to do it all on his own. He just underestimated how much it all was," she pauses, pursing her lips in a straight line as she holds in any emotion that wanted to escape, "we all did."

Knowing his sister was going to break down now that they were talking about Sunwoo, he curses to himself, "god dammit, I'm fucking sorry, anyway. I know you're feeling a lot of emotional pain that not either of your weird high school love interests can solve."

Solar stares at him in disappointment for a moment. "Don't call them high school love interests, seriously," she tells him sternly, but he obviously had no intent of listening to her.

"That's what they are," is all he insists, before sighing as he turns towards the balcony door, "well, we better get back in there, little sister. Thanks for checking on me," he walks closer to her to hit her back. But like every time, Jaehyun was stronger than he thought, causing her to nearly stumble over.

Her eyes burn into his soul as she snaps, "seriously, can't even help yourself when I'm literally half crippled?!"

He only shrugs, "sister, I would only call that about one-fourth."

She was unamused. "You're a fucking idiot, Jae."

Beaming a smile at her, the mood had grown serious again. There was a question tickling the tip of his tongue, before he finally decides to propose, "so, are you sure you're going to be able to talk about it? I'd like to tell you we can wait, but it's better to get it out now than later."

Having had already had this talk, Solar figured it was coming as she nods in assurance, "yeah, yeah, I am - don't worry."

Because just like he said, it was better now than later.


153:00 | make up to break up

When Solar and Jaehyun had headed inside, she'd been stopped in the hall by Junhoe. Since they hadn't really gotten to talk much since they'd got to her apartment, he was asking her how she was and like everyone else, if she would be okay talking about it. Solar had told him she was only really concerned about Jinhwan, and when he asked why, she explained it to him.

There'd been a look of shock on his face, before it had faltered, "well, no one can blame you," he tells her, "she was literally trying to kill you. It wasn't like that weird bitch didn't have it coming, either."

"Do you think he's going to be upset?" she nervously queries, and there's an uncertain look on Junhoe's face. 

He hesitated for a good while. He knew Jinhwan well, better than most people, and he also knew everything he had saw that happened - which was indeed true no matter what Jinhwan tried to say. Did he think his hyung had some kind of feelings for Jimin? He wasn't sure, and he would hope the answer would be no. After all, why would he when he had someone like Soomi? The thought was just disgusting.

Still, you couldn't be too sure, and Junhoe knew that a part of Jinhwan likely had to have some kind of liking to her. With that, he cautions, "Either way, Soo, he doesn't have a right to be upset, your life was literally on the line..." he trails off, pausing for a moment in thought as he seemed to recall something, before he hesitatedly adds, "but... remember, Jinan-hyung's also been through a lot, too, we all have."

Solar had shaken her head, knowing he was right but yet she still couldn't shake the feeling. Right now, none of them needed to be against each other. Drama being stirred up would cause fights, and fights would cause separation, which gave too many chances for something more to occur. She knew it was his ex girlfriend from a long time ago, she knew they must have had something at some point in time, and she knew that no matter what he said - she was still the girl that he cheated on her with. Solar didn't know what the hell it had been about such an ugly psychotic being, but she felt that a part of Jinhwan had some kind of attachment to her. 

That, or it had all just been like Jungkook said - out of spite, to get back at her.

She just didn't want to take the risk of causing something right now. Her and Jinhwan, currently in his head, were doing good. They were back on their weird friendly terms, and she didn't really mind it that way, even though she couldn't help but resent him deep down. She hated that she was even having to worry about this specific thing instead of focusing on the fact that it was her manager.

Her fucking manager, she was going to have to tell them all that, too. She hadn't told that bit of information to quite anyone just yet. Just thinking about it made her feel sick to her stomach.

Knowing there was no more stalling she could possibly do, she tells Junhoe they should probably go ahead before everyone gets impatient. 

Solar had followed Junhoe back into the living room to join everyone. Her eyes rake across the room of all the friends, seeing the way Jungkook was gleaming at her, clearly glad she was back, yet concern was written in his irises since he knew what was going on in her head. Her breathe had almost hitched in her throat when she looked to Jinhwan, who was still sitting there in that same position in the middle of all the boys, criss-crossed, with his hands cupping his own face, caressing his temples as he appeared rather distressed. She thought back to the brief story he told, what had happened to the poor boys. Was it really the best time to throw this at him when he was already traumatized? 

No.

Did she have a choice?

No.

Bobby and Hanbin were still trying to ease Jinhwan's nerves, and the group had been in a conversation with her siblings she hadn't heard the beginning of. 

"The authorities aren't going to know who was good or bad, are they?" Donghyuk had been thinking out loud, a rather horrified expression on his face as he tried to make sense of this kind of thing, "What if whoever did - they're all staff members, right? - what if they try to make something up?"

Jinhwan's voice is hoarse when he adds, "and my fingerprints are going to be there somewhere, all of ours are. It would be easy to trace it back to me."

Solar had shaken herself out of her thoughts as she heard this, realizing she had been standing there like an idiot for a while. The only ones who had noticed were the boys of BTS, who had been waiting for her to come sit with them. Due to her apartment being rather huge, they had enough space just barely for everyone. Nonetheless, it was extremely cramped, but moveable. 

The Bangtan boys occupied the left side of her living area, sitting around on the singular sofa there and then on the floor. Jinhwan and the others were on the right, and her siblings, Hee, and childhood friends were sitting on chairs they brought in from the kitchen. 

Taking her seat again next to Jungkook and Jimin, the group had basically all greeted her as she sat down. She had greeted them already when they got here the first time, which was terrifying for her, as they all wanted to each hug her so badly that she felt like she was going to get her other arm broken. Thankfully, they were all patient enough to take turns instead of trampling over her.

Jungkook's hand goes to grab hers, and he held it not as a romantic gesture or a way to bother anyone, but to comfort her. Solar sends him a smile as if to thank him. 

It would be okay.

They could all get through this if they had each other, they had to. They had to find her manager and put him in the dirt.

"Don't worry," Jaehyun had assured the iKON boys, "we made sure to get rid of any evidence, you can all relax."

She couldn't lie, she was a little proud of her siblings for that.

"No one can trace a thing," he goes on, but still exhales deeply despite what should be positive news, "but that doesn't really solve much for us. Most of the staffs that were working with them were killed, and the ones that weren't - along with the culprit - have probably ran as far as they can manage or are in hiding," he halts his words. And in that moment, Solar felt the biggest lump form in her throat as everyone's eyes seemed to wander to her. Until finally, her brother finishes, "which is why, we need to know every detail of what happened, Solar."

Everyone is sending her either a nervous glance or a reassuring smile. Jungkook squeezes her hand tightly that he was still holding, leaning over to whisper in her ear, "you'll be okay, Mi, I'm right here with you." His voice sent shivers down her spine, but somehow, gave her the strength as she sat up straight.

Though, just as she's going to start telling her story in the way she recalled it, a suddenly concerned Jinhwan had piped up in his seat, "are we sure she's ready to talk about it? Soomi-ah's gone through a lot in the last twelve hours, she might not feel well enough."

She's shocked that he said anything, until she recalls that they were on good terms, at least for right now. Despite how shaken up he was, Jinhwan was still worried about her for good reason.

"I think she's-" Chaerin had started to say, before Solar had cut her off with her own voice.

"-I'm fine," she clears her throat.

She wasn't, but she had to be. Even though all of it had indeed been too much on her mental, what could she do other than keep going and face it?

She'd been so focused on being afraid of Jinhwan's reaction to the news about Jimin, that her mind had momentarily forgotten about the real issues at hand. She'd been distracted, more so, from the thing that was destroying her inside. 

Opening her mouth to speak, she closes it as the image of Sunwoo's weak body flying to the ground flashes in her head. The way he had already clearly taken a beating, which the doctor's said was a concussion to the head. The way that even through he was already so hurt, he managed to jump in front of her right when it was going to be too late for her. The way that even when he was losing, he refused to let anything happen to Solar Lee. 

She would never forget it, the way his brown irises still managed to twinkle when they met her own as he fell to the ground. She could still see the bullet hit him no matter how hard she tried to erase the image from her mind. 

Still, she knew he didn't go down without a fight. That wasn't what Kim Sunwoo was about, after all.

So, with the deepest breathe she'd ever taken, she tells them everything. Everything about how she heard fighting when she reached the top floor thinking it was her siblings, how she heard gunshots, and how they had disappeared when she entered. She explained how he'd snuck up behind her and tried to choke her, to him about to put a bullet in her head, to how Sunwoo showed up.

When she got to the part about Sunwoo getting shot, she coughed on her words. Jungkook and Jimin had hurriedly comforted her, and she had cleared her throat, annoyed with herself, before she went on to tell them the rest regarding the incident - most importantly, that the culprit was her manager.

She had yet to tell the part about Jinhwan's ex, the felt like that should come later.

Everyone was sat there in shock after she uttered the words. No one knew what to say, what to think, all they could think about was how sick it was. 

"It makes sense," Jaehyun had finally observed, "the woman was iKON's manager, right? It makes sense, I can't believe we didn't realize it earlier."

"Fuck," Chaerin is cursing in frustration, "now that sick piece of shit got away."

"There's no way he'll ever show his face at YGE again," Namjoon speaks the obvious from where he's sitting on the floor.

"Do you think he'll try anything else, though, after that?" Donghyuk asks no one in particular, more so just wanting to know what everyone's standpoint was. 

"Probably," Jaehyun sighs, knowing it was the last thing anyone wanted to hear, "just with a different approach, he no doubt is aware he won't ever be able to physically get Solar."

His words sink in everyone's heads, until they realize what it is that he means.

"Oh," Jungkook swallows a lump in his throat, his mind wandering back to 2018, "you think that means..."

"... another scandal." Solar mutters the rest for him, burying her face in her hands as she comprehended the entire thing. 

"Look, we just have to find him before then," Chanhee assures her, "though, without that insolent, I'm not quite sure how that's going to be possible."

"True," Chaerin mutters dully, staring at her feet, "we really need Sunwoo."

Sunwoo, they needed Sunwoo more than anything.

Solar had yet to break the rest of the news she had, particularly for Jinhwan, because she realized there was still more she had to do. It was something she should have already done, but hadn't thought of because of how much she'd been going through. Even now, she was still mentally coming back down to Earth. Too much had happened to her.

But the mention of Sunwoo hurt her, it would hurt her every time. Sure, he was alive, but he was in a coma. There was always a chance of someone not waking up from a coma, and she knew he had been in a bad state. He'd been shot twice, and one was right in the chest.

Whether it had gotten his heart or not, that didn't mean it wasn't still fatal. She knew the chances of him really making it out of this was slim, they all knew it. But no one wanted to say it, at least not to her.

They didn't have to, though, because she knew it.

Everyone had kept talking about her story and what their next move should be. They went on about where they could possibly be hiding, and if there was anyway to find their address. Jaehyun was saying he could get access to the database that Sunwoo used to find people since they obviously had his name, and they could go from there.

She was hearing the words, but they were going in one ear and out the other. Jungkook and Jinhwan both notice the state she's in, as she had stared down at the floor of her apartment, beginning to tremble as the scene started to replay in her head like a broken record.

She should have done something. It was all her fault. 

She should've done something, anything.

She let him get shot. She was the reason he was dying.

The thoughts cut into her like knives. She feels Jungkook shifting in his seat, about to reach out to her in worry. But before he could touch her, she rises to her feet. Solar mutters something along the lines of that she needed some time alone, but no one seemed to pick up her words from how quiet they were.

Everyone only watched as she left the room, heading to her bedroom and closing the door behind her.

And as if on queue, Jinhwan and Jungkook rise to their feet.

Since Jinhwan was closer to the hallway from his seat, he was already further than Jungkook. Still, the taller boy hadn't given up as he stands right before Jinhwan, peering down at him with annoyance since he was blocking the way. 

"I'll check on her this time," Jinhwan vows, eyes already burning with disgust as he meets Jungkook's, "I need to talk to her, anyway."

Unconvinced, Jungkook crosses his arms over his chest as he retorts, "that doesn't mean she wants to talk to you, notice who she's been with the entire night. What Mi needs right now is comfort, and you probably aren't the one that'll give her that."

His words were harsh, but he meant every one of them. And everyone in the room couldn't help but understand where he was coming from with them. 

Jungkook was also worried he was going to find out about Jimin. After seeing how Soomi was doing, he knew she clearly was not ready to relay that news to him yet. He didn't need this idiot throwing anymore stress at her.

Jinhwan only narrows his eyes at him, unaware of the rather reasonable circumstances that Jungkook had for doing what he was doing. For once, it wasn't just about the competition, it wasn't about getting the girl. But Jinhwan didn't know that, and he'd only snarled out, "you need to be jumbled, Jungkook." Before spinning on his heel and heading towards her bedroom.

Jimin had appeared behind the youngest, putting a hand on his shoulder to stop him, because he knew well Jungkook was going to explode from that. "Kook, just let him go," he urges him, "he's not going to give up, just hang around and make sure nothing goes wrong, okay?"

Jungkook had only sighed in response.

Meanwhile, Solar had been sniffling and trying to contain herself as she held the phone against her ear. She was breathing heavily, pacing around her room, and trying to stop herself from crying so much. It was especially embarrassing thanks to who she was on the phone with, but she knew they understood.

"Solar, honey, we are on the fucking way!" Hyunjoon had yelled from somewhere in the background, "Thank God I bought that private jet, let's fucking go! We don't have fucking time to waste!"

"I knew it was a bad idea letting him go alone," Younghoon had sighed, "but we know he never listens to anyone but himself."

"Don't worry, Solar, we'll be there as soon as we can," Sangyeon assures her on the other line, being the one with the phone, "we have some things to handle here, but I promise we'll be there to help you with this. This guy isn't fucking getting away, not after this."

"Thank you guys," her voice is hoarse, barely a whisper as she sniffles again and again, "I-I just can't grasp that he really-"

"-Don't worry," he cuts her off, "this is fucking serious now. I know you're feeling a lot of remorse and hurt, but we need you to keep your head up for now, alright?"

She's about to say more but the sound of her bedroom door opening cuts her off. Figuring it was Jungkook, she doesn't turn to look at them as she breathes, "yeah, alright. I'll keep my head low. Hopefully you guys will be able to figure out where he is, I don't think he's going to be coming out of hiding anytime soon, but I can provide you as much information on him as I have."

"Good," Sangyeon responds, "just don't allow anyone to go to the police." He didn't have to elaborate more, because she was smart enough to know what would happen if they tried to go to the cops. 

"Yeah, I know," she sighs.

"Alright, Sol, see you soon then. Keep an eye on Sunwoo."

"Of course, Sangyeon, thank you guys."

The line is cut off shortly after that, and the good hand that was holding the phone falls back to her side. A deep exhale leaves her lips, and she's already voicing her worry to who she presumed was Jungkook, "Kookie, I really don't think I can..." she cuts herself off when she fully turns around to face the presence behind her, and only then did that scent of perfume sink into her nostrils.

Just the sight of Jinhwan made her breathe hitch in her throat. She kept telling herself she didn't want to see him, but a part of her did. She couldn't act like just a week ago after her party she wasn't spending the entire night with him as if nothing ever changed. Not to mention the fact she kissed him at the party. She knew that there was a lot that was fishy about the entire cheating incident, there had to be some lies in there somewhere, but she also knew she'd always feel for him in some part of her heart. 

Not knowing what to do because she was caught off guard from the fact he was here, her eyes trail away to anything that wasn't his face. The tension between them was immaculate, and Jinhwan was nervous, too. You could tell by the way he pursed his lips and the way his eyes ever so slightly quivered, he was hesitant, he knew something was off. Just when he thought he had fixed everything after that night, after the gift he worked so hard on, there she was, preferring Jungkook over him. 

He didn't get it, but he had a good idea what it had to do with. 

Hesitantly, he asks, "don't think you can do what?"

At the fact she was getting pulled into the last situation she needed today, the brunette heaves out an overdramatic, loud sigh. Stepping away from their proximity, she chunks her phone onto her mattress before falling back onto it, green eyes staring up at the ceiling with a blank expression on her face. Finally, she deadpans, "I don't want to talk about it, Nani."

Jinhwan couldn't hide how much that saddened him. Why would she talk to Jungkook and not him? Was what happened still bothering her that much? It sure didn't seem like it that night. 

He knew she had been through a lot and her mind was probably all sorts of mixed up, but there was no way there wasn't something bothering her that had to do with him. Unable to contain it anymore, he blurts out as he sits next to her, "what's going on, Soo?"

Solar couldn't help but feel bad from the way his voice fell. She hated to be the reason anyone close to her was hurting. 

Her eyes can't help but make their way to him, and she felt her heart fall out of her chest at the sight of his pained expression. "Too much," she mutters in response, not knowing what more she should say. Because it was too much. She didn't have the courage anymore to tell him about Jimin, she didn't want to start a fight, she didn't want to see him hurt anymore. But she didn't want to bring up the entire cheating incident, either, because it would be a hard conversation she wasn't prepared for right now, either.

"I'm sorry," he suddenly maintains, catching her off guard, "I know you're still thinking about it. I don't know why I thought that night would have fixed everything for us when I didn't talk about the main problem between us, you know, other than Jungkook."

Solar sits up straight, not knowing what to do since she realized there wouldn't be any avoiding this conversation after all. Jinhwan had already seemed to know.

"I know you said you forgave me, but it's clear it's still bothering you. And I don't blame you, what I did wasn't okay, and I was too focused on trying to make excuses instead of just admitting to my wrongs," he goes on, his eyes nervously shifting away from her, unable to look at her as he said the things he was saying, "so, no more excuses, Soo. I did do something wrong while you were in New York, and I did have the power to stop it. But I was so overcome by anger and resentment, I never did. All it would have taken was for me to be honest with everyone about what was going on, but I was too stupid to do that... and I'm sorry. I'm sorry for betraying your trust, and I'm sorry for ruining the best thing that ever happened to me."

It wasn't the first apology she had heard from Jinhwan.

But it was likely the most meaningful.

She could feel her chest aching when she gets the guts to look back to him. His eyes are glassy when they meet hers, and she didn't know what more to do in that moment than scoot over and pull him in for a hug. 

"Nani," she whispers into his neck where her face was buried, inhaling the familiar scent she would always love, "I love you," she mumbles, barely audible, "I'll always love you."

She had said it before not long ago, and it was true. There was no denying it, she would always love him, just like she loved Jungkook. It was about deciphering the different kinds of love, understanding what they meant, and which one was what she really needed in her life.

His arms wrap tightly around her waist, practically squeezing the life out of her. She hugged him as tightly as she could manage, too, but for a different reason. She knew it would be the last time - at least for a long time.

Because she had a feeling of what was coming, especially now that he had been completely honest with her. Jinhwan admitting to having had cheated on her multiple times with Jimin meant something more to Solar. It meant that there had to be some kind of emotion there, and it meant there had to be some kind of reaction when he'd find out. 

Sure, he could say that he loved her, that he was sorry, that he regretted it - but how would it change when he found out Jimin was dead?

"I love you too, Soo," he expresses right back.

And they sit there, holding each other like that for what felt like an hour. Though Solar hadn't said much at all to him, he knew what the hug meant. It was her forgiving him, her saying it was okay, which should have been a good thing to him.

Yet, that hug felt like she was saying goodbye.

They only pulled away due to their phones both going off in their back pockets. A knot forms in the bottom of her stomach as Jinhwan was already pulling out his phone, eyebrows knitting together as he reads what was on the screen, informing her, "it's an email from the company."

Oh no, her mind tells her, beads of sweat already beginning to form on her forehead. It had to be an update on all of the chaos, and that update would no doubt include the lives that were lost at the company, the bodies that were found dead, including the one she'd killed.

She hadn't even gotten to open the email herself yet when she could feel Jinhwan's gaze slowly fixating on her. Swallowing the lump in her throat, she dared herself to meet it. Would he instantly know it had to do with her? It wasn't like it said she did it.

But they both already knew it well.

And it was not going to be pretty.


154:00 | the list

His eyes were full of confusion and shock as they burned holes into her. Solar's hand is shaking from anxiety as she hurriedly opens the email on her own phone.

To all artists and personnel of YG Entertainment, 


                         After the robbery on a day that was meant to be an off day more most of our staff, it was risen to our attention that our company lacks the security needed to protect our workers and artists. To prevent this occurrence in the future, we are hiring full-time security guards and upgrading our security system to be on 24/7 unless personally changed by CEO Yangsuk. The building is undergoing repairs and renovations for these changes, and will be back in service by Wednesday, November 14. We expect all artists to resume schedules and attend the meeting that should now show on your calendars. 

Furthermore, a thorough police investigation on the culprit of this incident is being conducted. YGE is striving to end anymore violence. A ceremony will be held next week to mourn the staffs and civilians lives that were lost in this tragic event. They will never be forgotten as part of the YG family, and we encourage everyone to attend.

The email ended by listing the names of everyone who was found and considered to have been dead. Solar nearly choked on air when she read the list, seeing many names she recognized from being staff members she used to see all the time. It was horrible, she couldn't believe so many lives had been lost. Though a lot of them were actually working against her, she knew not all of them on the list could be involved.

She felt herself shaking as she read the last name - Shin Jimin.

It was true there were much bigger things she needed to be focused on right now. For one, the whereabouts of her manager and if he was going to try to come back to work. Though everyone had stated they didn't think he would have the guts to show his face and that hopefully the police would trace the crimes back to him, there was still a chance and it would obviously take time for them to trace him. She couldn't help but worry that he would be sitting there in her studio next time she went to the company.

How was she ever going to trust anyone from there again?

However, the matter at hand wasn't quite that, but the fact that Jinhwan was still burning holes into her. She finally looks up from her phone, daring to meet his unexpectedly harsh gaze. Opening her mouth to say something because she could already tell he knew, she didn't get to when he spouts out all-to-knowingly, "did that have something to do with you, Soo? Tell me the truth."

She knew it wasn't going to be good. There was an insistence in his tone, she could tell he wasn't happy, she could tell he was hurt. She guessed that being that they must have been close at some point in time, that since he cheated on her with her, there had to be a part of him that was going to be affected no matter how he felt for Solar. It was why she knew he was going to react badly, and now it was all unraveling and there was nothing she could do about it.

A hopeless sigh leaves her lips as she diverts her eyes, knowing he would fail to have any logic, "she was trying to stab me, Jinhwan." When his expression doesn't look that convinced, she raises her voice, tainted with bewilderment, "she was waiting for a cue to kill me!"

"That's not... she wasn't that kind of person. Sure, she was crazy, psychotic, persistent... but she wouldn't have killed someone," he's shaking his head in denial, "t-they must of threatened her, forced her against her will..." It was like he was struggling to grasp it all, the concept of someone being gone, the concept of someone being someone other than who you thought they were. Solar understood it because she'd been there, that was all she had been going through.

But her losing Sunwoo was different in more ways than she could count. She couldn't help but be frustrated from his lack of understanding as she only peers at him with disgust, "she sure didn't seem like she felt threatened or forced when she was talking about how happy she was going to be watching me die."

Her words still weren't getting through to him. At that point, she might as well have been talking to a brick wall. 

The caramel boy that was hugging her and telling her how sorry he was only minutes ago was now closing his eyes and exhaling deeply to hold in all the mixed emotions he was feeling. Jinhwan couldn't really think rationally at that point, he'd seen too much, he'd heard too much. Murder wasn't supposed to be this casual to anyone, especially not him or the girl he loved. 

"That doesn't change the fact," he breathes, his voice so low it was barely audible, "that you killed someone, Soomi. You killed her, do you realize that? You took another human's life." But death, murder, that was all normal in her eyes, wasn't it? Look at the way she grew up.

Meanwhile, a rather pressed Jungkook had been standing in between the hall and the living room, tapping his foot rapidly on the ground as he impatiently paced back and forth every few seconds. He hadn't heard anything like yelling or something, meaning it must be okay, right? He still wanted to just barge in there anyway, just to make sure he wasn't trying to murder Solar or something. Jungkook had no idea how he was going to react, but he figured it would be ugly.

As for everyone else, Jimin had retired from his watching Jungkook duty and was sitting back on the sofa next to Jihoon. The poor boy was starting to feel more comfortable around everyone since they were all so nice to him. He wished that he was there with so many of his idols under better circumstances, maybe then he would have a more carefree attitude, maybe then he would only be focused on how he was going to brag to his members later that he was now friends with some of the biggest idols in all of Korea. 

Sadly, that wasn't the case at all. He didn't regret meeting Soomi, and he didn't regret checking on her and getting involved in all of it, because he wanted to help. Though it was too much for him to process, as in he couldn't seem to fully comprehend how much was real - or more like he didn't want to - but he felt bad hearing about everything she had gone through. Especially knowing someone she had trusted did it to her, it was awful. She clearly needed some more trustworthy people in her life to look out for her where others couldn't, and he was willing to be that.

And maybe he also just wanted to have the bragging rights.

"I know it's a lot," Jimin had been saying, crossing one leg over the other and relaxing into the sofa, "you never realize how dark the world is until you witness it with your own eyes."

The sight of Soomi running into Jihoon, breathing unsteadily, a horrified look on her face with blood dripping down her arm onto the ground flashed in his head. Just seeing it again made him realize how right that statement was. 

As for the others in the room, they'd been brainstorming and trying to make full sense of the situation. Jihoon and the iKON members had also received the email, and explained what it said to Jaehyun and everyone else. From there, they'd spoken about the fact it was unlikely for her manager to try to return because of how risky it was for him. He was no doubt going to be in hiding somewhere, planning his next move from a distance.

"That or he's going to try to hire more people under him," Jaehyun had voiced, rubbing his temples as a stressed sigh escapes his lips, "we'll definitely need some backup for all of this. You know, people a little more equipped for this kind of thing, it's gone way too far."

None of them were offended by his words. In fact, everyone nods in agreement. Even Chanhee agreed despite how much he hated the thought of having to spend more time around the infidels that those gang members were.

"Mi just called them, I think," Jungkook announces from his stance by the hallway, "I overheard."

A breathe of relief leaves the oldest brother's lips, "that's great, they'll know where to go next while we wait for Sunwoo to recover."

But an uneasy silence falls over the room.

Everyone was thinking the same thing. And since Solar wasn't there, Chaerin had demurred, "do you really think he's going to? He got a severe head concussion and a shot to the chest. Who the hell survives something like that?" The way she said it was harsh, only because that was how she was, and she didn't care to be sensitive since Solar wasn't there. No one was really emotionally affected by Sunwoo, not like she was to the point they were going to cry about it. They just knew how badly they needed him for this.

"Well, they did say none of his organs were damaged badly," Chanhee recalls, "not that I'm thrilled either way, but they did say he was just in a coma due to the medicine, no?"

Kevin had gently slapped Hee's arm from his unnecessarily added comment, "hey, give Sunwoo some respect, his life is literally on the line."

The pink haired boy only sends him a face of absolute petrifiable disgust, "Sure, okay, maybe if he wasn't a pretentious, self-centered, cruel, mentally-psychotic, narcissistic, braindead, smart-ass, fuck-"

"-Alright, I think we get the memo."

Chanhee only heaves out an annoyed sigh, before he looks to Jaehyun for an answer to his previous question since he was the primary one who had conversed with the doctor. As soon as Hee's eyes land on his face, he noticed the look on his face and realized that he might not have told the full truth.

Everyone was afraid to know what that truth might be, and how bad it would be for them and especially poor Solar. 

She was already going through enough as it was.

Jinhwan was standing now, feet away from her stance on her mattress. His fists were clenched at his sides as he had tried to steady his breathing from how many emotions were jumbling inside of him. He couldn't grasp the reality of the situation, he was too focused on his feelings, and that was clear.

But the way he was treating her, the things he was saying to her, it hurt. Even if she knew he didn't mean them, even if she knew it was just because he wasn't fully there right now. She had already been so hesitant about where they stood, but this - this was going to make it clear.

"She's dead, Soomi," he deadpans for what was probably the 50th time in the last thirty minutes, "do you not feel anything? Are you that numb to things like this? You don't feel remorse, regret, guilt?!" he turns to her, eyes scanning all over her face in search for anything he just mentioned.

Solar only peers back up at him from where she sat, her green eyes blink a few times in contemplation. Was there anything right she could say here? Should she try to apologize and act like she felt bad?

No, because that would be a lie. She didn't feel bad for defending herself against a psychopath. And she especially wasn't going to because of the fact it was the girl she was cheated on with.

She couldn't believe he expected her to get on her knees and beg for forgiveness. It annoyed her, despite how hard she was trying to just be understanding. She was going through a lot, too, and he was failing to realize the genuine issues at hand. 

"Wow..." he gawks when she didn't say anything right away.

About to lose her mind, Solar rises to her feet, speaking just as he was opening his mouth again, "No, Jinhwan, the answer is no. I do not feel bad for winning against a literal psychopath that was going to send a knife through my heart."

The way she said it was like venom flying from her tongue. There was a fire in her eyes when she spit those words out, and it was a way he had never seen her before. A way that made him resent her even more.

"You're sicker than I thought you were, Soomi," he shakes his head, a ting of disappointment and disgust in his words, "but what do I expect? Look at your reputation, taking the lives of others is normalized to you. No wonder you liked Sunwoo so much, you're one in the same."

His words were harsher than she had ever heard him speak. Never did she imagine Kim Jinhwan would say something like that to her. The boy she always perceived as a perfect angel, the one who was always so understanding, the one she chose over Jungkook. The boy she swore once was meant for her, the one she always felt so softly for. Only around an hour ago was she telling him how she would always love him, and she meant it.

Which was why hearing him hiss those words at her was like a shot in the heart.

Solar spins around, facing away from him so he didn't see the tears welling in her eyes. Closing her eyes so they don't escape, she exhales to catch her breathe as she shakily sputters out, "Good to know you'd rather me have died, Nani. Don't act like you didn't do the same, don't act like you're so above me and Sunwoo, at least we do the things we do with reason and honesty, can you really say the same, or would you just be spinning more lies?"

If Jungkook hadn't burst through the door, there's no telling what would have happened. Solar knew it was him, her gut told her so, and she immediately turned towards the boy who had stopped in the doorway. His doe eyes were flaming when they looked to Jinhwan, who was now just standing there in awe and bewilderment. It was likely he was coming back down to his senses, but what was done was done. There was no taking back what had happened.

As much as Jungkook wanted to beat the life out of him until he was a bloody mess on the carpet, he was actually able to think rationally and realize that would be the last helpful thing anyone needed. Instead, he rushes towards Solar, engulfing her in a tight hug. As soon as he was in front of her, the brunette explodes into throaty sobs, wrapping her trembling arms around him and burying her face in his t-shirt.

Jinhwan stood there in shock, trying to understand what just happened, trying to understand what he just said. 

"You have ten seconds to get the fuck out of here," Jungkook had turned his head to chastise, "or you'll be added to that list." His voice was raspy, basically a growl as he had narrowed his eyes at the much shorter boy. 

Thinking back to the fact he had a loaded gun, Jinhwan knew he wasn't going to take the chance. He knew it wasn't his place to anymore.

He realized it as he spun on his heel, breathing rapidly as if he just ran a marathon, speed walking out of the bedroom and towards the door.

He realized he ruined everything - for good.


155:00 | reassurance

"What the FUCK happened?!" Jaehyun is booming, his eyes burning red and his large silhouette heaving up and down with fury. Fists clenched by his sides, he was having an extremely hard time holding in his anger despite how he was trying to be the rational one for everyone. But he knew damn well what had to of gone down when he saw Jinhwan rushing out of the room and out the apartment itself like a track runner. Everyone had heard Solar's loud, agonizing sobs and ran to her bedroom to see Jungkook trying to calm her down. 

But she was massively unstable all over again. Knowing that short piece of garbage had caused her to breakdown again was Jaehyun's last straw. "God, I am going to fucking kill that motherfucker..."

"What is that seriously going to do?!" Chaerin snaps him out of his angry daze, snapping her fingers in his face, "at least wait until after Solar isn't having a mental breakdown!" When he still looked like he was going to act on his urge of chasing the boy down, the younger sister grabs onto the sleeve of his leather jacket, jerking him with any strength she had as she warns him, "Hey, dumb ass, listen to me. Solar fucking needs us."

The eldest brother shakes his head, a grunt leaving his lips as he turns to face his poor sister. Solar was a mess, she'd fallen to her knees on the carpeted floor of her bedroom, burying her face in her hands as she was trembling with tears. Her sobs were so loud it was likely any neighbors were filled with concern, and she sat there, shaking like she was losing her mind. There was a permanent horrified glint in her green irises, as if she was watching the events she'd experience unfold on repeat. Because she was, it was all playing on repeat. Everything she had ever experienced, everything she had ever done wrong.

Jinhwan's words cut through her like silk. All of the defenses she had managed to build up, as strong as she had been trying to be through all of it, they'd been destroyed. She was destroyed.

"I can't..." she's screaming in exasperation, coughing on her own sobs as her hands grip at her hair, pulling at it in pain, "I can't fucking handle it anymore... I fucking can't."

"Hey, people, give her some space!" Chanhee is urging everyone, practically shoving them out of her bedroom. They figured that they should probably listen to him since he was her closest friend. Eventually, everyone had poured out back into the living room, all except for Jungkook, the siblings, her other childhood friends, Junhoe, and Jimin.

Jungkook was kneeled on the floor next to her, his hands on her shoulders as he gave her a massage, thinking it might calm her down. He was whispering calming words to her, anything that he thought might help. He might of been the angriest one in the room, even more so than her brother, but he was doing his best to not show it - what was important right now was helping her out of this. He couldn't fucking fathom that Jinhwan had the nerve to say what he did to her, to even start anything after what had happened. How did he have the fucking audacity? The fact he got mad over the girl he cheated on her with dying, it made him sick to his stomach. None of it was Solar's fault, and she didn't deserve to be feeling this way. She'd been through so much, and he just wanted to make it better.

Afterwards, he would kill Jinhwan.

Only afterwards.

Jungkook had already pulled everyone aside to explain what he heard happen. All of them were just as upset, some more so than others. The boys of iKON were just bewildered, they couldn't understand why their eldest who had always cared about Soomi so dearly would do something like that to her. It was disappointing.

Junhoe had been standing there in the middle of the room, only able to watch as Chanhee and Jungkook were comforting her. He didn't want to interrupt, and he had never seen Soomi like this to know how to comfort her. It wasn't like he even knew how to comfort anyone, period. He was like a deer in headlights, just trying to understand what was wrong with his hyung. All he could do was think back to everything, how he found Jinhwan with Jimin, how he had that gut feeling that something was wrong. He didn't think that Jinhwan really liked Jimin, he thought it all just was out of spite, that was what Jinhwan said, right?

So why would he be so upset? 

He excuses himself out of the bedroom, making his way into the living area where Jihoon, the rest of the Bangtan members, and the rest of his own members were sitting. Hanbin had already left to chase Jinhwan and try to figure out what was going on, and Junhoe was thinking he needed to go join in on that conversation. 

"I don't understand why he would act like that towards her," Donghyuk is faltering, shaking his head as he frowns, "hyung really loves Soomi, so what was that all about?"

Junhoe only scoffs as he falls onto the sofa beside them, "does he really love her? That's not how you treat someone you love."

Yunhyeong gives him a look of disapproval, but he didn't care. 

"Hey, he's been through a lot the last 24 hours," Namjoon had spoken up from where they were all sitting across from them, "he seemed like he realized his mistake, right?"

The members of iKON only sigh. He had a point, but was it really an excuse?

"It's still so unlike him..." Donghyuk's voice trailed off.

"So is what happened," Bobby defends, speaking of the incident they would never forget - as in when Jinhwan killed someone.

"He's shaken up," Yunhyeong sternly avows, "Jinan-hyung was already unstable, alright? He and Soomi were in a questionable state to begin with, he was like a bomb waiting to go off... and no doubt he's already realized what he's done."

But it still didn't explain his emotions for Jimin.

"Solar," Chanhee had been advising the brunette, who was still in shambles on her bedroom floor. Jungkook was still by her side, his arms on her shoulders as he looks to her best friend in concern as to what he was going to tell her. "Think about Sunwoo. Do you think he would want you to give up this easily? Are you going to give up this easily after how hard he's tried?"

Her sobs were slowly becoming less and less as time went on. That being said, it had been around an hour or more of her hyperventilating and sobbing. Her head was beginning to hurt by then, and she felt as if her entire world was spinning. Solar didn't even feel like she was fully there, her entire body just wanted to give up. Everything had felt so pointless. She felt like no matter how hard she tried, it would never get better. The whole world was always against her. How was she supposed to ever win?

Especially when she kept losing everyone that was close to her.

"Don't let it all be in vain, Sol," Jacob says from where he's sitting on her mattress with Kevin and Changmin.

"We're all right here with you," Kevin pipes up from beside him, "you didn't lose anyone. Sunwoo's going to be back, and Jinhwan's going to be begging at your feet in a few days."

Sunwoo, Jinhwan, their names were stabs to her heart. No matter what they said, she couldn't believe them. She couldn't believe Sunwoo was back until he was standing there right in front of her with that stupid smirk on his face and a cigarette hanging out of his mouth, with that all-too-familiar but pleasant scent of cologne and smoke radiating off him. She never thought she would be able to say she would do anything to go back to that night in New York at Cane's, or when he was sleeping right by her side after she got shot to make sure nothing else happened - or checking her bedroom for bugs every single day just in case they were to have managed to plant another, even though it was next to impossible. 

All the things he did for her, how was she ever going to repay him if he lost his life over them?

And Jinhwan... the boy she thought was the most innocent of them all had let her down the most. Not just with the cheating, but with the words he'd said. Jungkook had done her wrong before, too, but never in his life would he utter those words to her. Neither would Sunwoo - neither of them had or would ever say something like that to her. Despite this, Jinhwan was the last person she ever thought she would hear it from.

"I'm never leaving you, Mi," Jungkook had whispered, laying his head on her shoulder as he stares at the ground, "I love you, I'm sorry for all of this."

Solar wasn't crying anymore at that point. Her green eyes were as puffy as bowling balls, her body was still shaky, and she couldn't imagine even trying to get up from her stance on the floor. Her hair was a mop above her head from how much she had pulled and yanked at it, and she wasn't sure if she could talk from all the uncontrollable screaming she had done about how she was a terrible person and had lost everyone. 

Clearly overreacting, but that was what happened when you had a PTSD breakdown.

"I'm okay..." she manages to say, her voice sounding like a train wreck, "thank you guys..."

Hearing how hoarse her voice was, Chanhee turns to one of the friends, "get her some water," he commanded, before looking back to the brunette again, "does your head hurt?" he asked even though he already knew the answer. Holding a hand to her forehead for a brief second, he turns back to Jacob, who was already standing up to go do what he said, adding, "and Tylenol."

Solar didn't say anything as Jacob left the room. She only sniffled, feeling stiff as she scooted towards Jungkook so she could lay on his chest. He reacted immediately at her presence, wrapping an arm around her shoulders and snuggling his face into her hair. 

"Do you guys want me to order takeout?" Jimin had asked, sitting on the floor a few feet away from them, "We can all watch Gossip Girl to take your mind off things."

"That would be good," Chaerin had spoken up, "I am fucking starving, and Sol definitely needs some food in her system - plus, food always cheers her up."

"And Gossip Girl?!" Kevin is cheering, "Who can say no to Gossip Girl?!"

It was like a euphoria when the girl lets out a small chuckle. The sight of her briefly smiling was thrilling to Jungkook, as he squeezed her gently from excitement. "I'd love to watch Gossip Girl."

Jimin had nodded to everyone's positive response, taking out his phone and hurriedly working to call Solar's favorite takeout place. He rose to his feet, excusing himself out of the room so he could order. 

"I'm gonna go talk to everyone," Jaehyun announces as he gets up from the sofa he and Chaerin had been sitting on, "we all need to keep in close touch, but it'll be good to lay off for a couple of days while we wait for Sunwoo's gang to get here. We all need rest, and we can figure out the rest of this when Solar's feeling better," he exhales deeply at the end of his words, eyes dull as they fall on his sister. 

"Don't let too many of them stay," Chanhee had called to him as he was walking out, "I do not want to sleep on the couch."



+



So there you'd find them, sitting around Solar's - weirdly large - living room, all crowded around the flatscreen television to watch her favorite show and eat takeout. The sofas had been moved so that they were all together in a line, with the rest sitting on the floor that didn't fit. Though it wasn't like all the friends had stayed, since there was nowhere near enough room in her apartment to house that many people. The other Bangtan members had gone home, saying that they would be back to check in on her. As for the other iKON boys, they went to deal with Jinhwan. Hanbin had tried to come back, saying he wanted to explain some of what he had gathered from talking to the eldest, but her siblings had rushed him out, saying that Solar wouldn't be able to handle it right now. They weren't far off, though, because she likely wouldn't be able to handle anything from Jinhwan. After what happened, she was going to need a while to heal from that - if ever. She wasn't sure if she would ever be able to freely let him back in her life.

Which was insane to come from her, it was insane for her to even think. She always tried to forgive people for their mistakes because she knew she was guilty of making them, too. Mistakes were part of being human. But it was about the intention behind those mistakes, the motive, the thought process. She knew Jinhwan was shaken up, she knew he was confused, she knew he was going through a lot - but so was she. And he'd taken it all out on her, letting the truth come out. 

And the truth hurt.

So maybe Jinhwan wasn't the perfect guy she always envisioned him as. The one who was always understanding, the one who she swore would do no wrong. The thing was, Solar hadn't known him for that long. They only met around a year and a half ago, and a good part of that was during her alcohol addiction - the one he saved her from. He'd always shown her such care and concern, and saving her from such an addiction, it made her envision him as something so great - she practically idolized him. He was the sweet, romantic, empathetic boy she thought only existed in movies - or at least she thought.

But it wasn't impossible for him to have put on a façade during those times. As much time as they spent together over the course of that year and a half, was it enough for her to know him like the back of her hand - really know him? She'd known Jungkook for seven years. They met when she was only a mere eighteen, they'd met before they were famous, before they could even fathom anything like this happening. They were practically still children at that time. Solar and Jungkook, in a sense, had watched each other grow into adults - they'd grown together. She could say she knew Jungkook like the back of her hand, even after what happened with the scandal, because she'd known him for that long. After they talked about what happened and she learned the truth, the two had only grown closer than they had once been. 

She thought she knew Jinhwan just as well despite their much shorter time together. But after that she was beginning to doubt everything she once swore she knew.

Still, Solar couldn't erase all their memories together. As hard as she was trying to just focus on the TV screen, feeling Jungkook's chest heave up and down under her, all she could see was them, replaying in her head in a string.

It made her realize. As much as she might feel now that she didn't know him, he knew her, better than most people - maybe even better than Jungkook. That was the thing with Jinhwan, she had chosen him over Jungkook countless amounts of times. All the way from when she first stayed at Jungkook's house and decided to go to his dorm instead, to when she officially told him she wanted to be with him at KCON, to the fact she chose him instead of Kookie to meet her parents and reunite with her family for the first time since she was kicked out. Her choice had always ended with him. He was the one who met her parents, he was the one who stayed with her the whole time at her old house, and as much as she didn't want to admit it - he was there 8 times out of 10 when she needed him. He really had been the definition of everything she had ever wanted, and she realized he - and everything - only changed when the entire Jimin incident started.

Remembering all those moments they'd had before it all went downhill made her feel sick to her stomach. Was it possible for all of that to be a facade? It couldn't be, she had always adored him for a reason. She meant it when she told him she would always love him.

Maybe I really should talk to him, a part of her suggests, but she hurriedly shook it off as the words he'd so hatefully shot at her repeats in her head. She recalled the way his eyes were burning with an emotion she had never seen in them before directed at her - resentment, disgust, execration. So many things she didn't think he could ever feel towards her.

As the memories of everything they once were fade to the back of her head, she begins to wonder then if that was simply just because her and Jinhwan - as much as they swore it would never be possible - feelings had changed. 

Because as sad it was, and as much as they had always told each other they still loved one another, it was possible - feelings changed. It was possible they had both done so without noticing, and maybe they needed an extra push or occurrence to realize it. 

Or maybe she was just making that up as an excuse. Maybe they were really just sweeping it all under the blanket.

"Mi?"

Her head jerks up to the inquisitive voice of Jungkook, who's arm was still securely around her waist. He had been paying pretty good attention to the show - even though he had indeed seen many of it before thanks to her - but he couldn't help but notice that something was on her mind. From the way she kept diverting her gaze to her hands that lay in her lap, fiddling with her fingers, before letting out a low exhale every now and then. There was definitely something bothering her, and it didn't take a genius to figure out what it might be.

When her green irises peer back into his, he sends her a small reassuring smile to ease her nerves that he might ask something she couldn't currently answer. "Are you feeling tired now?" he hums instead, reaching up a hand to brush a piece of hair out of her eyes, "It would be good for you to get some rest, we can all clean up."

Not saying anything, her eyes instead rake over the mess of takeout that was already scattered around her apartment from everyone. The thought of just sleeping forever sounded rather appealing to her as she finally shrugs in response, "that would probably be a good idea for me."

Jungkook nods in agreement, rising to his own feet first and holding out a hand to her. He was beaming down at her with that smile of his as she took it, following his actions and jumping to her feet. As she did so, her entire body felt weak, everything felt weak. 

Her vision was a bit blurry for a second before it adjusted again, and she offered to everyone, who were still focused on the TV, "I'm going to bed now, guys. I'll see you in the morning."

"Finally, she sleeps," Chaerin was the first to say in her typical tone, but you could tell she was more than glad her big sister was getting the rest she needed, "goodnight, big sister."

"Goodnight, Mi!" Jimin had called, "if you wake up and need anyone, I'll be here!" he added, that reassuring angelic grin on his lips. 

"Me too!" Junhoe pipes in from beside him, "but I have absolutely no therapeutic charms, so he might be the better option."

The two of her best friends seemed to be forgetting about a certain someone in the room, though. Chanhee narrows his eyes at them, clearly unamused, before he scoffs, loud enough to bring any and all of the attention to him, "excuse me, I should obviously be your go-to, but of course I'm sure you'd put even that over me," he rolls his eyes, motioning a hand to Junhoe. 

Solar heaves out a sigh, figuring that his whole "giving her a break because she was crippled" statement had expired. It was likely obvious that the first person she would be talking to if she were to wake up during her slumber in case of any issue was Jungkook, who very well was aware of this, but even he was enjoying the current moment too much to bother to say anything. Instead, there was a humorous smile on his face as he only watched it play out, hand still holding onto hers.

A rather confused, but not offended, Junhoe had looked from the pink haired boy, then to Jimin, then to Solar. He did this a few times, seeming to process what he just heard. Junhoe would have probably insulted literally anyone else, but he knew from Solar that Chanhee was pretty serious about what he was saying. "I don't know if I should act offended or say thank you," he blankly shares.

"well, he did just call you a thing," Changmin informs, "so you might want to start there."

"Alrighty, get some sleep, Sol!" Jaehyun cuts everyone off just as Chanhee was opening his mouth again due to the fact he knew that he would go on for literally ever about this subject. "We'll figure out the sleeping situations."

Chanhee was still muttering to Jacob, Changmin, and Kevin about how offended he apparently was as Solar had only nodded, rubbing her eyes with her good hand that was no longer holding Jungkook's. Spinning on her heel, she weakly made her way down the hall to her bedroom with the brunette boy following her closely behind.

It was clear he had no intent of leaving her side - probably ever. 


156:00 | guilt

"Huh..." Junhoe had been muttering to himself, sitting at the dinner table in the kitchen, eyes full of slight surprise and even confusion as he was peering down at his phone screen. 

It had been a good few days. There was only one day until every YGE artist was expected to be back at the "improved" company to continue their schedules. There was still countless news articles about the company and how it was reinventing itself that they'd kept seeing everywhere. 

Solar had mainly kept away from her email and her phone in general. She had been pretty good about keeping her social media updated and checking her email in case anyone ever needed anything, but she had strictly decided to stay away from it for the sake of her health. This meant she hadn't seen any of the emails that YGE had sent out, and neither had Junhoe, until now. 

Junhoe, Jimin, her siblings, childhood friends, and Jungkook had all stayed at her apartment ever since the incident. Jungkook still hadn't left her side, he might as well had been chained to her. He even went with Solar every single day to go see Sunwoo, though he made sure to give her some space when she would sit with him. He knew it was important for her to have alone time with the boy. Jaehyun and Juyeon had been taking turns spending the night and supervising him, also keeping everyone else updated in case anything with his health changed.

But nothing had, as far as Solar knew. He was supposed to be in good condition, and only out for a few days because of the medicine they used during his surgery.

So he should be awake soon, right?

She had to tell herself that, if she didn't, she knew she would fall apart. It was hard enough after what happened with Jinhwan.

To say the least, her mental health hadn't been the best. She'd have a PTSD attack every now and then, which would range from absolutely awful to mediocre. Sometimes she'd wake up sobbing without even knowing it, which would result in a worried Jungkook beside her. He'd hold her for hours until she was able to sleep again, promising her everything would be okay and that they'd find her manager that was in hiding.

Which was the next obstacle, finding him before something happened - but she wasn't sure how easy that would be. I mean, he had been so sneaky all this time, right?! 

Or was that just because they were too dumb to have found him already?

Either way, slowly but surely, Solar's health had began to improve. Jaehyun was promising her that Sunwoo would be awake and back to being himself in no time, Hee was always making sure she wasn't skipping meals, Jimin had been practically giving her free therapy, and Junhoe - Junhoe was just Junhoe. 

The other Bangtan members also tended to stop by fairly often. Even Yoongi had stopped by on his own to check on her and show her a new song he had just finished. Getting to work on music again with him had been a pleasant and therapeutic moment for her that she didn't know she needed.

So, to say the least, she could finally say she was feeling better. But was it better enough to show up for work? Was it better enough to be releasing her new EP soon?

Probably not.

But it wasn't like she had a choice.

"What is it?" she finally hums, eyes diverting to Junhoe as she pops another grape in her mouth. Leaning back into the booth seat that was on only one side of the dinner table, she looks to her best friend in curiosity. 

For once, it was actually only the two of them. Last she checked, Jungkook was in the shower, and everyone else was in the living room. Chaerin, Juyeon, Jacob - and a very insistent Chanhee who swore they wouldn't buy the healthy options - had gone out for groceries. 

Having had still been staring at his phone like he was blind, Junhoe's eyes jerk up to her. He debated for a second in his head, before he decided to inform her, "Looks like the case on our old manager has gone cold, says they couldn't find any hard evidence to lead to a suspect."

Her eyes widen from the news. Perking up in her seat, Junhoe holds the phone in front of her face so she could read the email. Sure enough, there it was, right there.

On behalf of YGE, we regret to inform everyone that the case regarding our very own beloved staff member... has gone cold... the authorities could not find enough hard evidence to instigate the correct suspect... we are terribly sorry.

She skimmed over the whole email, before nodding to show she was done. Swallowing another grape, she exhales a breathe of relief, "well, I guess that's good news." 

But he had more to show her. A snort leaves his mouth as he hums, "yeah, well, there's more."

She didn't appear surprised. "Course."

"It won't really affect you, though," he goes on, eyes focused on his phone screen again, "apparently we're officially releasing our new mini album on Friday."

Upon the words leaving his lips, Solar's brows knit together in confusion. She contemplated for a bit, recalling the album that Hanbin had been working on for quite a while. Jinhwan had also probably wrote songs for it, as he said he was trying to get into songwriting.

But she refused to think about that.

"Did you guys already learn the choreo?" she's asking blankly, "that's on such a short notice, but you know, I guess it did get postponed."

Junhoe shrugs, "I'd say we know it pretty well, but if we don't, we have exactly three days to practice starting Wednesday."

"Well, good luck with that, loser," she wrinkles her nose at him, "I still have another week before  my schedule is absolutely painful."

"Fuck you, you're going to be the reason we don't win at any music shows."

"Do better."

Junhoe fakes a gasp, causing the brunette to laugh at him. He smiles ear to ear, happy to see his best friend in such a good mood. He was just worried how long it would last. Seeing her so destroyed was something he wasn't trying to see - ever again.

Though he knew that it would likely happen whenever she saw Jinhwan. He'd talked to him, and so had everyone else, but that didn't change what happened. He didn't know if anything could.






+





"Yah, Jinan-hyung!" Hanbin yells, jogging to try to catch up the short boy that was practically racing to his vehicle. When he gets no response, and Jinhwan doesn't even bother to look back at him, the leader yells again, but louder, "Jinhwan-hyung, we need to talk about this!"

Still, he didn't halt his movements. Realizing that he had no intention of actually listening to him, Hanbin speeds up to a run. Just as Jinhwan was unlocking his car in a rush, reaching for the handle hectically, Hanbin reaches the vehicle, stepping in between the boy and his car and leaning against the drivers door to prevent him from getting inside.

A look of defeat washes over Jinhwan's expression. Hanbin opens his mouth to speak, but as soon as his eyes rake over the eldest's, his mouth slams shut as if someone had forced it. He tried not to show his shock as he sees the state he was in. Not because he didn't think he was emotional over what happened, but because he'd never seen him like this before.

Not this destroyed. Jinhwan looked like a train wreck, with puffy cheeks from how much he'd already cried. His eyes were red enough to look like blood when they meet Hanbin's gaze with annoyance, "Hanbin-ah, move out of the way." He was trying to sound harsh, but his voice was weak. So weak that it cracked at the end, and he'd hurriedly sniffled in anger, using the back of his sweater sleeve to wipe his eyes. 

"Hyung, you really can't run away from this," he finally musters the courage to tell him, "we both know you didn't mean any of that."

A sob tried to leave his lips, but he covered it by coughing. Clearing his throat, Jinhwan furiously shakes his head, so hard his long caramel hair falls in his face. He looks to his feet, embarrassed at the fact he was here crying in front of someone. "I can't do that, I ruined my one and only chance at fixing us," he sniveled, "we just made up, she just told me she would always love me, and then I saw the email and it was like I just - I just lost it. I thought I fixed everything just to ruin it all again..." his voice falters, fading to a whisper, "now I've lost her for good."

Hanbin didn't know what to say. He didn't witness the incident first hand, he didn't know exactly what had been said. What he did know, was that both him and Soomi had been through way too much in the last 24 hours.

"You've both been through a lot in the last day, hyung," he tells him, "you're both shaken up and emotional, and people tend to say things they don't mean in those states. What you both experienced last night was traumatizing, and that takes time to recover from. Don't act like this isn't the first time you've fought. You weren't yourself, and Soomi wasn't either." Jinhwan had only been sniffling as he spoke these words, before Hanbin steps forward to pull his poor friend into a hug, "she'll come to her senses, and so will you. She'll understand that you were going through a lot, she knows you didn't mean it."

There was a lot of truth in his words. Hanbin always spoke knowingly, and what he told him was reassuring - if only there weren't other factors involved.

"Yeah, not if Jungkook has anything to do with it," he denies, a pitiful scoff leaving his lips, "I don't know if she'll ever really be able to forgive me for what I said... I can't even forgive myself."

"It's Soomi," he reminds him as if he had forgotten as they pull out of the hug, "she loves you, but it'll take time. You just have to give her time."

Jinhwan sighs, give her time. They were already on a break for so long, he had barely got to spend any time with her in what felt like forever. 

Except for that night. He wished he could go back, just to remember what it felt like to hold her there.

The sound of footsteps coming towards them caused him to look up from his feet. He wasn't crying anymore, but you could tell he had been. Pushing his hair out of his eyes to see clearly, the wind had blown harshly against them as a frantic Junhoe stops before them, eyes scanning the situation.

Just from the sight of his hyung, Junhoe had a feeling that he was already feeling guilty. As utterly tempting as it was to make fun of Jinhwan crying, he knew it was probably the absolute worst time.

But he was still Junhoe, and everyone knew Junhoe had no filter.

"You look like shit."

A disapproving sigh leaves Hanbin's lips, and Jinhwan was only glaring at him as if he wished he would fall through the ground into hell. "Is that all you came out here for, really, June-ya?" he scowled in disappointment. 

Starting to feel a little bad, Junhoe hurriedly vows, "okay, no, I actually came to help you find your common decency - but I guess you already did that."

"He knows what he did, June-ya," Hanbin is telling him in a scolding manner, "Jinan-hyung feels like garbage, which you can clearly see, there's no need to make it worse on him."

Junhoe didn't get to say anything to that, because Jinhwan had blurted, "is she okay?"

At the sudden question, the boys face falls. Jinhwan could feel his emotions acting up again as he remembers how he had pushed her into another breakdown, it was all him. As if she wasn't having a hard enough time, he had pushed her off the edge. The way she'd broken into sobs so loud it echoed through the room as she'd practically fallen into Jungkook, it was like she was in so much pain. And he had caused it.

Seeming to think hard about his words, Junhoe finally sputters, "she's - uh, pretty bad. But she'll be okay, I wouldn't worry about her, really."

It was his attempt at trying to be helpful and reassuring. It only annoyed Jinhwan as he shakes his head, "of course I'm going to worry about her, it's all my fucking fault."

"Look, hyung, I know you've gone through a lot," he finally deadpans, "but what the hell fueled that? How mad could you have been to say something like that to her?" He had been trying to hold it back, but Junhoe had to know answers - and he needed them now - anything to make sense of what the hell was wrong with Jinhwan. He couldn't just use the excuse that he had been through a lot, that he just didn't like the idea of someone dying, that was bullshit. 

If he really had feelings for Jimin, his literal psychotic ex girlfriend, then Junhoe wanted to hear those words from his mouth.

Jinhwan looks to be in a trance from his questions. Since he was slowly coming back to his senses, he thought about them hard. His eyes wander everywhere that wasn't on Hanbin or Junhoe, trying to understand himself and how to form it into words. 

Until he finally spills what he knew they really wanted to know, "it's not that I had feelings for Jimin, okay? But I can't act like she wasn't a part of my life before. It's hard to grasp that someone you used to be so close to is just gone, that they lost their life in such a way. It's even harder to understand that the love of your life killed them," he paused to inhale and exhale, trying to contain his emotion and not start bawling again. "Soomi is my whole world, but she's habituated to things I'm not. I know she was protecting herself, and I know that Jimin was involved with her manager plotting all of it. I'm not blaming her, either. It was just..." he pauses again, shaking his head in frustration that he couldn't seem to understand himself, "it was too much for me to take in with everything. Jimin used to be special to me, you know?"

Junhoe wanted to make a snarky comment about how he knew that for sure since he'd even cheated on Soomi with her. But for once, he stopped himself with that one.

"Soomi's special to you now, don't forget that," he says instead, "I think she'll forgive you with time, but you definitely need to get yourself together first."

Jinhwan only purses his lips, nodding in agreement as he mutters, "yeah, yeah, I'll do that, June-ya."

With that, Jinhwan had bid farewell to him before climbing in his car. Hanbin decided to go with him just to make sure he was really okay. Junhoe let them know he'd probably be staying at Solar's new apartment for a while, before waving as they left.

He watched Jinhwan's car disappear down the road, the wind sending chills up his spine. 

He really was sorry, he realized, but would that really be enough?


157:00 | be strong

"Excuse me, doctor, is there any update on his condition?" the frail brunette had been asking in a hectic manner, her eyes full of worry as the doctor had turned at the sound of her voice. She was standing right outside Sunwoo's hospital room, so he immediately knew who she was speaking of - not to mention the fact she came and asked about it every single day. The poor girl looked a mess, anyone could see so, with the way she wore no makeup, showing her dull skin and eyebags that had resulted from all of this. Her hair is tied on her head, but pieces still managed to fall in her face that she angrily pushes away, eyes practically pleading with the same doctor she had spoken to everyday. 

There's a sympathetic look in his eyes, as he rakes them up and down her figure. If Solar wasn't dressed so nicely, she would probably be getting judged right about then. Good thing she never left the house looking bad, she practically couldn't.

"I'm sorry, Ms. Kim," he cleared his throat finally, "we believe his treatment to be working, but healing from a pulmonary contusion doesn't happen quickly. We have to watch out for his fluid's overloading. He also had a serious case of cardiac tamponade, and while it's mostly under control, there is a risk of the pulmonary contusion setting it off again." Solar was blinking up at him as if he was crazy, literally half of the words he had spoke to her she didn't understand. 

"Please rest assured, we're keeping close track of his condition and if anything at all changes we will give you a call," he goes on, not even giving her a chance to stop and ask what this all meant, "be hopeful, Ms. Kim."

He bowed to her politely, before spinning on his heel and leaving. Solar was left there, blinking as she stared at the back of his lab coat, trying to piece together absolutely anything. She had a hard time remembering much from any science class in high school, so much so she found herself pulling out her phone as she made her way back towards the waiting room of the hospital, deciding to search up the terms she heard the doctor say.

What she did know, was that her siblings - particularly Jaehyun - had told her that he was only in a coma because of the medicine they'd used on him so that they were able to perform the necessary surgery. Jaehyun had told her that Sunwoo's organs weren't badly damaged, but this was sounding like the exact opposite of that statement.

Did he really lie?

This was the first time the doctor had gave her a full rundown. Usually, he would be too busy to give her much of an answer, but she guessed today he had more time - or maybe he realized she was in the dark and started to feel bad. 

Truthfully, it was probably for the better that she hadn't known of his true condition sooner. As her eyes scan the Wikipedia page, reading about pulmonary contusion and cardiac tamponade, only then did she comprehend that if she knew of any of this earlier, she would have fell apart even more so. Sunwoo's lungs and heart had been affected. He was in a condition that they needed to monitor and treat his lungs, but doing so could temporarily affect his heart condition, as it was in a state were it was likely to start bleeding out again. Such a thing happening to him could easily be fatal, especially with the fact he was in a coma which no one knew the length of. 

After reading the articles, she shuts off her phone, not even bothering to notice the texts she had gotten. Her head was too groggy, she felt like everything around her was falling all over again. How could she be dumb enough to think he wasn't in this kind of condition after getting shot in the chest? She was an idiot, an idiot who had just wanted everything to be okay. Solar knew it probably wouldn't have made sense for him to be perfectly in peak condition after that, but she wanted to believe that he was. Because then it would all hurt less, then it would make her think he'd be back soon.

While it was good for her to know the truth now, and while she didn't even have the heart to be mad at her siblings, it fucking hurt. Over the last few days, she had become stronger than she once was, but that didn't mean she wasn't going to be heavily affected by this.

And to think it had to be Wednesday morning, the day she was going to have to go back to YGE. The day she was likely going to see Jinhwan since their studios were so close together, and the boys would definitely be there in the dance studio to practice for the comeback on Saturday.
