It's okay, she tried to assure herself as she entered the busy waiting room. It was around 10 AM, and there were more than plenty of people in the hospital. Usually, when she'd visit Sunwoo, she would do it as late as possible, when there wasn't a lot of company. This meant it was more unlikely for her to ever be spotted.

But today she'd felt the need to go in the morning before she had to go back to the company. Going back was going to be traumatizing enough for her after everything, and she needed to brace herself for it. Seeing Sunwoo was a form of strength for her, because she knew she always had to keep going for him. 

Anxiety is creeping up her spine as she heads through the crowds of people. Pulling her mask further up her nose as if to hide her face more, she makes sure to keep her head down as she slides sunglasses over her eyes. The last thing she needed was to be recognized, and then articles would pop up everywhere about how she was at the hospital. 

Then again, it wouldn't be hard to just tell everyone she was visiting someone who was injured from the break in. 

But what would be hard to explain is if they saw who she was here with.

"Hey, are you okay?" Jungkook jumped to his feet immediately, rushing over to her in concern. He couldn't even see her eyes nor face since it was covered - much like his own - but just from her body language, he could sense something wasn't right. "What did the doctors tell you?"

Solar turns to glance around at all of the people. Did she really feel like talking about it in such a busy place? No, not at all. Wanting nothing more than to just get into the car, where it was safe and quiet, she turns back to the attentive boy. 

"I'll tell you in the car," she informs him. 

Jungkook nods in understanding, grabbing her hand and making his way to the exit. Solar follows closely behind, keeping her head down as she focused on the warmth of his hand in hers. Thankfully, she wasn't crying - at least not yet - and she was proud of herself for that.

They make their way past people, not looking suspicious or odd at all. Jungkook and Soomi were obviously experienced when it came to not looking like idols and not making it seem like you were trying to not be noticeable. It was all about feeling natural, feeling like you were normal people like everyone else around you. 

Though, maybe if they were better, that scandal would have never happened.

For this reason, the two of them should have probably been more careful. But all Solar was focused on was Sunwoo and his condition, and the fact she had to go back to work already. As for Jungkook, all he cared about what making sure she was okay, being there for her when she needed him. 

They had agreed to not worry about her manager since he likely wouldn't be trying anything for a while if he ever did after his failure. Plus, they didn't have much that they could do without Sunwoo or any of his gang members, which were supposed to be here any day now.

So a part of them didn't care, as Jungkook had let go of Solar's hand to instead wrap his arm around her shoulders. The girl cuddles into his side as she wraps her own small arms around his frame, inhaling his scent and closing her eyes as a brief moment of relaxation. 

The tall boy had clicked the unlock button to his Mercedes, causing her to open her eyes again. She'd sighed in obvious distress as Jungkook had opened the door for her, climbing in the car as she takes her glasses and mask off, glad to finally be able to breathe again.

Though, just as she did this, closing the door as Jungkook made his way to the driver's, there was a certain someone taking pictures of them from a distance.





+




"Do you have to go?!" Jungkook is whining much like that of a child as he reaches over the middle compartment of the car to place his hand over Solar's. His much bigger hand engulfed hers as he squeezes the life out of it, complaining on in his babylike tone, "Can't you just tell them you have too much going on? They'd have to understand after everything that happened."

Though Solar had already told Jungkook at least a trillion times that there was no way she would be able to skip out on her work. While she could agree with his insistence that it was honestly a little too early for YGE to expect all of them to casually show back up, what could she do about it? It wasn't like she had the power to waltz right into CEO-nim's office and demand he give them all more time off. 

Sure, she was horrified by everything that happened. And a part of her felt nauseous as she hesitantly looked up to the building they were parked in front of, but she knew she had to be strong about this. There was better security, guards and cameras, she reminded herself, there's no way my manager will have the guts to show up here.

Even though an article had been released regarding the fact that they had yet to find anything on the suspects of the incident, she still knew there was no way. 

"I don't have a choice, Kookie," she insists, finally looking back to the pouty boy. Jungkook's doe eyes play with her heart when she meets him due to how sad they looked, and it took everything in her to not just like over the middle compartment onto his lap. She tried to not show how anxious this was all making her as she shakes her head again, more so to convince herself, "I have to, and it's not like my manager is going to show up here again. They already told me he randomly 'quit,' so they're working on hiring a new one."

Jungkook exhales, staring at her for a long moment as she broke their eye contact again. He knew her well, well enough to know she was trying to hide how scared she was.

"Mi," he attests in that knowing tone of his, "it's not about that, it's about if you're ready for everything that going back to the company brings." Solar closed her eyes as soon as he had said her name because she knew he was going to say that exact thing. "Are you emotionally ready to go back to work after everything, especially this morning? Can you handle the busy schedule right now? Can you handle seeing that absolute fucking idiot?" 

His questions all slapped her in the face, and she had a feeling he would keep asking them - or start talking again for the 900th time about how much he hated Jinhwan and wanted to break him two foot legs. Before he could do so, she practically exploded in exasperation, "I don't know!" Jungkook had shut his mouth from her booming words, and Solar only leaned forward to bury her face in her hands as she muttered, "I don't know if I can handle it but the point is that I don't have a choice."

A pit of guilt forms in the pit of his stomach. Jungkook realized he might of pushed too hard completely without meaning to. He was just worried and he genuinely didn't think it was a good idea for her to go back to work already. Especially after she just found out Sunwoo's condition was a lot more severe than she anticipated, he didn't want to leave her on her own, especially in the building where it all happened. Solar had gone through so much trauma, being in that building would likely lead to another PTSD attack right when she was started to have less of those.

He couldn't stand the thought of that happening without him there to comfort her. Not being by her side for a split second was devastating to Jungkook, more so now than ever. 

His hand reaches over to fall onto her thigh, where he caresses it with his thumb in a soothing motion. Unbuckling his seatbelt, the boy scoots as close as he could to the middle compartment to lean over and kiss the side of her head in an apologetic manner, whispering in a saddened tone, "I'm sorry, Mi, I didn't mean to push so hard. I just don't want you to go through anything alone, I know how hard it is on you right now."

Slowly, she'd lifted her head up to peer at him, trying to let him know it was okay and she wasn't upset. Solar hadn't cried because she felt like she was dry of any tears at that point. But from the look on her face, you could tell she was tired, like she hadn't slept in weeks even though sleeping was all she had been doing. A weak smile forms on her lips as she places her hand over Jungkook's that was still on her thigh, shaking her head in dismissal, "don't worry, Kookie, I know you're worried," a deep exhale leaves her lips as her eyes linger on the screen of the car radio to read the time. She really had to go or she would be more than late, "but I need you to trust me, okay? I'm fine, it'll be fine," once again, she was speaking more to herself than she was to him. Jungkook knew this as he uneasily stares into her green irises, but nods anyway.

"Just - please text me every now and then?" he pouts to her as she was about to open the door, heaving her duffle bag over her shoulder, "I will spam your phone, and probably FaceTime you. If you don't answer I might try to climb through your studio window, seriously."

Solar turns to blink at him. "My studio is on the fourth floor."

But his serious expression didn't falter as he deadpanned, "I know."

Shaking her head, she couldn't help but smile at how he still managed to make her happy in the weirdest times. That chemistry they had was aiding her right when she needed it through all of this, and she was happy to have Jungkook there. He gave her a familiar sense of comfort she especially needed right now. Solar would always long for that sense, that familiarity, that peace that she felt when they were together, she didn't care if that was apparently "not what love meant" like Jinhwan had said. Because when she was with him, she felt loved, she felt safe.

"I'll see you at 5, Kookie."


158:00 | welcome back

"That was great, Soomi-ah!" her dance instructor was cheering, clapping a few times as she had turned to face the brunette with a gleaming smile. 

Solar bows in thanks, shaking her head as she tells her sweetly, "no, it's all thanks to your teaching, really! I feel like I'm really prepared for my comeback thanks to you!"

The lady grins ear to ear with a smile that seemed sincere as she slaps the girls shoulder playfully. "Well, that is my job, I'm lucky to have a good student like you, though," she's humming as she picks up her things at the side of the dance studio. 

Her instructor was already standing by the door, about to leave when she turns to Solar again, "make sure you keep working on the killing point, we'll focus on that next session, alright?"

"Absolutely!" she calls in response, bowing again as her instructor had bid her farewell and left the room.

A sigh leaves her lips as she watched her go, still breathing heavily from the practice. It had been a good few hours, meaning she still had a couple to go before Jungkook was picking her up. Right now, more than anything, all she wanted to do was collapse on the floor and sleep. 

Her shoes squeak against the shiny floor as she walks to where all of her things were. Sliding her back against the wall, she let out a long exhale as she digs in her bag for her phone, picking it up and turning on the screen to check her notifications.

She also had texts from Haechan, who she had guiltily forgotten about. She'd missed their coffee meeting last week and hadn't even texted the poor boy as to tell him why. Though, he clearly had a good idea after he had seen the news about her company, and had just been texting her telling her he hoped everything was okay and to let him know when she had free time since her comeback was coming up. There was a small smile on her lips when she read and replied to his texts, feeling thankful that he was such a nice kid and wasn't trying to stick his nose where it didn't belong. The world needed more people like him.

Since her practice was over, she wasn't quite sure what more to do. Thankfully, she had finished the last songs before everything had went haywire. Either way, she could always try to just produce and record everything on her own anyway. Though it would be a lot harder and probably not as polished, she could definitely still try.

Though as her mind recalls the events that had taken place in her studio, she wasn't sure if she would feel comfortable stepping foot in there.

Her siblings had dragged Jimin's body out of a less suspicious place when it all happened. A few days after the incident, all of the YGE artists had been taken into questioning just to see what they witnessed. Most of them had told white lies, as obviously no one was going to admit to anything that really happened. Since Jaehyun knew how to cover up evidence, not a single thing could be traced back to them. No one would ever know that they were the ones who killed Jimin - and that producer, and no one needed to know since it was for a greater cause anyway. 

But what happened was still molded into her brain. Even if she was apparently so "used" to those kinds of things, that didn't mean they didn't affect her like they affected other people.

At the same time, Solar wanted to work on music. Just like she had told Jungkook in their texts, that was a safe place for her. It was a place she felt like she could let go, that she was able to express herself in ways she could never imagine doing otherwise. Working on music was her therapy and her career, and she knew that she would have to go back in that studio eventually. 

Even though every single studio had been cleaned and revamped to practically look anew, as the company had informed everyone - she still didn't think she could stomach it - not today. Not after what happened with Jinhwan. She wasn't strong enough to sit in that room again yet.

From the fact she realized then that she couldn't work on music, she pouts. Blowing air into her cheek, Solar lets out a dramatic exhale as she sat there on the wooden floor of her dance studio, feeling rather unproductive now. She wondered if there was anything she could possibly do with her day now to even pass time until Jungkook would be here. She'd already had dance practice and vocal practice, and her rap practice wasn't until tomorrow. 

A sudden knock sounds at the door, causing her eyes to jerk up in that direction. Before she even says anything, it's cracked open, and the security guard - there's one for every room in the building - peeks his head through the crack. His eyes rake over the modernized dance studio, before they fall on the small girl that was resting on the side. Clearing his throat, he sends her a small smile as he informs her in an accidental booming tone, "Soomi, there's someone here with a pass for your studio, claiming they're a friend. Do you know them or should I alert security?"

His words left a nervous pit in her stomach as she wondered who it could be. This was not her first rodeo at this point - or even her second, and she was not about to deal with anymore crazed psychopaths somehow managing to get into her studios. 

She tried to appear calm and collected to the security guard as she used the wall to jump to her feet. The security guard realizes she was coming to see, and opens the door a bit more for her as he stiffens his back against it in a formal position where he would be able to grab a weapon if needed. His actions only made her more anxious as her sneakers click against the ground, and she wondered who it could be. If it was Junhoe or any of the iKON members, they wouldn't have a pass. 

"You're a what?" the security guard had been deadpanning to the person, his tone dripping of annoyance. He was clearly unimpressed and didn't believe a word that was coming out of their mouth. Solar found herself feeling glad that the security guards were so professional - that was, until she heard the voice.

"I said, I'm also an artist at YGE, don't you recognize me?!" the boy had been insisting, not in a rude way. In fact, he sounded more so sad that the man didn't know who he was. "I'm a friend of Soomi-ah's. Here," he pauses, holding up a hand as if he was a stop sign to the guard, who was only glaring down at him from his outstanding height - and the boy was fairly tall, too. The brunette fumbles with his pockets, patting each one in search of something, before a look of joy spreads over his face and he pulls out another card. Practically shoving it in the guard's face, he explains again triumphantly, "see? I'm not some weirdo that wandered in here, I'm also an artist here."

The guard didn't even really look at the card that was being held in his face. He glanced at it one time before remarking, "Okay, Jihoon of YGE, I still do not know you, and I still cannot permit you to bother Soomi." His words were almost sarcastic, and it made Jihoon was to catapult out the window.

Until finally, his saving grace appeared after what felt like decades. Soomi's small frame slowly appeared in the doorway of the dance studio, her eyes full of confusion that disappeared once they met Jihoon's. A breathe of relief had left her lips, as she offers him a greeting smile, "oh, Jihoon, hi. How are you?" 

Jihoon thought that surely Solar speaking to him so kindly and showing that she clearly knew him would be enough for the guard to let him by. But the man's facial expression hadn't changed one bit, he only continued to stand there, practically blocking his way. 

Feeling a little nonetheless frustrated with the fact he had been arguing with this guard for thirty minutes now, he returns a close-lipped smile, trying to hide his annoyance towards the guard as he tells her, "I think it could definitely be a little better. Can you tell this guy that I'm not some freak?"

At the realization that the guard had yet to move, Solar's eyes widen a bit, and she glances to the hard headed guard. "Ah, sir, you can let him in, he's a friend," she assures him in a honey tone, "thank you for the cautious measures, I appreciate your efforts." She bows to him to show further thanks even if he didn't see it. Really, he was just doing his job, but Solar was glad to have someone keeping another incident from happening. 

Finally, the guard nods curtly, stepping aside to allow the poor boy to enter the studio. As he does so, a loud exhale of relief leaves his lips in a dramatic matter. Jihoon sounded as if he'd been drowning underwater as he extends his arms by his sides, turning in a circle around the studio, "thank God, I was starting to actually feel like a weirdo."

She found herself laughing at her newest friend as he had then stopped his idiotic looking spinning when he seemed to process where he was. He was in Kim Soomi's dance studio, his mind practically screamed at him as he turned to admire everything. The walls were a jet black, with the cushioned attachments that were usually in dance studios. The thing was, they looked so soft to the touch, like you'd want to sleep on them. Mirrors lined one of the walls, and behind him was the YGE logo hand painted with Soomi's logo. The speakers that hung on the walls looked like they'd costed a fortune, and there was even what looked to be a relaxation corner with bean-bags and a mini-fridge.

He was bewildered. He wished their dance studio looked like this. Except, they didn't even have their literal own dance studio, they just used the unnamed ones.

Jihoon snaps back into reality when he hears her walking away, to the corner he was just thinking about. Solar had bent over to open the fridge, grabbing a new water before she falls onto one of the bean bags, leaning back and closing her eyes. 

After debating with himself for a minute on if it would be okay for him to sit too, he made his way over. We're friends, he reminds himself as he hesitantly falls onto one of the bean bags, calm down, we're friends, she said so herself.

Still, he felt bad.

"I'm sorry if I bothered you," he blurts before he could stop himself. The brunette slowly opened her eyes, yawning before she looked to him intently, "I just wanted to check on you after, you know, all of that. I know being alone might not be all that easy, so I wanted to offer you some company."

Solar found herself wondering just how much Jungkook had told Jihoon. He seemed to have a general idea of what went down, so he must know the full story. Though she wondered if any of that included things about her past.

Either way, she felt thankful for him. 

"You're not intruding, I'm glad you came," she assures him with a grin, easing Jihoon's previously spiked nerves, "it feels weird to be back here after everything. I haven't even been in my music studio because I don't think I can face it alone."

He nods in understanding, pursing his lips for a moment as he debated with himself again. It seemed that a common issue around Solar and any of her friends - since literally all of them were his superiors - he found himself second guessing everything. He never knew what to say, even though he had long ago realized after spending that night with them that they were normal ordinary people just like him. His nerves just kept getting the best of him.

Finally, he builds up his courage. "I can go to your studio with you," he wanted to physically punch himself from the way the words slid off his tongue, but it was far too late to take them back. He hoped she could understand he was trying to be helpful. "Whenever you feel like you want to do it, I can be there to make it slightly less scary - hopefully," he adds.

Solar was practically staring into his soul at that point, and it made him melt into the floor. Who could imagine being alone with Kim Soomi in her own dance studio?! He couldn't, and it made the fact that it was happening that much nerve-wrecking for him. 

He got so caught up in his own head again that he didn't notice her staring at him for a few minutes. Solar could tell he was nervous around her, for reasons she understood. She had a feeling Jihoon would loosen up over time, and it would make her feel bad to pester him about it. Still, she found the younger boy almost cute as she chuckled, shrugging, "that's sweet of you, I was going to go there today but I didn't think I could stomach it after - that."

Jihoon nods, feeling a bit better since he didn't exactly get rejected. If this was her dance studio, what the hell did her music studio look like?! He couldn't even begin to imagine.

His mind wanders back to everything that happened that day. It was weird to think it had already been around a week since those events occurred. They'd be molded into his head forever even though he hadn't seen much of the commotion other than Solar and her bleeding hand. Just hearing about what actually happened was traumatic enough. He wasn't one to agree with any kind of violence, but he could understand why Solar did what she did. There was nothing wrong with protecting yourself against someone who was literally trying to kill you. The way he saw it, she did what she had to do. But he could imagine how that negatively impacted someone, and for that reason, he felt bad for her.

He felt bad for her because of everything. He never thought the biggest female idol in Korea would be going through such horrific things, as far as being betrayed by so many people under her own company. It even made him uncomfortable.

Not to mention the fact she'd seen someone close to her get shot. 

Not knowing what more to say, his eyes wander to her hand that was laying in her lap, and he curiously finds himself asking, "how's your hand?"

Solar perks up a bit from his question, as if she had forgotten the fact her hand was injured - because she had. After a while, she got used to only using one hand for her daily activities. At this point, she was starting to use her hurt hand anyway, as the pain was slowly beginning to suffice - or at least hurt less when she tried to do minimal tasks. 

Despite having the cast still on, she was still practicing and intending to have her comeback. When she had her first stage, she was just hoping it would be healed enough to be out of the cast. 

"Fine, I don't feel it that much anymore," she half-lied, and Jihoon seemed to know that couldn't really be true since she had an entire knife go through it.

Silence falls over the two, but it wasn't an awkward silence, at least not to Solar. She was exhausted, to say the least, and had leaned back in the bean bag, closing her eyes and sprawling her arms out on either sides of her. Jihoon watched as she did so, chuckling a little to himself because she looked rather silly.

"Jihoon, I can feel you staring at me," her voice nearly made him jump, and only then did he realize he was.

Her teasing tone made his cheeks grow red, but to his avail, her eyes were still closed so she had no idea. He didn't falter as he hums defensively, "sorry, you just looked stupid."

He couldn't believe he just said that.

"Yah," the brunette snaps, suddenly sitting up in her bean bag, eyes peering straight at him, "who are you? Junhoe?"

"No, I'd say I'm a good five or so inches shorter."

"Wow, you really took that literally."

"And my hair isn't black, I prefer a lighter brown," he'd went on in contemplation, "I'd say I have more of a casual style, also."

Solar was blinking at him in bewilderment, but she found herself laughing. This kid was something else. "Alright, I get the memo," she insists, "plus, if you were actually Junhoe I'd have beaten you up with a bean bag by now."

"He's got it rough," he says as if he was sympathetic for her best friend.

Solar shakes her head, "I think I'm the one who has it rough, try dealing with him for longer than sixty minutes."

Their conversation was cut short by Jihoon's phone going off. The loud ringing echoed through the dance studio, causing the boy to sigh in annoyance as he shifted in his seat to dig it out of his pocket. "I swear..." he's mumbling to himself as he turns on the screen, "aish, they never leave me alone." Solar assumed it was his members as he'd excused himself to take the call, going to the other side of the room.

Deciding to mind her own business, she pulled out her own phone to check the time. She'd been talking to Jihoon for a while now, meaning there was only around 30 minutes until Jungkook would be here to pick her up. A small smile spreads over her face as she realizes how quickly time had passed, it was nice talking to him. Just having casual company was peaceful, it was nice after everything that had been going on.


kookie: I GET TO SEE MI IN 30 MINUTES

kookie: IM SO EXCITED

kookie: BEST DAY OF MY LIFE

mi: jungkook...

mi: ur so

kookie: cute?

kookie: absolutely irresistible?

kookie: i know, thank you

mi: stupid

kookie: what's it like lying to yourself mi

mi: ur so annoying

kookie: you know what 

kookie: i'm getting the scissors

mi: NO YOU ARE NOT 

kookie: I CAN CUT MY HAIR IF I WANT TO

mi: DONT FUCKING TOUCH IT


Solar is laughing to herself down at her phone screen. She was enjoying her and Jungkook's stupid texting sessions while she was at work. It was obviously all fun in games, although she would genuinely be upset if he cut his hair.

Her focus on texting was cut short by the exasperated voice of Jihoon across the room as he tried to speak lowly into his phone, "this is exactly why I didn't tell you guys! I knew you'd all be weird!"

She peers at him in wonder as to what was going on. It didn't take a genius to guess it had to do with her.

"No! Absolutely not, you guys are embarrassing!"

Jeez, he was really giving it to them.

Trying to mind her own business, she distracts herself on her phone again by opening Instagram. It was the first time she had actually been back on social media since everything happened. Her eyes bulged as she realized how many notifications she had, mainly from concerned fans asking if she was okay and saying they were there for her after the incident at the company. She found herself feeling comforted as she read through a few posts they had made.

She would always be thankful to them. Solar realized she definitely needed to post on her socials just to let everyone know she was okay and that she would be seeing them soon. Though the news about her comeback had been released, she felt like it was important for her to personally let her fans know it was happening.

Agreeing with herself that she would post right then since she had nothing else to do whilst waiting for the clock to hit 5 and Jihoon to finish his phone call, she swipes to go to the camera app. 

But her eyes caught something in her feed as soon as she switched to the tab. 



@ gnani: see you on Saturday ikonics 


She sat there for a good five minutes, staring down at the picture like an absolute idiot. Until her throat began to feel dry, and she found herself coughing a bit as if she hadn't been drinking the water that was beside her. Jinhwan invaded her mind like a virus, taking over every part of her body and making her crave him. Seeing him in all his glory was a slap in the face, a reminder of everything, a reminder of the words he'd so hatefully spat at her. 

Though she'd been doing so good with getting him off her mind, and Jungkook also helped with that, seeing him made her chest ache all over again. The more she recalled that time the more it felt like some sort of sick dream, all of it did. 

And the more she felt the urge to talk to him. But she didn't think she could do it, she didn't think she was well enough to do it. How would she know he even wanted to talk to her? How did she know his feelings hadn't changed? 

Her mind seemed to scream at her that he was likely only right down the hall. The boys were likely in their own dance studio, practicing like their lives depended on it since their comeback was only in two days.

"Are you okay?" 

Her eyes jerk up to meet Jihoon's, who were full of concern as they peer down at her. She tries to talk, but her throat felt hoarse. Clearing it a few times, she sends him a small smile, "mhm, I'm fine, no worries."

Jihoon didn't seem to believe her. Falling back onto the bean bag, he wracked his brain for what to say to lighten the mood. 

"hey, do you want to hear a joke?"



159:00 | creative juices

The days after that Thursday flew by like absolutely nothing. Before anyone knew it, it was Saturday, the weekend - and also the release of iKON's new mini album. Not that this was a big deal to anyone outside of YGE, but for anyone in the company, it was always important when an artist had a comeback. It was why Solar couldn't walk through the halls without hearing someone talking about, and it drove her insane.

It wasn't that she didn't want to support her friends. Solar just couldn't stand to see Jinhwan, and she had already informed Junhoe and the other boys of this. They were understanding about it, as always - you know, all of them except for Junhoe, who kept telling her she just might want to give it a listen for a reason he wouldn't explain. 

She'd hate to disappoint him, but it wasn't happening. She had her own things to focus on, like her own comeback that was four days away.

And she could finally say, she was feeling a bit better again. After that awful bombshell when she found out about Sunwoo's condition, she didn't think she would be feeling any better anytime soon. But Solar was becoming a little stronger with each day that passed, she was finding a way to find joy in the things around her, whether it was her friends or Jungkook. 

At the company, now, she also wasn't alone much. Jihoon had ended up keeping her company everyday now, so much so that the security guard wasn't pestering him anymore. She could tell he was slowly beginning to feel more comfortable and stop being so self conscious around her, and it was nice to have another friend under YGE that she could trust.

Since they were in the company and Jihoon was only a trainee, they didn't have to worry about any ridiculous rules, either. Even though no one really paid attention to them anymore - clearly. Though the "no friends" rule had mainly been done away with, the only one anyone had to pay attention to was the dating ban. And it wasn't like she was going to be making out with Jihoon ever.

That would be weird.

The days that had passed were almost carefree to her. She had peaceful times at the company practicing and hanging out with Jihoon, and then she went home to Jungkook and all of her friends, where they would watch movies and enjoy each other's company. Every night, she would head to the hospital to visit Sunwoo with Jungkook, staying there with him for at least a couple of hours before she'd head home. It was her daily routine that hadn't really consisted of anything regarding the incident.

Jaehyun, Juyeon, and Chaerin were likely handling that. She hadn't really spoken to any of the gang members because they were in contact with her older brother. Though everyone had hoped they would've been here by now, they'd supposedly gotten caught up with business in New York. Though the siblings were worried that they wouldn't be here in time to help, they never mentioned it to Sol. The last thing they wanted was to set her off again now that she seemed to be doing better.

So, that was how it had been. No one mentioned it to her, allowing her to be distracted in her own world of peace.

"You've never wrote a song before?" she had been gawking at the boy walking beside her on the 4th floor of YGE. They had just left her dance studio, as Jihoon had met up with her after her dance practice for today. It was around lunchtime, meaning that there was lunch being served in the cafeteria for anyone who wanted it. Usually, Solar was too caught up in her work - or with Jinhwan - that she had never cared to go, but Jihoon had insisted she come try to the lunch foods. 

The boy only shakes his head, a smile on his face from how she was reacting as if it was the craziest thing she had ever heard anyone say. "No, I mean, it's not that I haven't tried," he admits, "song writing just doesn't come naturally to some people."

"What?!" she cries, "bullshit, we're going to my studio after lunch."

She had said those words in a demanding tone, not thinking twice as they flew off her lips. Knowing well that she had yet to step foot in her music studio still, Jihoon found himself looking at her in confusion and shock. He tried to figure out if he should ask about it, or be keep his mouth shut and be happy she was feeling ready to do such a thing.

Too afraid of the first option, he sticks with the second. "Really, should I just start being embarrassed now?"

"No!" she's chuckling in dismissal, "it'll be great, once you get in the flow of it, the song will just hit you."

"That sounds a little painful, I think I change my mind."

"Nope, too late!" she sternly tells him, "no backing out of this one, Jihoon."

"Okay," he sighs, and she thought he had actually just given into her demand. That was, until he hums, "what if I tell you I'm only going to rap?"

Solar was very aware he wasn't a rapper thanks to him attempting to rap her song Lip & Hip the other day. Still, she shrugs, "you do whatever you feel like you're meant to."

"Wow, Kim Soomi is so poetic, no wonder she's a hit songwriter."

She snorts humorously at his comment. He had a weird witty sense of humor about him that she found rather entertaining. Jihoon was always cracking jokes and lightening the mood, he was much like Junhoe in that sense. But he was a lot better at giving advice.

And probably a lot smarter.

Solar was so caught up in their conversation she didn't notice the other person that was walking out of the elevator they were heading towards.

Jihoon himself had only noticed when it was too late. He felt someone burning holes into his soul, causing his eyes to curiously wander away from the girl whom he was walking with. A confused look remained on his face as he wondered why anyone would be staring at them no intently, and he felt his stomach drop once he received an answer to his question.

Kim Jinhwan sported an all black attire, blonde caramel locks falling in his eyes as he made his way back towards presumably his studio. He looked to of been about to leave for a stage as he made his way through the halls when he'd noticed the two. 

The poor boy walking with Solar felt his throat go dry. For the short time he knew Jinhwan personally, he was really nice to him. Jihoon hadn't, like everyone else, witnessed the fight between them first hand to understand what it was really like or how bad it really was. And now that everyone seemed to dislike Jinhwan, he was scared to give his honest opinion on the situation - which was that he didn't think Jinhwan had any ill intent, he couldn't be a bad person. Jihoon had seen the way he looked at Soomi.

Jinhwan wasn't glaring at them rudely. His eyes were full of only longing as he peered at the brunette, who's head was down as she was looking at her phone, texting Jungkook back because he wouldn't stop using the threat of cutting his hair if she didn't reply. Perhaps she felt the glare, too, but didn't care to look up, or maybe she was too afraid to. 

Either way, the only one who caught Jinhwan's gaze was Jihoon, until it was too late and he'd disappeared past them. 

Swallowing the lump in his throat, they finally reached the elevator. Solar had yawned as she looked up from her phone, only then noticing the bewildered look on Jihoon's face. "Why do you look like you just saw Jesus part the ocean?" 

He was still at a bit of a loss for words, unable to help but wonder what would have occurred if Solar had noticed Jinhwan. 

"Uh, I had a sudden inspiration," he blurts in response, "for a song."

Solar beams from this, practically smiling ear to ear as she cheers, "really?! That's great, we can work on it right away!"






+




"Yah, Jungkook," the voice of Park Jimin had filled the maknae's ears, who was leaned over to peer at his laptop screen on Solar's bed, eyes narrowed as he was skimming over the words on his screen. At the presence of his hyung, he hadn't even looked up from how focused he was, causing Jimin to urge him as he makes his way deeper into her bedroom, "Jungkook, we need to talk."

"Jungkook," he calls again when the boy still doesn't bother to respond. The mattress had sunk under the new weight added to it as Jimin sat on the side of the bed by Jungkook, and only then did the focused boy finally decide to acknowledge him.

But he was clearly not happy about being bothered. His eyes show that exact feeling as they dully look to his friend's, "yes, Jimin-sshi? How can I help you today?" he deadpans the questions, only earning a look of disapproval from Jimin. 

"Jungkook," he sighs, his eyes seeming to inform him that whatever was on his mind was serious, "have you noticed anything off about Mi?"

His doe eyes blink a few times in confusion. Jungkook wandered through his mind for a moment, recalling the last several days he had spent with her. He hadn't thought her personality was different, the only thing was the fact she was getting better. Why was Jimin acting like there was something negative about that? 

"Sure," he puzzles, "she's happier."

Jimin let out a deep exhale, appearing rather distressed as he shifted his position on the bed, running a hand through his messy hair and diverting his attention elsewhere. He could feel Jungkook's confused eyes on him as he did so. He seemed to realize, then, that Jungkook didn't know of the events that had occurred with Soomi in New York. He was lucky she even told him to be fair, it was something she had kept fairly secret to literally everyone else other than her sister who had caught her.

Her resurfaced drug problem. He was afraid of it coming back, even though she had her siblings and Chanhee here, who would never let something like that happen. Jimin just couldn't understand how she could become so upbeat, like she was walking on rainbows everywhere. It was concerning to him, not because he didn't want her to feel better, but because he worried that she was feeling better only due to one thing. 

He opened his mouth to speak, before shutting it abruptly. He can't tell Jungkook that, that would betray Soomi's trust. And if she wanted Kook to know, she would tell him. It wasn't Jimin's place to do that.

"Is there something I'm missing?" Jungkook was seeming to get the memo because he couldn't seem to grasp what Jimin was getting at, "Are you hinting that she's been drinking again? I would know if she was."

"No, it's not that," he shakes his head, "I just worry about her, Kook."

Brushing his long hair out of his face, he narrows his eyes at Jimin. Jungkook was more than lost, but his hyung didn't seem like he was going to tell him anything more. "Well, I would hope so," he blatantly responds, "you are supposed to be one of her closest friends, after all."

He diverts his eyes from Jimin after that back to his computer screen.

The boy had exhaled deeply once he did so, as this meant he was likely letting it go. Jungkook was confident in his ability to watch over Mi, and he believed that if she had a serious problem he would know. The thing was, he never expected her to ever hide anything from him. His trust was high in Soomi, so much so that he would never suspect she was doing anything behind his back.

Jimin realized he would just have to figure it out on his own. Her siblings and childhood friends were too busy already as it was. He didn't want to give anyone else more stress, they were all worried enough.

So, he changed the subject, but as his eyes fall onto Jungkook's laptop screen, he was more than curious at what he was possibly doing. "Why are you looking at flights to Jeju?" he inquires blankly as the dark haired boy had been leaning forward in concentration again.

"Aish, hyung," Jungkook only responds disapprovingly, "why do you have to be so nosy?" 

Jimin already knew why anyway, as a ping of jealousy pinches at his chest. He pushed it away the second he felt it, shaking his head as he rose from the bed. He had other things to focus on, anyway, like Soomi's well being.

And he hoped his suspicions were only that - suspicions.





+





"Do you think it sucks?" the boy falters once he looks to Solar's facial expression, perceiving it as something negative since she had yet to say a word, "it's okay, you can tell me it sucks," he tries to assure her thinking she was afraid to blurt out the truth of her opinion. Jihoon hadn't actually had a song idea when he told her, those were just the first words that came out of his mouth. Now he had to actually try to come up with one, which was something he had never done seriously before, and he had a feeling his nauseating attempt had left the famous songwriter rather disappointed. 

Solar didn't say anything right away. Her lips form a straight line, pieces of her brown hair falling in front of her face as she scooted a little closer on the seat towards her piano. Raising her hands, her fingers are placed above the keys, and she was clearly in immense concentration, as if she was planning an entire song all in her head, as her eyes rake over the keys repeatedly, seemingly counting something.

Suddenly, a triumphant look takes over her expression, and her hands fall off the piano and back onto her lap - all without playing a single key. Jihoon realized, as he watched her, she genuinely had come up with a song like that. He couldn't say he wasn't in awe by her skill.

"No," she finally declares, "every song starts somewhere. I didn't wake up one day and just have an entire hit song engraved in my brain."

She should've expected one of his typical comments. "Are you sure? You are Kim Soomi."

"Jihoon," her tone is full of warning as she looks to him sternly, "stop talking about me like I'm some God or something, I've just been doing this for a long time."

She didn't sound upset or angered by it, Solar just wanted Jihoon to see her as a normal person, a friend. She hated the thought that she was his superior. When she was with him, she felt like a normal person, almost like she was a trainee again messing around, and it was an enjoyable feeling she wanted him to feel with her, too. 

The boy had been in deep thought about how he should respond, when he didn't get a chance to, because she had sat up straight on the bench they were sharing again. Her hands fall over the keys once more, and she briefly glances to him as she asserts, "here, I have an idea for the melody, tell me what you think."

Jihoon could only admire her as her fingers had effortlessly began to dance across the keys. Her green eyes weren't even focused on the keyboard they sat in front of, but instead, they fluttered closed as she played, as if she was inhaling the music like oxygen. She was clearly in her own world, captivated by the notes that filled the room around them, and he found himself thinking how beautiful it was.

Before he knew it, she finishes the short lived melody, opening her eyes again and turning to him on the bench with a grin, "well, what do you think?!"

Not wanting to admit he might of been paying more attention to her than he was the song, Jihoon only knows to nod, "it's great, how did you come up with that off the top of your head?"

"I don't know," she shrugs blankly as she turns away from him again, "it just happens." He thought she was going to play more as she placed her fingers over the keys again, and he piped up at the sight. That was, until she added, "okay, now sing the lyrics you have so far with the melody. Ready?"

Jihoon's breathe got caught in his throat. It caused him to cough a couple of times as he looked to the expectant girl, who was only peering at him, waiting for him to signal that he was ready. He was indeed not ready, and he probably never would be, to sing in front of one of the biggest celebrities in Korea. He was a mere trainee, and it was hard enough being evaluated by his own vocal coaches every month, nonetheless by Soomi herself. 

And it wasn't like he was even warmed up. He was going to make a fool of himself.

She noticed his nerves getting the best of him, and reached a finger to poke his cheek, "hey, chill out, I'm not even a singer."

True, she indeed was known more for her rapping.

"Fine," he exhales, clearing his throat to try and prepare himself, "I'm prepared - possibly." Because now he was more focused on the fact he had just poked his cheek.

Solar chuckles at him, before she slowly counts down from three. As soon as she hits one, her fingers begin to dance across the keys once again, so easily she didn't even have to think about it. Music was all she had done the last eight years of her life, that was the only reason it came so naturally to her. She didn't expect someone who wasn't as experienced as herself to immediately appear so, because she definitely wasn't in the beginning either - at least in her opinion.

Plus, Jihoon was a trainee who still had a while to debut. Though it was coming up, most idols still weren't at their best by the time they debut. It takes years of being an idol to really reach your potential. She didn't assume that he was a bad singer, she just didn't have high expectations.

But as the boy had closed his eyes, taking a deep breath, and began to sing the few lines of the song he was wrote, she found herself feeling a little shocked. Her eyes wander to him as she kept track of the melody, watching his technique and posture, he was good for a trainee. 

The two of them were sitting closer than they were even aware of on that piano bench in her studio. Her studio that she didn't think she would have been stepping a foot in for a while, but frankly, she was doing fine. It was her idea for them to come here, and she didn't regret it at all. Solar was happy. 

Their shoulders are grazing each other as she continued to play. Jihoon had opened his eyes, seeming to relax into his voice as he was on the last line of the lyrics. Only then did he realize they were closer than he anticipated, as Solar's eyes as bored into his own.

He was in a trance.

A trance that was cut off from a knock sounding on the door of the studio.

It seemed to surprise Soomi, too, as she had jumped a bit from the loud sound. Her hands fall from the keyboard, but she didn't even have a chance to scoot away from the boy she sat next to as the security guard opened the door partly, "Ms. Soomi, you have a requested visitor, he says he's-"

"-Your absolute favorite human being and best best friend!"

The voice of Koo Junhoe made her snort as the security guard had let out a sigh of disappointment. "He's fine, you can let him in," she assures the tired looking guard, "thank you!"

She had then scooted away from Jihoon so that Junhoe didn't have a mental shutdown. Though, he still caught a glimpse as he'd walked inside the studio, after making a snarky comment to the poor security guard who looked like he hated his job.

"Oh, hi Jihoon," he offers the young boy, who only sends him a sweet smile and a short bow, "I saw your studio was occupied and I couldn't help myself," he's explaining his reasoning for showing up as he falls onto the sofa booths that outlined the walls, "and here she is, writing music like there's no fucking tomorrow."

Solar shrugs, knowing he was doing his June attempt at questioning her. "I couldn't hide from my own studio forever," is all she says, "plus, there's tight security in this place now, it's definitely a lot safer."

"That's for fucking sure," he scoffs, "dude literally knew who I was and still wouldn't let me in."

"Yes, Junhoe, that's his job."

"Okay and? My job is to bother you unannounced, how am I supposed to accomplish that now?"

She sighs as she peers at him impatiently. Junhoe's humor - while it was funny - also drove her to the brink of insanity. 

"Your face ruined my creativity," she deadpans, causing his eyes to fall out of their sockets as if this was the most absurd thing he'd ever heard.

"Incorrect," he snaps, "if anything, you should be thanking me - right about now."

Solar rolls her eyes as she rises to her feet, making her way to sit by him. Junhoe was thinking to himself for a moment, trying to decide if he should actually try to talk to her about what he'd come there for. His hyung's desperate face pops back into his brain, and he pursed his lips as he then looked from Solar to Jihoon.

Was it really a good time?

Realizing it was most definitely not, he tried to think of something else to say. And like usual, Junhoe's head always went to one thing first.

"You look like shit, did you not sleep last night?"


160:00 | music bank

Music Bank, and any other music show, was as hectic as ever. Staff and idols would always be seen rushing every which way, the halls would be bustling with life, and everyone was typically busy with getting everything together for each stage. It was fast paced, and presumably nauseating to anyone who wasn't used to it.

Ask Soomi a year ago, she would shrug it off like nothing. But it felt like forever since she had a comeback stage - because it had been. She was only realizing how long it'd been since she performed at that very moment, now that she was standing there in her dressing room, makeup done to perfection and hair curled to the tip. Her eyes were glittery, her outfit shining along with them under the bright lighting of the room. Standing in front of the large mirror that lines one of the walls, she exhales deeply, reminding herself that this would be nothing to her. She wasn't a rookie, this was easy to her.

Turning away from the mirror, her eyes are met with Kevin shoving the snacks, that were always set up on tables in the dressing rooms, down his throat like a literal psychopath. Jacob was scolding him, telling him that they weren't for him and he needed to calm down before he inflated. Changmin was just laughing at the fact he was getting whipped cream from the sweets all over his face. And lastly, Chanhee was complaining about how he hated all of them.

Her typical friends.

Solar's siblings weren't there with her, as there were other matters happening today they had to attend to. There was also the fact that Juyeon or Jaehyun were still performing their duty they promised her - to watch over Sunwoo at all times.

At the sight of her finishing staring at herself in the mirror so intently, Chanhee notices her movement towards them. The pink haired boy rushes over to her, cupping her face with his hands as if she was a newborn baby. "Oh, Sol, are you nervous?" he's observing, "you've practiced well for this, so stop looking like you're about to do improv."

She pouts, "hey, it's impossible to not be nervous before a stage, okay? You try performing on television."

"I would love to, Sol, but I'm not the one who chose being an idol as my career path."

"You'll do GREAT, Solar!" Changmin is cheering loudly. All the other friends had noticed the conversation occurring, and also the fact she had to go on soon.

"It'll be a piece of cake," Kevin is assuring her as he walks to sit on one of the sofas, shoving another cupcake in his mouth, "much like this," he adds with a mouthful of cupcake. His loud chewing as he spoke with his mouth open caused Chanhee to turn towards him with a face of absolute utter disgust.

"Can you not you vile fucking creature?"

Before more bickering could start, Jacob had cleared his throat. "Why don't we watch whoever's performing now to ease your nerves?" he offers brightly as he sits next to Kevin. Everyone had taken seats on the multiple sofas, and Jacob grabbed the remote to the large TV that hung on the wall to watch the current performers. The poor boy had no idea who was conveniently performing at that time, all he was trying to do was take her mind off her stage. 

He casually presses the on button, and everyone watched as the screen lit up. Solar hadn't been watching yet, as she was momentarily distracted on checking her social media and replying to texts. All of her friends were excited for her comeback, meaning that they were all watching it at home and spamming her phone about it. This included Jungkook, Jimin, and the rest of the Bangtan members, who she would've loved to drag along to her dressing room - except for the fact it would've made an easy ploy for a scandal. It made her wish they were already promoting their new album, but timing couldn't always be perfect.


kookie: WE'RE SO EXCITED MI!!!

kookie: 


kookie: DO WE LOOK AS EXCITED AS WE ARE?

mi: no u guys look like ur about to catapult off a fucking bridge

kookie: sorry jimin said to look serious

kookie: BUT I PROMISE WE'RE EXCITED

kookie: YOU'RE GONNA DO GREAT

kookie: i haven't heard the full song either!!! u only let me hear those snippets 

kookie: and i already KNOW it's good

kookie: bc mi is so talented

mi: i hope it's received by everyone else that way

mi: it's been so long since i've performed 

kookie: i know, first comeback stages are always hard

kookie: remember it'll feel natural after you get the first one over with

kookie: don't sweat, mi :) we'll be here cheering you on

kookie: wish it was in person though :(

mi: ur spirit is here in person

mi: probably the only reason i'll make it through

mi: tell chimmie i love him

kookie: wtf what about me

mi: i might also love you

mi: idk i'll get back to you on that one

kookie: i'm throwing jimin out the window


She sat there for a second, staring down at the text, until another notification banner caught her off guard.


chim: pls help me


Ah, she sighed down at the screen, Jungkook is something else. 

Only then did she tune back into what was going on. Jacob had mashed the button, revealing the current performer. Solar's eyes didn't wander up then since she was distracted. What caused her irises to jerk to the TV screen, however, was when she heard that voice - that familiar voice she had been avoiding for so long.

It'd almost been two weeks. Two weeks since the incident at the company, and two weeks since her and Jinhwan's fight that made their already suffering relationship crumble like leaves. She hadn't heard a word from him. All she had done was focus on her work, friends, and Sunwoo - forcing Jinhwan to the back of her mind. And whenever she talked to any of the iKON members, they understood that she wasn't interesting in hearing about him. Even if she did get the temptation to talk to him, even if she did have flashbacks to their times together, and even if a part of her still always missed him - she couldn't talk to him. Solar was busy, she was busy getting better, being happier - no matter what it took, that was what she would be.

And she was - she was happy. 

Which was why she never planned to listen to iKON's comeback. Why would she try to set herself off? 

But she realized then, as his honey voice had sunk through her ears, there was really no avoiding it. She would've had to hear it eventually since they were promoting at the same time, after all.

Jacob is sitting there in shock for a second, nervously looking to her. Chanhee had let out a disappointed sigh, about to likely call him an idiot for even turning the TV on, when Solar had abruptly blurted, "it's fine, you can leave it."

The friends exchange confused glances. They expected her to have broken down over it. Not because she was weak, but because Solar had just been having a lot of episodes like that recently. The fact she was so fine was unexpected.

Jacob only mutters an okay as he slowly sets the remote down. Solar's eyes wander upwards then to the TV screen, landing on the last thing she was planning to see that day. Jinhwan's hair was lighter now, and he was sporting a black suit as if he was going to an elegant event. The way it was styled left only a few strands hanging in his face, and it was a different look on him.

But he was pretty. He would always be pretty.

That wasn't really what she was focused on, though. She was focused on the words, as that voice filled her ears, sinking through her skin and ripping at her heart strings. The words she didn't expect to actually affect her.

In fact, it still seems like a lie
Like you'd come back with a big smile
Beautiful as you were
Only now I feel regret
All I did was make your beautiful smiling face covered in tears
This isn't what I wanted

Everyone's eyes had slowly fell on Solar again. They all were thinking the exact same thing - the words were speaking a little too literally.

Eventually ending up in this position, coming to this
I'm the one who messed it up
Couldn't even protect a flower like you
All the flashing sparks in our relationship
Have turned into white ashes
From just anybody to my love
From fate to lovers
Blossomed a beautiful love flower
Why has laughter turned into tears?
Once mine, now a stranger
Thought we'd be together forever

"Here, why don't we just-" Chanhee tries to say, rising to his feet to grab the remote control. He had a feeling this was becoming too much, just the way Jinhwan looked so sad every time the camera would flash to him. It was becoming too much for Chanhee, so he assumed it had to be ripping Solar's soul apart.

But she cuts him off as soon as he was about to press the off button, "No, Hee, it's fine, it's just a song."

She was the last person who would ever say that. It was just a song? Chanhee turns to blink at her, even he was a little lost as her closest friend on planet Earth. 

"Sol," he's saying in a cautious tone as he sits back beside her, "you're starting to concern me even more so."

Solar isn't paying attention to him, though. Chanhee was ready to pick her up and shake her until she explained what the hell was going on because he had never seen Solar Lee, out of all people, so lacking of emotion. She was the most emotional person he knew, and it was horrifying that she was so casually watching a performance by Jinhwan that was so clearly for her.

Her green eyes are only blankly staring at the TV screen as the final chorus of the song played.

If I knew it was our last, I would have
Kept you in my arms
I've never not loved you
From the beginning and even until now

The stage lights dimmed slowly before it went dark, and Solar had pursed her lips as it ended completely and the MCs came back on the screen. All of her friends were watching her in concern, but she didn't say anything to them as she sighed, rising to her feet and dusting herself off. She was supposed to be going on, right after iKON she now knew, and she had to focus on her performance more so than that song. 

Don't think about it, that's not what matters.

Chanhee rises to his feet, ready to bombard her, but he didn't get a chance to as a series of knocks sounded from the door of the dressing room.

"Soomi, are you prepared for your stage? There's five minutes till, we need to get you in position and do outfit and makeup checks!" her new manager had been calling from the other side of the door.

"Yes, manager-nim, I'm ready!" she calls back brightly, before turning to the friends who were all standing up now, "well, see you guys after, wish me luck!" she offers them with a wide smile, before spinning on her heel to head to the door.

"Good luck, Sol!"

"You'll do great, just don't fall!"

They all watch as she leaves the room, closing the door behind her. The friends all instantly turn to each other, the same looks of worry on their faces, as they'd clearly been waiting for the moment she left to discuss.

"What was that about?" Kevin is the first to say, "I've never seen her so - unbothered - which you know, isn't bad, but it's so unlike her."

"I know she's just forcing herself not to think about Sunwoo or Jinhwan," Changmin is thinking out loud, "but that song was clearly for her, I swore she would've started crying. I was so worried as soon as he came on the screen..." he trails off, a frown tinting at his lips.

As they all continue to voice out their concern and confusion, Chanhee had a knowing look on his face. He realized that this seemed weirdly familiar in more ways than he could count. It caused an angered knot to form in his stomach, even though he knew he shouldn't let himself be angry since he didn't have an absolute confirmation. But yet, he had that gut feeling, and Chanhee's gut feelings were never wrong.

Especially because they had been through this before.


+



Breathe, just breathe. 

She repeated those words in her head what felt like a million times. But Solar still felt on edge, she still felt like a tiger about to pounce on their prey - or particularly, anyone who dared to bother her.

Her heeled boots click loudly against the tiled floors as she speed walks through the halls of Upper East Side High. Students pass her, busy with their own lives and conversations, but a few still notice how upset she looked - and the fact she wasn't with the boy that was always glued to her side.

And being that everyone knew of them and their relationship all too well, she heard the whispers as they passed, "look at her, do you think she had a fight with Sunwoo?!"

"Maybe they broke up!"

"No way, he's whipped for her - I don't really get it, though."

She sends the current students passing a flaming glare that they would've never expected from the likes of Solar Lee. The sight causes them to flinch, rushing away as fast as they could possibly manage.

Me and Sunwoo breaking up?

Maybe not if he would just fucking do what she asked him to.

Still, she was lucky she had an alternative when her boyfriend had told her no. Out of all people, to think he was the one who was going to betray her like that, it annoyed her to her core. 

Her green eyes are full of what was beginning to feel like everlasting anger, searching and searching for the familiar boy. Just as she's about to likely have a mental shutdown right in the middle of the hall, she sees him coming out of the bathroom in her peripheral vision.

Solar lets out an exhale of relief, feeling as if she was witnessing the coming of Christ. Her palms are sweaty and her legs almost begin to shake as she hurriedly rushes in his direction. With each step she took, she only felt closer and closer to getting exactly what she needed to take her anger and edge off. She would be back to normal, living her life - but probably still not too happy with Sunwoo.

But she didn't need to tell him that because he didn't need to know the actions that were about to take place.

Hyunjoon spots her from down the hall, a beaming smile spreading over the boys lips as he winks to her. She found herself trying not to laugh as girls that were walking by him or just admiring him from the sides had caught his action towards the particular girl. Their eyes wander to her as jealousy had raged in their bones, and the sight of them narrowing their eyes at her as if they wanted her to explode right there was funny to her. 

It wasn't anything new. Hyunjoon was always a ladies man. She found it almost gross, the way he had girls wrapped around each of his fingers. She guessed they were just obsessed with the entire "bad boy" vibe that he had, from his leather jacket to his face piercings. He was exactly what you'd see in one of those movies - you know, except for the fact he was literally a gang member.

This was also the reason so many girls foamed over her own boyfriend, and why she knew so many of them would be happy to see her relationship over.

Too bad they were shit out of luck.

"Solar!" a voice had been screaming from behind her, "Solar fucking Lee, if you don't fucking stop!" She knew that insistent tone like the back of her hand, and for that reason, she had absolutely no intention of halting her movement.

Trying to ignore the fury that was creeping up her spine from the simple fact that he was bothering her, she takes another deep breathe. You're almost to Hyunjoon, and then everything will be fine, she reminds herself, but her best friend's pruning words only cut back into her head.

"Solar!" he snaps, until he was close enough to grab her wrist. 

Not expecting this action, she curses as she nearly falls backward. The fact he had the guts to do that to her had her fuming as she turned to him, and at the sight of her facial expression, an all-too-knowing one takes over Chanhee. "What the fuck do you want?!" she didn't wait to exclaim, pulling at her dark hair in annoyance, "Seriously, I almost fucking fell!"

But Chanhee could care less about that. All he could do was let out a deep sigh of disappointment, shaking his head as he breathes, "Sol, Sunwoo cut you off, didn't he?"

Ignoring the lump that forms in her throat, she scoffs, turning away so he didn't see the defeated look that spread over her face. "No," she snaps, "and it doesn't fucking matter, it's none of your business, Hee."

Knowing that she was just being snarky because of her withdrawal, he rolls his eyes. "Solar, it's better for you this way," he sternly informs her, crossing his arms over his chest and having to speak a little louder due to how busy it was in the halls at that time, "even your crime boss boyfriend is worried about you for a reason, Sol."

Not wanting to hear a word he was saying, she tuned his voice out as Hyunjoon finally reaches them, bidding goodbye to the girls that kept following him around like a lost dog. His hand goes inside his leather jacket pocket, which Solar was watching like a hungry dog. He waits until he's right in front of her, so close their bodies were practically one, to take it out and put it into her hand. As soon as she felt the pill bottle and the small bag in her hand, a triumphant look spreads over her expression. Quickly, she shoves it in her bag, before the two step a bit away from each other. 

"Oh, my poor Sol," Hyunjoon is cooing as he brushes a piece of hair out of her distressed face, "I didn't think it would hit you so hard, Sunwoo really is a cold one, huh?"

The way he was talking would make anyone assume that her and Sunwoo got in some kind of fight - which was partly true. But it was more so about what the fight was about and what it involved. 

Chanhee knows this all too well as he steps into the conversation, squinting his eyes at the two. "Did you give her more, seriously?!" he hisses to the gang member, who looked as innocent as could be as he meets the boy's angered eyes, "her own boyfriend cut her off so you're going to fucking supply her?!"

Frankly, Chanhee wasn't intimidating no matter how hard he tried. After all, he was literally talking to a gang member. Hyunjoon carelessly shrugs as he takes a vape out of his pocket, inhaling some of the fruity taste before exhaling the vapor into the air. Being that this was right in Chanhee's face, the flower boy curses in frustration, waving his hand around the air to get it to go away. Only then, did Hyunjoon casually hum, "chill out, honey, you're about as controlling at dear Solar's parents," he shakes his head sympathetically, before wrapping an arm around the girl's shoulders, who was sitting there waiting for their talk to end, "look at her, her beautiful face is so stressed. She doesn't deserve to feel that way, now does she?"

Chanhee blinks at him, his eyes seeming to ask if Hyunjoon genuinely believed him to be that unintelligent. "She feels that way because of the fucking drugs!"

But the boy only shakes his head, "because she's off them, they help her, hon. Can't you see that?" 

Having had enough of the conversation and figuring that Hyunjoon had her friend under control, Solar spins on her heel to practically bolt towards the restroom. She couldn't handle the waiting anymore, she was going to lose her mind if she didn't get a fix at that second. 

She'd left so fast Chanhee didn't even have a chance to catch her again. He's about to follow her to the girl's restroom, but Hyunjoon grabs the sleeve of his coat to stop him. "Why don't you let her be? Let poor Solar wind down, especially after you've been nagging her like a parent."

Chanhee only glares at him, sending bullets through his skull, before yanking his sleeve out of Hyunjoon's grip. He could care less about what some hoodlum gang member thought of him, it was the last thing that was important. He couldn't let her keep doing this to herself, it was destroying her. 

The bathroom seemed to be empty, which was Solar's luck. Chanhee's shoes slam against the tiled floors as he rushes inside, turning the corner into the main part. As soon as he does so, loud clanking is heard - the sound of someone moving around in a rush and dropping things. It was presumably Solar, and as he entered the restroom, his guesses were only confirmed to be true.

"Fuck!" she's crying as she drops some of it on the floor. She hurriedly used the dollar bill in her hand to snort the last bit, before shoving the rest in her purse like a mad man. She was about to take some of the pills when he entered, causing her entire body to go into panic mode as she rushed to hide them in her bag, too.

"Hey, just a reminder, this is the women's restroom!" she snarkily calls to him, but Chanhee didn't care.

"Give me the drugs, Solar," he demands as he walks up to her, but her green eyes only peer back at him as if he was insane.

"What? No."

"Solar, let me fucking have them," he booms in frustration. And before she could react, he reached out and tried to yank her purse off her shoulder. Solar yelps in surprise, before she starts tugging it back, trying to protect it like a prized possession. "Stop doing this to yourself, I'm trying to help you before you ruin your life!"

"You're overreacting!" she groans, "That's a common issue of yours."

But Chanhee managed to open the bag, he just couldn't get any of the items out. That was, until he pulled so hard they flew out onto the floor. 

Solar screeches in agony at the sight, immediately falling to her knees as she tries to grab them. It was a pitiful state to see her in, as her breath was ragged, beads of sweat forming on her forehead. Chanhee had to put a stop to it, as he rushed over to grab the pill bottle. Even if she still had the coke, at least taking the pills would halt some of it. He had to be okay with that for now, as he gripped them in his hands, exhaling deeply.

The brunette jumps to her feet, clearly angered at the fact he was doing all of this to her. In her head, she could only see it as negative, Solar couldn't understand that he was trying to help her because that was exactly what the drugs did - they helped her. They were a part of her she now needed to survive.

"This has to stop," he tells her sternly, before making his way into the stall that was beside him. 

"Chanhee, don't you fucking DARE!" she screams at the top of her lungs, but the boy slams the stall shut in her face, just before she got inside. "GIVE THEM BACK!" she yells, her voice booming through the bathroom. Her fists clench as she slams them against the stall door, "GIVE THEM THE FUCK BACK, CHANHEE!"

"This is for your health, Solar," is all he says, that same motherly tone he always used so evident. But you could hear the sadness that was tinting his tone, the hurt that was taking over him, because it was hard to see someone you cared for - nonetheless your best friend - in such a horrid state. Her brain was practically rotting, and it broke his heart to see the bright, nerdy girl he once went everywhere with in such a destructive state. It wasn't her. It wasn't who his best friend was.

Opening the cap, the girl no doubt hears the click sound of it. It makes her fists hit the stall harder, using all of her lacking strength to try and open it, as her screams fill the bathroom. Students that were passing by could surely hear it, but they were likely too afraid to see what was going on.

The sound of the toilet flushing filled her ears, and she found her body shutting down as she realized what that meant. It was her last hope, and her last hope was gone - all gone.

Her throat begins to feel dry as she turns away from the stall, feeling so many mixed emotions taking over her. "I CAN'T BELIEVE YOU FUCKING DID THAT!" she finds herself screaming before she could stop it. She clenches her fists so hard that her nails cut into the palms of her hands, beginning to bleed. Solar didn't even feel it, though, as she stood there, breathing heavily as if she just ran a marathon. 

She felt like she was about to explode, like she would hurt someone. She couldn't believe he would do such a thing to her, she was disgusted, fuming, horrified. She was so many emotions in one that she didn't know how to handle.

The stall door suddenly unlocks, and she jolts at the sight. Her clenched fist finds itself rising, and she no longer seemed to have any recollection of what she was doing. If Solar's punch would have hurt, no one would ever know, as Chanhee's face slowly came into view as he opened the stall door. 

She was about to lunge at him, letting out all of her pint up anger, when two arms wrap around her from behind. 

"No!" she's screeching, but her voice had faltered in defeat as her arms fall limply back to her sides. Blood is dripping onto the floor from how deep her nails had cut her hands, and a sob leaves her lips as she stood there, hyperventilating, in the women's restroom.

"Sweetheart, hey," the voice of the person that was holding her whispers, "hey, it's okay, princess. I need you to breathe, okay?"

She had been upset with Sunwoo after their fight about him refusing to supply her anymore. She wasn't even planning to see him again today, but she guessed that Chanhee had alerted him of what was going on. This meant he knew that she tried to get them from Hyunjoon instead, and Chanhee had stopped her - for the most part. Being that she was still in withdrawal, this caused a pretty bad breakdown from her, and Sunwoo knew this well. He knew how this stuff worked, so he knew what she was going through.

But most of all, he knew how to calm her down and bring her back to Earth.

"Put a stop to this," Chanhee points an accusing finger at the boy who was holding her, "she can't go off to college like this." 

Indeed, they were all supposed to be preparing for college soon. This didn't mean they were leaving the safety of the Castle, it just meant that most of their time would be spent attending one of the Ivy League schools - specifically Colombia, that was right down the road from the Castle.

And Chanhee knew as well as anyone else that if she kept up her act, she would never be able to attend a place like that.

Sunwoo only sends him a look, as if telling him to get out, and he scoffs at him before he obliges. Chanhee had done his part as far as he was concerned, and he needed time to take in everything that had happened. 

Meanwhile, Sunwoo had only squeezed the girl as tight as he could manage. His lips leave lingering kisses on the top of her head, as he coos to her, "it's okay, princess. No one's mad at you, okay? Everything's going to be fine."

"Sunwoo..." she cries out, coughing on her own sobs, "I can't..." her voice weakly fades away, but he knew well of what she was trying to say.

"You can, Sol," he insists, "and you will - because you have me right here with you, and I'm not letting you do this to yourself anymore."


+



"Excuse me, could I run to the restroom real quick beforehand?" 

The memory lingers in her head as she locks the bathroom door. Her breathing is unsteady as she walks to the mirror in the confined space, hands gripping the edges of the counter as if she was about to rip it off the wall. 

Her eyes wander to the mirror, blinking a few times as she stares at herself. She knew what was happening to her, she knew it well. She knew it so well but yet she couldn't stop it.

And Sunwoo couldn't save her this time.

She stares at herself for a long few seconds, trying to ease her irregular nerves and breathing.



She was in her stage outfit, makeup done to perfection. Solar appeared to be what she always was to the public - untouched, endlessly happy, careless, free. How sad was it to know she was the exact opposite of all of those things, as her hands had fumbled with the fuzzy jacket she would be wearing for her to stage.

Only moments later was she closing her eyes, exhaling as if she was suddenly relieved. The pain she was once beginning to feel again would subside in no time, and it gave her the strength to smile to her once dulling expression in the mirror.

Everything was fine, those were the words she told herself. But even Solar knew deep down she was a mass of destruction, a time bomb waiting to go off. 

It was a matter of when it would.



161:00 | the coolest gang member

Everyone around me is judging me, but I don't have the power to say it
I'm not asking for a lot
I just want to be protected by someone
My long sleeves cover my wounds, so it helped me feel stronger
Don't come near me, all my words are full of lies
If I just get out of here, I can do anything, so I said
Go away, I'm not afraid of being alone
I want to look stronger
But I wonder if I really am that strong
So try me, I love the pain, I finally understand it now
Pain is such a beautiful feeling 


Solar, as effortlessly as ever, performed her choreography, keeping her vocals as stable as she had always managed to do. Like every other comeback, she continued to prove why she was the top female artist in her country. Anyone watching her on the television would have never guessed the hardships she had and was going through during that time, they would never have guessed how much she was really struggling deep down. Because all Solar ever showed was flawless performance, complete with a gleaming smile that never seemed to falter.

Little did they know how much meaning her new song had to her.


I want to feel, I want to feel this pain
I wasn't realizing all those songs I heard were such a precious thing
Even though I was singing them so casually
I was told that I was loved
but I didn't notice it
