I don't care if you have anything else to say
Go and hate me all you want
If I keep on going forward
I wonder if the sadness will go away
All the blood and tears I shed
I've painted them into colors, and they've become beautiful


There's a bright beaming smile on her face as she poses for the ending fairy pose. The directors signal after a few moments that she was okay to move and the show had focused back on the MCs. A breathe of relief leaves her lips as she was finally able to catch her breath, turning her attention to the crowds of fans that were in the audience. So many of them were holding up signs and her lightsticks, and they started screaming once they saw she was turning to face the audience.

"Thank you my Neverlands!" she yells out, making a heart with her hands. The crowd screams louder to her as she does this, and she felt her heart ache in her chest. Solar was lucky to have so many people that supported her in everything. 

But sometimes she found herself wondering if they would still support her if they knew everything.

Still, the smile remains on her face as she waves to them, making her way off the stage so they could prepare for the next artist. As soon as she enters the backstage area, the makeup artist had rushed over to use oil sheets on her face and fix any creases. She thanks them with a bow, and they'd only send her a kind smile, letting her know she had done great on stage.

Her new manager and the outfit stylists were likely in her dressing room waiting for her. Knowing the sooner she got back to her dressing room, the sooner she could leave, made her pick up her pace. She also wanted to check her phone and see Chanhee and the others to know what they thought.

Despite everything, Solar felt on top of the world as she made her way down the hallway of Music Bank. Anyone who saw her would probably think she must be on top of the world - because it sure felt like she was.

Her adrenaline still pumping, she found herself reperforming her choreography like a complete weirdo as she made her way down the concrete hall. Humming to herself, she greets the people who pass her, which mainly consisted of other artists that were clearly nervous to be in her presence.

Solar was lost in her own world. Her own bubble of happiness, especially now that it had kicked in.

Around ten minutes later, she was out of her stage outfit and back into her normal clothes. Her manager had cheered for her, along with any other staffs, before bidding that they would see her at the continued rehearsals at the company tomorrow. After saying their goodbyes and swearing her new song would win awards, they left her in the dressing room with Hee and the other friends.

"That was so cool, Sol!" Changmin was boasting as he gives her another side hug, bouncy from the joy he felt after seeing his close friend's stage, "I always forget how talented you are!"

"Right," Kevin is fake sniffling, wiping an imaginary tear from under his eye, "who would of thought Solar Lee would become a worldwide famous idol." He pretended like he was crying until he seemed to have an abrupt thought, his head jerking to Solar as his eyes widened a bit in wonder. "Hey Sol, you think NCT are here today?"

Everyone in the room let out a collective, disappointed sigh.

"Really? You're going to bother about that now?" Jacob shoves his shoulder slightly.

"Cut the weird obsession," Chanhee had finally spoke in annoyance, as he was distracted on his phone, "no one wants to hear anymore." Kevin opens his mouth to speak, but Chanhee raises a finger in front of his face as if to say stop. "Close your mouth, Kevin, please close your mouth."

The boy frowns at how insistent and nonetheless mean Solar's best friend was. Trying to cheer the dull boy up a bit, she pends, "you have Haechan's number, right? Just ask him."

"I don't want to be weird!" he cries out, and no doubt was he about to go on another rant about how much he loved them and he didn't want to seem like some creepy freak just texting the guy all the time. The friends found themselves wondering just how much he had already been texting him from the way he was talking about it.

"Looks like we need to get home," Chanhee had announced amidst it all, turning off his phone after reading a text and shoving it in his pocket, "as much as I'm not fucking thrilled, there's some company back at your apartment."

The brunette knew exactly who he was speaking of. She pipes up at the realization that the members were finally here, and immediately made her way towards the door. 

"Great, let's go!" she's already calling back to them impatiently, and they all exchange looks at how energetic she always seemed to be.

It made Chanhee feel nauseous. He had to do something, it was just a matter of when. There was no way Solar would just normally be this happy after all of that - no way. Especially to see Sunwoo's gang members, which would only involve more talk of Sunwoo's condition and what happened. 

But right now probably wouldn't be the best time.

Meanwhile, Solar had held a hand to her head as she made her way down the halls. The booming of music against the concrete backstage walls could always be heard from the stage, but instead, she heard screaming signaling that whoever was currently performing's song had just ended. She manages to wave to people that pass her, rubbing her temple with one hand before letting it fall back to her side before someone tried to ask if she was okay.

She was fine. She knew well she was just starting to experience the petty side effects that came with her feeling better. All she had to do was suck it up, she used to be more than used to these. 

Little did Solar know, there were side effects she had not thought of. Side effects that would change more than how she acted.

But all she could focus on then was her vision that kept fogging up every few steps. She knew where she was going anyway since the hall was straight until you reached the main backstage area, but it was upsetting that she found herself struggling to see anything ahead of her, as black dots scattered across her vision. 

Another one was a temper, it gave you a temper. And she found herself growing annoyed as she reached a hand up to rub one of her eyes, just as voices had echoed from behind her, "Sol, wait up!"

She ignored them due to the fact she was stubborn. There was truth in her newest song, more truth than she wanted to admit. Solar wanted to be independent. She wanted to stop relying on people to do everything for her, it made her feel like a loser.

So she kept walking as if she wasn't seeing spots and as if her legs didn't feel wobbly. 

"Sol, you might want to stop-" 

Chanhee had tried to yell to her, speeding up to a jog to try and grab her. He knew more than positively now that she was having side effects from what he thought. He also knew how Solar would be acting, and the fact she likely had no idea where she was even going. It broke his heart into pieces at the sight of her that way again, but all he could do was try to help his beloved best friend as he runs, reaching out to try and grab her sleeve.

But it was too late, as bad as he wanted to prevent such a terrible event from occurring, he wasn't fast enough. Solar had stumbled eventually thanks to her putrid eyesight, falling over her own legs and landed straight on the hard floor.

"Oh my God, are you okay?!" Jacob and the others were crying in worry as they rush to her.

Chanhee heaved out a sigh at her obvious state, bending over to grab both of her hands and pull the bewildered girl back to her feet.

"Man," she's muttering, "this stupid shit..."

"Are you dizzy from your stage?" the oblivious Kevin asked her as Chanhee had an arm around her shoulder to keep her from falling again until she regained her normal eyesight.

Solar blinks a few times like a mad man, watching as her sight was slowly becoming clear again. A ping of guilt hits her chest from her friend's question. As much as she was ignoring any emotion, she found herself asking at the back of her head if what she was doing was really okay. How would her friends react if they found out? Chanhee was going to kill her. How would Jungkook react? He had never seen her when she this way - as in, he'd never seen the wrath of her withdrawal.

But once she started, she knew there was no stopping, not unless hell broke loose. 

And Solar was going to avoid that at all costs. She was just feeling better, that was it, there weren't any other factors involved. Of course she was still hurt over Sunwoo, and of course she missed him, and of course a part of her would always be upset over the fight with Jinhwan. But that didn't mean she couldn't find happiness, she still had Jungkook and her friends. Solar was just getting better, she repeated to herself in her head, over and over until she felt confident in her reasoning.

With that, she shrugs as they finally make it to the exit of Music Bank, "yeah, something like that..."


+


Solar had practically burst down her apartment door. She was screeching like an animal as she threw her bags beside the door, and at the sound of her high-pitched noises, everyone's attention had been diverted to her. There, in the living room of her brand new apartment, were eight boys she was more than thrilled to see for many reasons. 

Her arms were already in the air, prepared for a hug, as Hyunjoon had ran towards her. "Solar, my gorgeous queen!" he's crying out. But instead of accepting her hug normally, the boy bends down the pick her up by the legs, spinning her around in circles as she coos, "Gosh, are you okay? I'm so sorry, honey, your poor heart must be destroyed. Don't worry, the boys are here to solve this once and for all, okay?" 

It was weird to think that Hyunjoon was also the member who enjoyed literally skinning his victims with the way he was babying Solar, who only hugged him with a giddy smile on her face. 

Confused from all the commotion, a sleepy Jungkook had wandered out of Solar's bedroom. He'd been in there since everyone had gotten here, figuring it would be awkward for him to try and meet all of them without her here. Jungkook didn't really know what to expect, since he had never met them before and had only see Sunwoo. Solar had never spoken too fondly of some of them, so he was expecting it to be a tense atmosphere.

What he was not expecting was to enter the living room to see her being spun around like a princess by one of them. Jungkook halts by the hallway, blinking a few times in confusion as how she was giggling away like she had no cares in the world. He guessed she had to be close with some of them since she knew them for so long, but it still made him jealous to see her grinning at someone like that.

Only then did he take in the rest of them. There were eight, eight boys standing or sitting on the sofas. Each of them had on the same looking leather jacket, though they each had different charms and decorations on their own. Everyone of them had tattoos lining their arms, piercings in places he didn't know even existed, and looked like they came straight out of one of those crime movies. Their hair was messy atop their heads, bruises and cuts on some of their faces, and lastly, weapons tucked into each of their pockets. He found himself wondering how they even got those things into Korea, but it was definitely not his place to ask.

Jungkook would say he was tougher than most, but the sight of eight real-life gang members was a little intimidating. 

"Whoa..." he hears Jimin mutter behind him, having had just come out of the room, too. 

"We're so sorry, Sol," Sangyeon had been saying in the meantime as he gave her a small hug, "we know Sunwoo's state has affected you greatly. As his members, we feel like we've failed, too. For that reason, we promise we're going to find whoever's done this."

Solar shakes her head at him, a nervous smile tinting on her lips. "It's okay," she assures him, "the doctor's been saying he's doing better."

"Fuck," Eric is mumbling from his spot on the sofa, "I guess he was returning that favor, huh?"

The memory breaks into her thoughts like glass, and it caused her wince as she recalled it. She was just a kid, really, a kid who walked in on her crime boss boyfriend about to be shot. That was the first time she ever killed anyone, and at the time, she swore it would be the last. 

But she'd done what she had to do to protect someone she loved. 

As much as Eric's words made her want to punch him, she found herself realizing he was right. In Sunwoo's head, that was probably exactly what he was doing. She know he never let the fact that she had to save him fade away.

"God, this kid," Chanhee is scoffing from behind her, "I know you're a fucking literal imbecile, but can you at least try to have some sense?"

The youngest only shrugs as he looks up to Chanhee who was glaring at him, before he deadpans, "no."

As expected from a gang member, empathy didn't exactly come easy to them. But as she looked up to them as they talked about Sunwoo, she saw it in their eyes. The fact their leader, the boy who had trained and taught them after his father, the boy who gave them a home, the boy who made them a family - seeing him in this condition, seeing his life finally being threatened, and feeling like it was their fault - it tore them apart. It was the first time in her life she'd ever seen Sunwoo's members show so much emotion.

"Let's begin by searching the database," Sangyeon clears his throat, "Jaehyun, come give me all of the details and we can get started." Her oldest brother had nodded following Sangyeon towards the hall, along with Younghoon and Hyunjae who typically helped him with those sorts of things. Sangyeon's eyes catch Jungkook and Jimin, who were still standing there like lost puppies, and he offers them only a nod before descending into the extra bedroom that he had set up all the technology in. 

"They should have a general idea of where to search later tonight," Hyunjoon is telling her, grabbing her arm and leading her to a sofa so they can both sit down with the others. "We're going to go see Sunwoo in about an hour. Jaehyun told us he and Juyeon have been taking turns watching over him, we're probably going to take over that job now," he's explaining casually to let her know what was going on. When Solar's eyes divert to her feet, Hyunjoon raises his hand to lay against her forehead, "oh, honey, you're burning up. Are you sick?"

At his question, her head jolts up to meet his eyes again. She knew that Jungkook, Jimin, and Chanhee were all watching, so she couldn't say it out loud. Her eyes seem to convey a message that Hyunjoon understood, and he send her the smallest nod so as no one else noticed.

"Wait, so are we starting the search tonight?" Eric is asking like the idiot he was.

Hyunjoon heaved out a sigh at his dumb question, not even looking to him as he hums, "Eric, sweetie, close your mouth, remember?"

The youngest heaved out an annoyed sigh, tired of always being told to just shut up. He opens his mouth, presumably to start bickering about just that - but Hyunjoon's attention had already diverted away from him. The dark eyed boy rose a brow when he noticed two people he didn't recognize across the room, and he looked to Solar as he hummed, "Solar, who might the two hunks standing like lost puppies in the hallway be?"

Confused at first, her eyes wander to where his were, falling on Jungkook and Jimin. Jimin was having a conversation with Hee, and it looked rather serious. Jungkook, on the other hand, stood there, leaning against the wall on his phone with a dull look on his face. His bottom lip subconsciously tucked out as he scrolled through something, and she found herself calling out to him, "yah, Jungkook, come here."

His eyes are gleaming when they look up to her, glad that he was finally being acknowledged. The boy nods in greeting to some of them as he passes, making his way to the girl who had scooted over on the sofa to make room for him. 

"My God, Sol knows how to pick the pretty ones," Hyunjoon is observing as Jungkook falls on the cushion next to her. 

She found herself laughing at Jungkook's expression, as he clearly had no idea what to do. This was his first time meeting them, so she figured it would be best to start with an introduction. "Kookie, this is Hyunjoon, he's the only gang member I've never hated," she explains, and the boy sends Jungkook a wink, causing her to shake her head, "he's - uh - he's different."

"I'm just honest," he says, as if correcting her, wrapping an arm around her shoulders as he motions to the air as if he was displaying a picture, "and I enjoy killing people, but it's really just a hobby. It's 2021, you know? We have to be open about these things."

Solar exhaled deeply, about to speak up and tell him absolutely no one wanted to hear about that. She knew that once Hyunjoon started talking about his weird likings, he would do so for a good while. Before she could stop him, he reached into his leather jacket pocket, pulling out a gleaming sharp object. It was a knife, one of the sharpest knives Jungkook had ever seen. The handle was shiny dark wood, carved into it were symbols he couldn't make out from where he was sitting. It looked deadly, and that was confirmed from the fact it was covered in blood. 

"This is my good luck charm," he's sharing, looking down at the knife in his hand like it was his most prized possession, "magnificent, right?"

"Hyunjoon," Solar breaks him out of his loving gaze, "I really don't think Jungkook wants to hear about that."

"Right," he breathes, tucking the object back in his pocket and leaning back into the sofa, his eyes wandering back to Jungkook as he quizzically pends, "you're one of those good boys, huh?"

Jungkook blinks a few times, still not knowing how to respond to anything he was saying. "I wouldn't really say-"

"-Oh, honey, I can tell," Hyunjoon yawns, "but I see that gun in your jeans, I just hope you'll know how to use it if the time comes."

Meanwhile, Chanhee and Jimin's hushed conversation had finally been coming to an end. It was true Jimin had also noticed something just was off but he hadn't wanted to bother Chanhee with it because he felt like the boy had enough going on. Jimin also just had a suspicion, he hadn't ever seen Solar when she was on drugs - nor had he seen anyone when they were on drugs - he didn't fully understand what it did to someone because he hadn't grown up around things like that. 

Hee, on the other hand, had witnessed it all - he knew the highs and the lows, specifically of Solar and her usage. It was why he felt the need to talk to Jimin, knowing he was close with her and he would be the best option as her other best friend. 

Jimin was glad that Chanhee felt like he could talk to him about it. But he wasn't sure if he was glad he knew Solar well enough to realize there was something wrong or if he was sad due to the fact this meant his suspicions were correct, at least according to Chanhee.

"We can't do anything right now," the pink haired boy sighed, rubbing his temple with one hand, "she has too much happening between Jinhwan, her comeback, Sunwoo, and now the gang's search... forcing her into withdrawal will be bad for all of us."

Not really understanding, Jimin blinks at him in confusion. "So, you think it's best to wait?"

For the first time, Chanhee felt unsure. His eyes wandered to Solar, who was laughing like she was the happiest girl in the world as she wrapped her arms around Jungkook and Hyunjoon, pulling them into a hug. He wished that she was happy under better circumstances, that she was happy because she genuinely felt that way. But he knew that if she wasn't under the influence, there was no telling how low she would be. Chanhee realized then, he wasn't really sure when she started to know if she had been on them the entire time since the incident, but it was definitely likely.

"I don't know," he finally sighs, "but I think it's more of what we have to do right now. Just keep an eye on her, okay?"


162:00 | heroin state of mind

"Oh, Solar, honey," Hyunjoon is shaking his head as he's standing there in between her legs, holding a hand to the side of her head, "you poor thing, don't worry, I would never forget my stash." The brunette girl was sitting on the edge of the counter in her bathroom that was connected to her bedroom, a pouty look on her face as she met Hyunjoon's eyes. Their position - and perhaps the way they acted with each other in general - might appear a little odd to anyone that hadn't grown up around them, but it was fairly normal for them to act that way. He had always been the one she got along with the most because of his different personality, and the fact he always treated her like she was some form of royalty. Really, it was just Hyunjoon being a ladies man, but he didn't mean it in that way towards her. More so, he knew the kind of shit she put up with when being with Sunwoo, and he tried to ease it the best he could. 

Reaching into his pockets, he pulls out a bottle, handing it to the eager girl. She takes it almost instantly, popping the cap off and taking a couple out. Throwing her head back, she didn't even care for water as she shoves them in her mouth, swallowing angrily. The feeling of the pills going down her throat caused her to let out an eased sigh as she looks back to Hyunjoon. The otherwise heartless gang member had a dull look on his face as he peered up at her from his stance. She didn't think he would bother talking about anything sensitive, until he sighed, "you're really going through it, aren't you, sweetie?" he shakes his head, the chains he wore practically everywhere on his body jingling as he turns away to pace across the bathroom, "It's still hard for me to believe, too."

Sunwoo beat his way into her brain, and she found herself biting her lip as she diverted her eyes to her feet. It still hurt, it still hurt so bad, and she couldn't imagine how bad it would be if she didn't have the drugs to numb it. They made everything seem okay even when she knew it wasn't. It was like her brain was permanently convinced she was happy no matter what. It was the only reason she could so casually watch iKON's stage earlier, the only reason she was as bouncy as ever like nothing happened, and the only reason she wasn't having panic attacks anymore when she thought of Sunwoo. 

"It was right in front of me..." she mutters, her voice barely audible as the event replays in her head. She felt like she needed to talk to one of the members about it, as she had only briefly explained the occurrences, and they hadn't forced her to do more since they knew it was hard on her. "Seeing him there like that... it doesn't feel real," she whispers, raising one of her hands up in front of her face. Solar looked like a lost child as she squinted her eyes at her hand, opening and closing her fist as if to comprehend that she was moving it, that she was real. Because for a while now, she hadn't felt real, she just turned everything off, disconnected. "Every time I see him there, I keep thinking he's going to sit up and laugh at me, tell me it's some sort of sick joke to prove how much I cared."

"He knows you care, honey," Hyunjoon soothes her, "we know it's harder on you than any of us. I never thought I'd see the day you two were apart. Especially when you walked through that door, I could tell by the way he looked at you then he wasn't letting you go ever again," he diverted his eyes to his feet. His mouth opened and closed a few times, as if he was debating - for the first time ever - on what to say next.

"I never thought I'd see the day we lost our leader," he finally breathes, squeezing his eyes shut so that he didn't appear weak in such a moment. But Solar knew, Solar knew he was holding in all of that emotion. She knew all of them were holding in all of their emotions. It was Sunwoo, their leader, their leader that made them all feel strong, their leader that raised them, their leader that gave them a family when they hadn't had one. 

All of them had gone through so much together, and to lose the main component of that family, was heart wrenching for anyone. So, people could say that gang members were cold, they were heartless, they killed people with ill intent - and maybe those things were true. Maybe they didn't have consideration for others, maybe they didn't care how they hurt people, but it was all a means of surviving in their world. 

But when it came to each other, losing someone was the one thing that could bring their humanity out. And she saw it then, as Hyunjoon turned away from her to face the wall, exhaling as his voice growled lowly, "we'll find them, Sol. We'll fucking find them, and I promise I'm going to be the first one to personally skin them alive."

She knew he meant every word. 

Before she could say another word, Hyunjoon exhales as if to regain his momentarily weak composure, reaching into the bag he had with him as he simultaneously speaks, "now, I have a wide selection going on right now, I would usually offer you something minimal - but, since it's hard times right now, I feel like you might need this more than I do." Solar wasn't sure what he meant, until he pulls out a syringe filled with God knew what. 

To be honest, she didn't really care to ask, she just shrugs and holds out her arm. Hyunjoon finds himself giggling as he didn't wait to inject it into her vein, "it's your favorite~," he coos, and she had an idea as to what it was by then. A breathe of relief leaves her lips as he finished, chunking the syringe back into his bag. "Here, keep this somewhere safe, just whenever you need a quick one," he hums, pulling out a small baggy with the powdered version. 

Solar sends him a grateful smile, "I don't know what I'd do without you." Taking it from his hands, the safest place she could seem to think of right then was inside of her bra. 

"Anything for you, my dear," Hyunjoon charms, before he sighs as the remembrance of the busy night ahead of them, "as much as it would thrill me to spend more time with my lovely Solar, I'm afraid I have a night of murder ahead of me. Speaking of, your sweet boy toy is probably wondering where you are, too - you should probably tend to him."

Climbing off the counter, she was beginning to feel much better already as she blinks at him, "the way you say it makes him sound like a plant or something."

Opening the bathroom door, Hyunjoon walks out first as he cajols, "what's wrong with that? As long as you can smoke him, you know?"

Solar chuckles in disbelief. By then, the two were entering the living room of her apartment, where everyone was lounging around still. The gang members, minus a few that were watching over Sunwoo, and her siblings were crowded around the equipment set up on her coffee table, chatting amongst themselves as they seemed well at work. She found herself wondering if she should ask about their progress on finding out the details of where her old manager had left to, but decided against it. Something inside of her was telling her not to worry about it - perhaps something that had just literally been injected in her vein.

So she wouldn't. Instead, she searches for Jungkook, but he seemed to be MIA. 

Knowing he had to be in her bedroom, she spins on her heel to head back down the hall, when a nervous Jimin appears in front of her. She hadn't noticed him walking towards her previously, as he worriedly hums, "hey, how are you feeling? Chanhee told me you had a bit of a fainting fit after your show today."

Thinking back to what happened during her mere moment of weakness, she purses her lips. Pushing locks of brown hair behind her ears, her eyes dart elsewhere that wasn't Jimin, focusing on the walls of her apartment as she responds casually, "mm, I'm alright, Chim. Just not so used to performing anymore, I guess, and I skipped out on meals today like an idiot."

Jimin tried not to show he didn't believe her. Though that was a fair answer, he shakes his head, lecturing her, "aw, come on, Mi. You know you can't skip out on meals especially when you have a show."

Solar giggles, but it wasn't a nervous one. She was too good at acting. She had done this far too many times. It was something else that Chanhee warned him of, you would likely struggle to ever catch her in the act - to ever catch her failing her acting. She was good, skilled, she knew how to avoid doing exactly what you expected her to do to confirm that she was really back on them. 

The only way you would know for sure, is by the health signs she exhibits when she's off them for any period of time - just like today when she had a dizzy fit. 

"Yeah, I know, don't worry about me," she shrugs it off with a reassuring smile, "I'm just trying to get through these promotions," before he could say anymore on the subject, she hurriedly ends the conversation, "anyway, I'm gonna go see Jungkook, so let's talk later, Chim." Unable to get another word out, the poor boy only nods as she walks away to her bedroom door. 

Watching her disappear, he sighs, thinking back to what Chanhee said. At least she was happy, right? He knew that if she was truly conscious and aware right now, she probably wouldn't have the energy to do much of anything - nonetheless focus on her promotions. So maybe he really should leave it alone, just until things calmed down. Maybe until tonight, when hopefully Sunwoo's gang members had a hit and hopefully made some progress on this entire nightmare. Then he would do something. Then he would make sure she stopped. 

But right now, he couldn't.

And he sure hoped Jungkook didn't find out in the meantime. 

Meanwhile, a jumpy Solar had entered her bedroom to find Jungkook. He hadn't looked up to greet her, for once, as she did so. Instead, his brown eyes were intently focused on his laptop screen, brows creased downward in concentration. He had a bit of a scowl on his face, as he seemed to be watching a video. 

Wondering what could be giving him that expression, and too drugged out to even use her brain to think about it, she giddily walks closer to the bed. Jungkook's head jolts up to see her there, about to jump on the mattress like a crazy person, and seems to snap back to reality as he closes the video he'd been watching. His once disgusted expression is replaced with a grin as he watches her plop beside him, "hey, Mi, how are you feeling?" 

Solar stretches her arms out above her, before she lays flat on her back with a big exhale. "I'm okay, tired after such a long day, but glad I got the first show out of the way," she hums, before turning on her side to peer at him, "speaking of, what did you think?"

Jungkook observes her carefully, pursing his lips for a moment before he sends her a soft smile, "it was great, Mi. I could tell you worked hard, it seems like it's being received by the fans well, too."

This makes her smile as she lays flat again, "yeah, I'm still working on more music, too. It's really been good therapy for me recently."

His mind was wandering as he watches her demeanor. To think she hadn't said a word about everything that was going on tonight, she hadn't even mentioned going to see Sunwoo at the hospital - or anything that was related to this entire disaster of recent events. He knew that Solar was traumatized, and it wasn't like he wasn't glad that she wasn't letting it bother her anymore. He was happy to see she was done having those horrible PTSD attacks. But it was still odd, still out of place. Though, he felt as if it was a common thing for someone who had gone through something as traumatic as she had to block it out as if it never happened. 

He wrote it off as that. She was blocking it, her brain was refusing to allow her to face the real world problems she had going on because of how much it would hurt her. 

And really, this wasn't far fetched. But it still wasn't the full story. 

But he didn't know that. It never even crossed his mind that she could be doing something like drugs. The only thing he thought about was the possibility of her drinking problem resurfacing, but just like he told Jimin, he would know if that happened. He was with her most moments of the day, after all. If she was downing bottles, he would see. Plus, this was not how a drunk Solar acted in the slightest. It was something different.

"That's great, Mi," he finally responds, "I'm glad to see you feeling better after all of this, I know it's been hard."

For a mere moment, memories flash through her mind like a wildfire. Despite the distraction and euphoric state she found herself in, she still saw it sometimes. She still saw the bullet as it hit Sunwoo, she still felt the sinking feeling in her chest, and she still saw the menacing smirk that was on the face of someone she once called a friend as he pulled the trigger. More so, she still recalled the words that her ex-boyfriend had so hatefully spat at her, as if she was some kind of vile monster that dared to walk the Earth. She wouldn't forget it, as much as she was trying to. She still thought about the state that Sunwoo was in, the fact that there was a scary chance he wouldn't ever wake up again. 

But the drugs kept it from hurting her. 

It was why she needed them. And she wouldn't allow anyone to take them away from her - not this time.



163:00 | the mission

"Well, here's what we got," Sangyeon is sighing as he leans closer to the laptop screen, narrowing his eyes to peer at all of the information he'd received, "full name, birthdate, family members, phone number, current residence, license plate," he trails off as he listed everything that they had compiled during their search that including using the police database - which was most definitely not legal, "we can use these. This is a good start," he confirms. 

Everyone had exchanged glances as they take this in, likely beginning to think about what their roles would be in this situation. Jaehyun, who was sitting beside Sangyeon, was quick to think, "Safe to say that he's not going to be at his house - also safe to say he's more than likely switched vehicles. We could try tracking the number, but if he has any sense, he likely ditched that phone, too. Though, I guess it's worth a try."

Sangyeon mutters a sound of agreement, hurriedly going to a different software to input the phone number that was given in the database. After several seconds of him angrily typing away, he presses enter to reveal the current location of the phone number. Jaehyun had leaned closer to the screen as the smart boy of the gang had zoomed in on the map so everyone could get a clearer sight of the apparent location of not their culprit - but at least, his phone.

And as soon as he did, everyone's face fell. 

"Huh?" Chaerin is the first to blurt, "why the fuck would it be there?"

Some of them were in clear distress, knowing that this had to mean something, but they didn't know what. It wouldn't make any sense for this to be his location. In fact, it was next to impossible. 

"I'm really unsure," Jaehyun mutters as he stares at the screen, as if he thought staring at it longer would make it change. But minutes passed, and the phone didn't move, showing that it wasn't on anyone's person, but still. "There's no explanation as to why his phone would be at YG Entertainment. I want to say maybe it was left when the shooting happened, but they cleaned and remodeled the entire building, they wouldn't confiscated something like that without a doubt."

He made a good point. 

"It had to of been placed recently," Sangyeon let out a deep exhale, clearly more stressed than ever, "I don't know how he could've managed such a thing unless he had someone do it for him. But it wasn't for any reason other than it has to be some kind of trick, some kind of game."

"But the security is serious there now," Chaerin shakes her head, "you're telling me there's still people working with him on the inside? God, fuck that place."

"I don't know, there's only one clear way to find out, and I don't think anyone here wants to send Solar to do it."

Everyone looks around, knowing they more than agreed heavily with that statement. But they didn't know many candidates that were capable of gaining access to YG Entertainment with no questions asked. The only one who could would be someone who worked there. And they were worried about what this meant. 

There was a deep silence that filled the living room of the apartment. That was, until a knock at the door echoed through all of their ears, startling some of the friends who didn't expect such a sudden noise so late. 

And being that it was so late, it set some of the gang members off. Many of them reached in their jeans, pulling out weapons. Hyunjoon was the one who did so first, but as always, he held that ridiculous knife he always carried around closely as he made his way to the door. "Don't fret, they'd have to be a fucking dumb ass to show up here," he's announcing as he grips the door knob, twisting the lock. Despite his assuring words, he kept his knife handier than ever. 

He wasn't quite sure who he thought he would see on the other side of the door. Maybe in some weird world, it would be his leader, there to tell them all what to do. Sunwoo was the only one Hyunjoon was ever really used to taking orders from, after all. He was the only one he had ever respected as a superior to himself. 

But of course, that was only a dream. As his dark eyes were met with someone he had never seen before, and definitely someone that wouldn't be here to hurt any of them. He stares at the boy in confusion, until he notices what he was holding in his arms. 

"My, my, don't tell me there's another one," he's humming humorously, shaking his head as he raises a brow to the new soul, "why, which one might you be? I don't know if I've heard of you yet." 

The boy on the other side of the door looked as if he was seeing a ghost. He knew Jungkook had said something about her knowing real life gang members, and that was confirmed when he saw the man who'd been shot at the company. But he did not expect to see more at her apartment when he paid a visit that night, wanting to congratulate Solar on her new EP. 

Nervously, he smiles, not wanting to ask what any of those words meant. Jihoon pretends he didn't see the weapon that the boy tucked back into his jeans as he bows, "uh, hello, I'm Jihoon - I'm a friend of Solar's at YG, I wanted to come by and congratulate her for her show today," he explains, when a few more of the so called gang members appear in the doorway. Suddenly, he felt like he was going to be sick, as he calls out worriedly, "but if it's a bad time, I can come back tomorrow, no worries, really!"

They exchange some looks, before Hyunjoon chuckles, moving aside to make an entrance into the apartment, "no, it's not at all, dear. I know Solar would kill me if I turned down cake."

Plus, there was something important that he had said - he worked at YG Entertainment.

Jihoon sends him a thankful but shy smile as he enters the house. It would be a lie if he said he wasn't terrified at the sight of all the newfound gang members, who quite literally looked like people from some kind of video game or movie. Though, he felt a bit better once he saw Jimin and the rest of the people he had become friends with, who kindly greeted him as he entered. 

"That's so nice of you!" Jacob is crying as he walks into the kitchen with him, "I'll go get Solar, she'll be excited to see you - oh, and the fact you brought cake, of course."

With that, the boy rushes off down the hall, leaving Jihoon to walk back into the living room, not really knowing what else to do. He notices the hectic demeanor of the gang members and Jaehyun, who were still talking about the plans that were taking place that night. He knew it wasn't really his business, but he couldn't help but listen it to what was happening. 

"There's no way it would be any kind of bomb," Sangyeon was saying in regards to the phone that was mysteriously located in the entertainment building, "to be fair, I don't really have any idea. It's likely there as some kind of message."

"That would make the most sense to me," Younghoon cuts in. 

"But would it be smarter to get that first?" Jaehyun is asking, "I don't feel like we really have the time to wait around until tomorrow, either way. It would be in everyone's best interests to search the house first, see if we can find and destroy any evidence he may have on Solar there - and maybe even find anything that'll lead us to his current whereabouts."

Sangyeon is nodding along as Jaehyun speaks to show he agrees with his plan. 

"I say we go after his family, I haven't had any fun since we got here. Let's not forget the damage he's caused, everyone, he shot Sunwoo, I would say that's a pretty good reason he deserves it," Hyunjoon is insisting in that sassy tone of his, twirling that knife around his fingers, "I don't need much help, anyway. Haknyeon, Eric, and I would do just fine."

The current one in charge seemed to think on it for a minute. If this was any other time, he would decide against it. Sangyeon was the most tame gang member of them all, but he was the smartest. It was why he was in charge in any instance when Sunwoo was gone - as in right now. They all trusted he had the best judgment. And the boy knew it was never good to do any acts of violence that weren't necessary to the mission. 

But, Hyunjoon was right. He had shot Sunwoo, he was the reason their leader was fighting for his life in the hospital, stuck in a state none of them had ever seen him in despite how long they'd known him. And they would do anything to get revenge, even if it meant they had to follow him to the ends of the Earth, kill his entire family, burn down his house - they would do it. 

"Permission granted, Hyunjoon," he finally asserts, "do whatever you see fit, I would recommend taking at least three other people for backup purposes. It's dangerous to travel in small groups right now, especially during dark."

A dirty smile takes of Hyunjoon's face, as he was more than excited. Sangyeon had taken out his phone to mark the address of where their culprit was previously residing, before he offers the laptop to Hyunjoon so he could search the family member's locations. The boy very happily did so without another word. 

"Alright," he goes on with a deep breathe, "Haknyeon, Juyeon, Eric - you're going to be with Hyunjoon... Jaehyun, Hyunjae, Younghoon... we're going to check out the address and see if we can find anything of use," he calls out the plan, before pursing his lips and looking around to make sure everyone heard him and was aware, "make sure you're armed with at least three routes of defense. We have bulletproof vests and any other gear in the duffle bags in the corner... if you feel like you need any kind of booster, talk to Hyunjoon... anything but alcohol. Are we clear?"

Everyone calls out in agreement.

With that, the teams began moving to gather everything they needed. Jihoon was left to stand there in awe, not sure if he wanted to ask any questions about what he just heard. He was more than afraid of these people, of the fact that things like this were real and not just something you saw in a TV show. His head felt like it was spinning the more he thought about it. How could they kill people so casually, use weapons like nothing? It was crazy to him, the last thing he ever thought he would hear when he became friends with Korea's most famous idol. 

But here he was. 

"Hey Jihoon!" a voice had called from the hallway behind him, and he turns to see a waving Solar, "I heard you brought cake, thank you!"

He's about to walk over and talk to her, where he could finally congratulate her like he wanted to do all along. And more so, just ask how she was doing, even though he knew she was probably tired of being asked that. 

But before he could, Jaehyun had walked up to her, pulling her aside into the kitchen to tell her about the events that were occurring tonight. 

Solar had been staring at her brother a little surprised as he relayed everything to her. She knew that they were planning to do something tonight, but she guessed she just didn't expect all of this. "You're sure none of you need any help?" she's asking, not really sure what she should be doing in such a situation. But she felt a bit bad that she wasn't making herself useful in anyway. 

Jaehyun nods in assurance. He had yet to tell her anything about the phone that was at YGE, as he knew she would feel the urge to go see what it was immediately - and that was the last thing she needed to be doing on her own. It had already seeped into everyone's mind that they would ask Jihoon to do it, as he seemed like yes, a rather innocent kid, but also fit enough to hold his ground. They were really their only option as of right then. 

But, he wasn't planning to keep it from her entirely. He knew that never went well. 

"We're good, Sol, Chaerin, Jacob, and Changmin are all going to the hospital to watch Sunwoo since the members that were there are coming here for the mission, so keep that in mind," he informs, "the weapons are in the bags in the corner. I would possibly look into hiding one in every room where only you know of it's location."

"Sure, okay, sounds good, Jae," she nods.

Weirdly enough, Solar didn't have much more to say, but Jaehyun didn't have time to stop and fixate on that. He knew that everyone would be ready to go in a short amount of time. "Alright, little sis, be safe. I'm going to talk to Jungkook real quick."

With that, he left her there in the kitchen to ponder everything that was going on. As much as she had been avoiding it, she couldn't help but think about it. 

It'll be fine, her brain tells her, what's the worst that could happen?




164:00 | the harshness of reality

"Mm, this is great, Jihoon," Solar is exclaiming, disregarding the fact she had a mouthful of cake. The poor girl was eating it like a psycho, and had some of the others in the room snickering at her - or Chanhee, wrinkling his face in disgust as if he was witnessing some horrific act. "The berries really make it so much better," she goes on as she finishes another slice of the cake Jihoon brought, "I appreciate it, really."

The boy grins, shrugging as if it was nothing, "it's no big deal, I just wanted to congratulate you on the big release."

"Please never eat like that again," Chanhee couldn't help himself from snapping, "I know you're under some stress right now - but that is never an excuse to eat so repulsively."

Solar had only rolled her eyes, not caring enough to even start with her dramatic best friend. Jimin had nicely cut in to say that Solar's eating seemed fine to him, causing the girl to gratefully thank him for standing up against the bully of Chanhee. 

Despite what was going on, everyone was having a rather peaceful time. It wasn't that no one was thinking of everything that was happening tonight, but they knew they had to cheer up and put on a fun act for Solar's sake. Even though, she would likely be fine either way - thanks to her secret friend, that was. 

The only one who shockingly wasn't joining in on the fun was Jungkook, who was too distracted by his phone. He found himself curiously watching the new iKON music videos earlier, as Jimin had told him something one of Solar's friends had told him regarding what occurred today at Music Bank. 

"I didn't think it was a big deal until I listened to the song myself. You know, nothing moves Mi more than lyrics..." the words of his friend repeated in his head. Jungkook found himself thinking, what in the world could be so emotional in a song written by someone as dimwitted as Kim Jinhwan - so he couldn't help himself. He had to hear it. 

And he wasn't really impressed. He'd caught on to his acts, to his ridiculous antics of trying to manipulate and sway Solar. One moment, he would swear he would never cheat on her, and the next he was insisting that he did, but it was only when they were on a break. All he ever did was twist his words to help himself. The words he spouted at Solar when he found out about what happened at the company had told the rest of the story. Jinhwan could try all he wanted with his sappy lyrics and sad music videos, but Jungkook refused to ever let Solar forgive him as long as he was in her life. And he was planning for that to be a long time. 

He was glad that the stupid song hadn't moved her at all. Though, he could admit he was confused, as Jimin was right - Solar loved her meaningful ballad songs. If anything would move her, it would be that. He couldn't imagine her blankly staring at the screen like nothing. But he liked to think it was just because that was how over she was with him. That was a positive, if you asked him. 

So, he pushed it to the back of his mind. That was, until her turned on his phone screen to see a text message from Koo Junhoe. 




junhoe: hey jungkook, sry to intrude on ur wonderful nite but i wanted to ask how soomi is doing and just for like an update 

junhoe: i tried to come see her after her show today but she was already gone so i figured must be a lot of shit going on, didnt want to stop by and bother any1

junhoe: WHICH IS BIG FOR ME 

junhoe: anyway, everyone including the idiot dwarf have been asking ab her 

jk: hey it's ok. she's doing fine, super distracted for the most part which i figure is good for her. rather her be in her own world happy then having more of those ptsd attacks

jk: sunwoo's members or w/e got here the other day. i dnt wanna type it all over txt but safe to say they're def making progress. me, jimin, and a few others r just back at her apartment with her keeping her occupied while we hope to hear some good news or a lead at least

jk: i know u guys r a little busy rn with the new album, but if u have time feel free to stop by. shes off tomorrow so she would be happy to see u and any of the guys

jk: except for that sack of shit. leave him at home. 

jk: and tell him his sappy cry song is ab as moving as a disney princess movie

junhoe: LMFAOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

junhoe: u kinda rite

junhoe: he will not be happy to hear it but god am i fucking happy to relay the message

junhoe: anyway ty for the update, tell her i miss her and will try to stop by soon 



With that, Jungkook sets his phone down with a sigh. Only then, did he notice Solar's absence from the table they'd been sitting at. For a mere moment, panic settles in his chest, until music fills his ears and his eyes find her in the living room. A smile is on her face from ear to ear, and she's giggling away like a child as she dances around to the music she'd turned on one of her speakers that hung on the walls. The sight was cute, he found himself thinking as he admired her jumping around with Jihoon and Kevin, but it was definitely out of place.

There was no way Solar could be so upbeat and bright right now of all times. She was so destroyed over Sunwoo only a week or so ago. How could she just wake up one day and decide she was fine? He knew she was strong. But this was a different kind of strong.

A different kind of strong he wasn't sure if he wanted to get to the bottom of. 



+



"We're very sorry, we've been trying everything we know to do. I'm afraid his body isn't healing as expected... this could be to a wide arrange of reasons, though, I'm going to point it towards the high dosage of heroin found in his blood... I'm sorry to inform you that he has undergone heart failure... As of right now, all we know to do is place him on life support, though his percentage of recovery is very low right now... We recommend taking some time to process, before coming to us with a final conclusion... Again, I'm very sorry."

The words repeated in the three's head like a broken record. Chaerin had stood there, in the hospital room of Kim Sunwoo, her mouth open like she forgot how to move. All she could hear was the words the doctor had just spoke, repeating in her head over and over, and the continuous beeping of the machine hooked up to the crime boss she once swore she hated more than anything on Earth. But he was also the crime boss who had done anything and everything to protect her beloved sister that she loved more than anything. 

She realized then, as she comes back to reality, her green irises trailing to the unconscious boy that laid on the hospital bed, they couldn't lose Sunwoo. Losing Sunwoo for good would destroy Solar. It would rip her apart. She already lost Jinhwan, which to be fair, was a good thing in Chaerin's eyes. But they all needed Sunwoo. He was important to everything going on right now. He was a dire part of not only Solar's safety, but all of their safety. They were relying on him to wake up. 

"Fuck," she curses to herself, feeling panic rise up her body faster than she could process. She had felt rather mellowed out recently, but right then, this was enough to break her streak of peace. Chaerin holds a hand to her head, letting out a chorus of deep exhales as she tries to figure out what she's gonna do - and more so, how she's gonna relay this news to literally anyone. 

"Hey, it's okay, guys," Jacob cuts in her thoughts with a soothing tone, "let's all calm down and try to keep a level head." Though, she knew he was mainly talking to her. Changmin was in shock, too, but he was nowhere near the state she was in. 

"Fuck," she cries again, but loudly this time, as if she was yelling to the skies. Before she could stop herself, her shoes are booming against the tiled floor until she's standing right beside the unconscious boy. Like the helpless wreck she was, she leans over him to grab his shoulders, shaking him up and down as if it was some kind of magic spell, "Listen, Sunwoo, as much as I fucking hate you and would thrive seeing you die in any other circumstance, you better not fucking give up right now!" She's yelling to him, and something inside of her was telling her that if she kept going, he would open his eyes just to make some kind of smart ass remark about how he knew she always liked him all along. 

So she sat there, shaking his shoulders and screaming at him until she couldn't anymore. Until she was almost out of breathe, until tears were rolling down her cheeks, until she couldn't stand to look at him anymore. Jacob had comfortingly placed a hand on her shoulder, unsure if she would be accepting of any other kind of affection. The two friends, as well as anyone else, knew Chaerin was just a time bomb waiting to go off, and they did not want to set it off right now. 

More than anything, Chaerin felt guilty. Somehow, she felt like this was partly her fault. She felt like she could of done something - like she should have contributed more, told Solar that he was in Korea in the first place. She didn't know what good it would of done, but she was telling herself it would've done something. 

The hard truth was, there were several ways the expectant death of Kim Sunwoo could have been prevented - but it hadn't. And all she could do was dread her big sister finding out the new truth, that Kim Sunwoo was never coming back. 



165:00 | relay

It would be around 9 AM when both teams had returned from the events of the night. By now, most of the friends who were staying back at the apartment had fallen asleep, unable to fight their need for sleep even despite their eagerness and concern for what had happened during the missions. Jihoon had ended up staying the night, too, passing out on the couch along with Jimin. Even Chanhee had finally gone to bed. But there was one person of the bunch who insisted on staying awake until everyone else was back, just for safety. 

This had left a quiet apartment up until firstly, Jaehyun and Sangyeon's team kept pouring in. They didn't appear too tired, and almost seemed perfectly fine as if they hadn't just broken and entered in someone's home. Next, was Hyunjoon's team, who surprisingly enough - seemed satisfied with the deed in which they had just committed. Especially Hyunjoon, who wore a proud smirk on his face as he had entered the apartment. Despite the fact they had just killed however many people as they "deemed fit," there wasn't much blood on their clothes to show that they did so. They were professionals, after all. 

Professionals that never got a break, that was for sure. Everyone had taken seats on the sofas and the floor, chatting amongst themselves of what happened. The sudden commotion had woken anyone who had been sleeping in the living room up to slowly peer around at the new presences in the room. Once they realized their return, Jimin and the other previously sleepy friends had sat up in a rush to hear the news. 

"Well, judging from Hyunjoon's face, I'm going to say that went well," Sangyeon observes firstly once everyone had quieted down, "as for our search, the house is completely abandoned and wiped clean. There wasn't much to see, but we did find his passport, leading us to assume he's still in the country."

"He can't be too far," Jaehyun cuts in, "he's still in close contact with whoever is still assisting him at the company. It's safe to assume it's likely only one or two people left working for him that weren't banned from the company, so they're keeping low profiles." He pauses for a moment in thought, "but they still managed to plant his phone for so God forsaken reason in such a weird place. Speaking of, Jihoon, we have an important favor to ask of you..."

Jaehyun had went on to explain everything regarding them tracking the number to somewhere inside of YG Entertainment. Jihoon knew enough about the situation to know it was severely out of place to be there. He also knew that the thought of possibly running into someone as crazy as these people seemed to be was terrifying. But he would be an idiot to say no to real life gang members. Plus, he decided, at least he was helping Soomi, right? 

So, he agreed. Jaehyun thanked him, saying he would be telling Solar as well and the two of them could find it strictly together. "Here, you very well might need this," he finished, pulling a pistol out of his pocket and holding it out to the boy, "just in case anything goes haywire. If you don't know how to use it, give it to Solar." 

He definitely did not know how to use it, but he wasn't going to tell him that. Jihoon tried not to tremble when he took the weapon from Jaehyun's hands, pretending it was no big deal as he tucked it in his jeans pocket. 

Everyone had settled in, discussing what their next move was. They decided the most they could do was see what was on that phone and what exactly the game was with him somehow leaving it there. 

Jungkook had made it his job to text Junhoe to inform him that there were still people who worked for the culprit inside the company. 

Meanwhile, Hyunjoon had been saying that he had questioned the man's family before he killed them, but they didn't have any knowledge of the man's current location. However, there were still more family members that were still alive. Whilst they all sat around, they'd composed a plan that he would go out and kidnap the last of the man's family. If they still didn't know anything, he would make them find out. 

It probably would of been in everyone's best interests to have kept the majority of the family alive and have already done that - but they could admit their emotions got the best of them. 

Either way, the plan for the day was set.

It was when the door had burst open to reveal a rather shaken up looking Chaerin. She was by herself, leaving everyone to assume the other two stayed back at the hospital to watch Sunwoo. Her hair was a mess on her head from the heavy wind outside, and her eyes were red as if she had just cried a river. Still, she appeared as hard looking as ever - though, it didn't take a genius to see Chaerin had not had a nice night. 

"Hey," Jaehyun is the first to say, rising to his feet along with Juyeon. The two concerned brothers make their way over to her before she could even sit down on one of the sofas, "is there news or did something happen?" 

The tired girl doesn't say anything at first. Instead, she pushes past both of her worried brothers to fall onto one of their previous seats. Her body sunk back into the cushion as she stretches her arms out, muttering something to herself along the lines of I really need a pickup right now, before she closed her eyes for a moment. Chaerin was working on the courage to tell everyone, to tell them the new truth she had just found out. Solar was sleeping, she noticed, which meant it would be a good time to relay the news to everyone that wasn't her.

She still didn't know how any of them would ever tell her big sister.

"Chae, you gotta talk to us," Juyeon calls out, cutting off her thoughts. 

Heaving out an annoyed sigh at the fact she had barely gotten even a minute to relax, she blurts, "Sunwoo's dying."

The room falls silent. 

Even all the gang members stopped their conversations. Everyone's eyes fall on the girl who just snapped such words. A few of them are shocked, a few of them exchange confused glances. Until Sangyeon speaks up, "are you saying there was an update on his condition?"

None of them really wanted to know the answer, but they didn't have a choice.

Chaerin exhales, feeling her emotions taking over again from how horrible all of this was, from how much she hated everything that was happening. She felt about as helpless as she did when she lost her big sister, and now she found herself fearing she would lose her again. Stupid fucking Sunwoo, insisting he do everything himself. Look where it got him, dead.

"His heart failed," she finally uttered out, blowing air into her cheeks as a distraction so she didn't cry. She hated to think she was crying for someone she hated, she swore that Kim Sunwoo didn't deserve her tears, but all she could think about was her sister, her poor sister. "He's on life support as of last night, they said everyone needs to discuss what the next step is. His chance of waking up is slim."

Silence.

No one moved a muscle.

No one knew what to say. 

How did you face the truth when it was as harsh as that? How did they face the truth when it meant that they were losing their leader? 

"Fuck," Hyunjoon curses under his breathe, rising to his feet and disappearing to the bathroom, unable to face what was happening - and more so, not wanting anyone to see him as vulnerable as he was. 

"Wait... so they mean they want us to..." Eric starts to say, but he trails off when his voice cracks. Even he knew what it meant. The doctors wanted them to decide if they wanted them to pull the plug on him, to truly end his life. 

"No," Younghoon is the first to assert harshly, "any chance that there is, we'll take it. We'll wait. Sunwoo will pull through, I don't believe in any world he won't."

The boy might have been speaking from false hope, but it still made everyone feel better hearing it from Younghoon. 

"Are you gonna tell Solar?" Juyeon found himself looking to Chaerin again.

The girl only shrugs dramatically, burying her face in her hands as she mutters, "I have no fucking idea."

"Let's keep calm, everyone," Jaehyun clears his throat, "no one speak a word to Solar. We have to keep our minds on the mission at hand, as hard as it is."

He was right. They had to, especially now. Because all of this happened thanks to him. 

And more than ever, they were motivated to find him. They would end this, once and for all.



166:00 | deja vu in the worst way

"A phone?" Solar repeats what she was just told, blinking in confusion as she peers at her older brother, "he planted a phone in the company?" 

Jaehyun didn't really have to tell her what this meant. She was smart enough to know. The only thing she didn't understand, much like everyone else, was what the reason for doing this would be. More so, she found herself processing the fact that there were still people who worked for him in what was now supposed to be a safe company. How? How had they managed to go unseen? 

Nausea creeps up her throat the more she thinks about it. Though she was happy there was so much newfound security, she still worried that somehow one of them would find a way to harm her. Even though they had all make it clear he clearly wouldn't try to hurt her again physically - at least not soon, how could she be so sure? As far as she knew, they could be anyone. She didn't want to take risks anymore. It could be anyone from her current producers to her dance instructor. No one was trusted.

With that in mind, she made sure that she would be armed when she set foot in the company again.

"I already told Jihoon, do you think you'll be able to get it today?" 

Solar sighs, shrugging, "well, I was off today so they're not gonna be expecting me, but I can go in to work on music, I guess."

Jaehyun nods, "great, make sure you're armed, and don't go anywhere alone."

"Yeah, I know the drill," she mutters. Her eyes wander across everyone sitting in the living room, most of them fast asleep despite it being 1 PM - they'd had a long night, after all. Once they land on Jihoon, he notices her staring, being one of the few people awake other than Jaehyun, and perks up. 

"I'm ready to go whenever!" he calls to her cheerily, trying to seem as if he was feeling perfectly fine. Really, Jihoon was more nervous than ever. 

"Let me just go talk to Jungkook and then we can go," she hums to him. Jihoon nods in understanding, watching as the girl spins on her heel and disappears down the hall.

Her words weren't really a lie, she was going to Jungkook, she just needed a little something first. 

Solar could feel the nerves rising inside of her. She didn't know exactly what was bound to happen when she found that phone, or more so, what could've already happened. But her gut was telling her the obvious - it was not gonna be something good. It wasn't like it would be anything that would hurt her physically, after all. Which meant something she was horrified of. 

It left her feeling sick. 

And the only way she could combat it, combat all of this, was drugs. Closing herself in the bathroom, she clicks the lock before digging in her bra. It wasn't long until she pulled out the powder, letting out a deep breathe at the sight. Just that, seemed to soothe her. 

Only minutes later was she leaving the bathroom as if nothing happened. Perhaps today she just had a bit of a stuffy nose, allergies. Nothing abnormal for her, except the fact she was magically happier than anyone would ever expect her to be under all of the current circumstances. 

With a sigh, she stands outside her bedroom door, about to open it to see what Jungkook might be up to - which was presumably sleeping. Though, she begins to hear a sudden melody coming from her bedroom, and the soft sound of the boy strumming his guitar fills her ears. As much as she didn't want to eavesdrop, she couldn't help herself. Jungkook never failed when it came to songwriting, she found herself wondering what kind of masterpiece he would release next.

She paused for a moment to think back to the last song she had even heard him sing. The memory felt rather ironic, and she shook her head, pushing the thought away. This better be a happy song. 

But the boy didn't sing any words, he only strummed softly, beginning to hum. Even then, his voice was pretty, something else even. Jungkook had a special talent he deserved more recognition for. 

Solar sat and listened to him for a while, until she figured she better hurry. Today wasn't exactly a day in which she had time to waste, after all. If she wasn't under the influence, she probably would have been out the door already, running like a crazy person to the company. But Solar's head was too cloudy, she felt too high in the sky to feel anything like worry - even when she knew she should. 

"Hey, Kook," she finally calls to him as she opens her bedroom door. At the sudden interruption, the boy jumps a bit, hurriedly setting his guitar beside him and trying to look as if he was doing nothing. 

"Mi~," he sing songs, "you doing okay? Did Jaehyun tell you about the phone thing? I'm guessing he wants you to try to get it today, right?"

He threw questions at her left and right, likely out of genuine concern but also to distract her from asking anything about what he was doing. She could tell he didn't want her to ask, and for once, she decided she wouldn't. 

"Yeah," she sighs, "not really looking forward to it, but surely it can't be too serious, right?" She pipes up, causing Jungkook to peer at her in confusion. He still hadn't gotten to the bottom of her different attitude, and it was beginning to bother him more and more. 

"Sure, Mi," he agrees hesitantly, "just be careful, alright? Do you want me to drive you?"

"It's okay," she shakes her head, "shouldn't take too long," she goes on as she grabs her car keys from the end table. Jungkook had yet to say anything else as the girl was running to the door, "oh, and you know I'm going to have to ask about the song when I get back."





+ 





"Do you have any idea exactly where it might be?" Jihoon had been asking, seeming as confused as ever. He'd felt uneasy the entire day, and was feeling even more so now that they were at the company. He just couldn't stop thinking about the fact that there were still evil people that worked here, people that were helping someone so crazy. The more he thought about the situation, the more he didn't feel safe at YG at all. And he found himself wondering how such evil people could have been under his nose for so long. Even though it wasn't exactly a positive that he was now involved in all of this, he felt happy knowing he was helping someone like Solar. 

Speaking of, Solar was in rather deep contemplation. There was no telling what kind of train wreck she would be if she wasn't under the influence, but thankfully, she wouldn't have to find out as long as no one knew about her secret. The two are sitting on the bean bags in her dance studio, where they had been after their search when they first got here had gotten them nowhere. 

"I have no fucking idea," she finally deadpans, an aggravated sigh leaving her lips afterwards, "we sure checked every obvious place, I'm starting to think maybe the idiot genuinely left it in the toilet or something on accident."

Jihoon finds himself laughing, in awe that she was making a joke in a time like this. "That would be a nice surprise, honestly."

Suddenly, a knock sounds at the door, causing the two of them to jump. Solar's eyes are like knives as the security guard had cracked open the door, calling formally into the room, "Soomi, there is someone here to see you, he says he's-"

"It's me!" the annoying voice cuts off the security guard, "It's fucking me, your favorite human!"

Junhoe, she sighed, she hadn't talked or hung out with him for what felt like a while. 

"Yes, you can let him in!" she calls back. 

Within a moment, the goof known as Junhoe was waltzing into her dance studio like he owned it. Solar raises a brow as she watches him come over, falling casually onto one of the bean bags as if he was invited. 

The boy wasn't oblivious, and he catches the questioning look from her. "What? Jungkook told me what was going on, I'm here to help."

"We could use it," Jihoon hums, "we've had no luck so far, dude must've dropped it in the toilet."

Junhoe shrugs, "hey, it happens to the worst of us. You ever leave it in your back pocket?"

The two began to laugh and mess around with each other. They were actually a pretty good match being that they had a similar humor. Since Jihoon was only around him and Solar, he found himself being more carefree than usual. Junhoe was surprised, he never really got to know Jihoon, but he was actually funny.

Solar had just been sitting there, thinking as hard as she could despite how her head felt so cloudy. The girl groans, honestly about to just say she didn't care enough to find it anymore and that they were wasting their time, when it hit her.

The girl jolts up, causing the two boys to stop their antics and look to her in wonder. Her eyes burn holes into Junhoe as she demands, "June-ya, have you been in your studio today?"

Blinking, he thought on it for a brief moment, "no, I just got back from the show today not that long ago. Why - do you think?"

She shrugs, "never hurts to look. I feel like I've walked around the entire company at least five times."

Not bothering to argue, Junhoe nods, rising to his feet, "alright, I can't promise that you won't run into anyone you don't wanna see."

But Solar wasn't even thinking about that right now. How could she? Thanks to her lovely secret, Jinhwan was successfully pushed to the back of her mind. The fact she hadn't seen him in so long also helped.

So the three headed down the hallway of their company, the two boys greeting the people they passed. The only one who didn't was Solar, which had some people confused, as she was too busy in thought. 

Until finally, they were standing before the famous iKON studio. Junhoe looks to Solar, as if seeing if she was prepared for whatever or whoever they might find. But the girl just stares at the door blankly, impatiently waiting for him to grant them access.

With that, Junhoe sends a nod to the security guard, who opens the door for them. 

The three pour in the studio, the door slamming closed behind them. Solar was about to immediately get to work on her searching skills, but she stopped as soon as she had a clear view of the entire studio - thanks to the boy that was now standing in the middle of it, gawking at her.

Jinhwan closes his mouth, realizing he probably looked like an idiot standing there. He tried to find his words, but he found himself feeling sick. His mind wanders back to what he had found in their studio today when he was just coming to work on his songwriting, and he swallowed a lump in his throat. He was going to have to tell her, whether he felt like he could or not.

Finally, he looks back up to her, only to see she was staring into his soul. The only thing was, her face was blank. It looked like there wasn't a single thought in her head, like she was this void of nothing. He found himself feeling more confused than ever, not understanding how that would be possible for her. She wasn't feeling the same way he was?

Though, once he recalled what he had done to her, he guessed he didn't blame her. 

"Really had to come to the studio today?" Junhoe had been sighing in annoyance. 

Jinhwan ignored him though as he stepped forward. His hands - and practically entire body - were trembling, but he knew he had to tell her. 

But how the fuck did you tell someone that?

"Uh," Jinhwan stutters out, his eyes wandering to the two boys that were standing on either side of the brunette. He purses his lips in thought for a second, before he hesitantly asks them, "June-ya, Jihoon, would you guys mind going outside for a second?"

Jihoon looked like a deer caught in headlights, not knowing what to say. Junhoe, on the other hand, had immediately rolled his eyes, "I really don't think that's a good idea right now-"

"It's fine, go outside," Solar cuts him off, leaving the tall boy in a state of surprise.

He wasn't mad about it though, he just didn't think she would be ready to talk to Jinhwan so soon. Without another word, the two leave the music studio, leaving Solar there to stand by herself before Jinhwan. There's a bitter expression on her face as she meets his eyes. You could tell she wasn't fond of him in the slightest, and he couldn't blame her one bit.

And to think he was going to be the one to tell her. He didn't want to watch her crack before his eyes again. He assumed she was just trying to act hard right now, he didn't want to watch her fall apart. 

Letting out a deep sigh, he found himself craving to just talk to her first. "So, how have you uh - how have you been?"

Solar stares at him for a long moment. She seemed to debate with herself for a while, before she stepped away from him to take a seat on one of the booths. It felt odd to be inside the music studio, she found herself thinking. It didn't feel like that long ago that the two of them had still shared one studio, after all.

"Fine," she finally monotones, not really interested in saying more. Just because she didn't think about the incident between her and Jinhwan on the daily did not mean that she had moved passed it - and it especially didn't mean she forgave him. Solar had gone her whole life giving everyone chance after chance, but yet she didn't know if she could ever give Jinhwan another one after all of this. Somehow, someway, his scar managed to be so much deeper than the rest. She guessed it was because of the way she had felt about him. 

Even though most of her emotions were hidden away thanks to the drugs, she found that soft spot in her heart aching when she looked up to him. 

"I know it won't help to tell you I'm sorry," he blurts out, unable to help himself from saying something, "but I am. I know you don't want to talk to me, but I want to prove it to you... somehow... I'm going to prove it to you, Soo."

The girl only sighs, too busy with everything else going on to even begin to think about hearing an apology from him. "Just... what did you really want to tell me, Jinhwan?" She knew there was something important, just from the look on his face. Right now, she didn't have anymore time or patience to deal with his small talk and weak apologies. 

She could practically see the beads of sweat form on his skin. He opens before closing his mouth several times, seeming to backtrack on his words. Until finally, he looks down to his hands, before he holds something out to her. 

Solar was confused at first, she wouldn't have known it wasn't his phone. And she was lost as to why he would be handing her his phone in the first place, until it had sank in. 

Her green eyes cautiously look back up at him. "You found it," she mutters, slowly taking the item out of his hand.

"Yeah, noticed it when I came in here after the show," he clears his throat, sounding like he was about to belch right then and there, "I-I heard about what was going on from Junhoe... I-uh... I don't know how to... tell you..." he trails off, leaving her to stare at him in even more confusion.

"What are you talking about?" she demands, "Did you already turn it on?"

Jinhwan falls onto the booth on the other side of the room away from her. His voice is barely audible as he mumbles, "yeah... I-I did."

Solar is looking at him like he's crazy, not knowing why he would be acting so nervous. She turns on the phone herself, noticing there was no passcode. Her lips form a thin line as she swipes across the homepage, trying to figure out what the hell the purpose of the phone being there was, especially since Jinhwan must know already. 

She opened app after app, but still didn't see anything important. Most of the things that could even begin to help them had been wiped clean. The phone was practically brand new, with nothing on it that could lead them to anything. 

That was, until she opened the Safari app. It opened to an email account, one she wouldn't have thought much of, until she saw the emails that had recently been sent - to news media stations.

"What is..." she's whispering to herself, brows creased in concentration as she taps the email. 

Nothing would have prepared her for what she saw.
