And when she saw it, it was like deja vu. It was like she was suddenly being thrown back into 2018, and there was nothing she could do about it all over again. 

Because people can destroy you with the touch of a button, with one email, one picture - your entire life could be over. It was that easy. It was something that always traumatized her, especially after that year. The year she almost - and sort of did - lose everything. 

And now it was happening all over again.

Even the drugs couldn't prevent the emotions she began to feel like a hurricane. Mouth open, the phone slips out of her hands, clapping against the tiled floor of the music studio. She couldn't move, she couldn't utter a word. All she could do was stare at the pictures in the email, the pictures that were molded into her mind. 

Pictures that had now been sent to practically ever gossip news station in the country - pictures of her and Jungkook. 



167:00 | the perfect ruined moment

"No..." is all she could utter out, her body beginning to tremble with fear, "no... this can't be happening. It's not real..."

Jinhwan was horrified at he watched her. He didn't want her to have a PTSD attack again, he had been glad she had gotten over them. But he didn't know if it was okay for him to try to comfort her, he didn't know if that was what she needed right now. 

"Soo," he calls out to her softly, scooting beside her on the booth, "Soo, you need to breathe, okay? Just calm down, calm down and breathe."

But she couldn't. There was no telling how long it would take for them to start publicly posting about it. It was going to be huge, she knew it was. Her and Jungkook's fame was worldwide now, they were some of the - if not the most - known idols. This was going to be ages worse than it once was, and there was nothing she could do about it. 

"Oh my God..." she cries out, burying her face in her hands. And she cried, she cried like there was no tomorrow. She cried and cried until she couldn't cry anymore. 

And poor Jinhwan didn't know what to do. 

Before he could say anything more, she jumps to her feet, as if she'd gotten this sudden burst of energy. "I have to go," she's muttering, talking more to herself than him, "I have to tell Jungkook..."

Jinhwan called out to her as she left, but she probably didn't even hear him. Junhoe and Jihoon piped up when they saw her exit the music studio, but she didn't pay them any time of day. No, Solar only ran, she ran to the elevator like she was running for her life, smashing her finger into the button angrily, leaving the two friends to watch her in confusion. 

She knew she had to hurry. She had to get to Jungkook and tell him what was going on. 

There was no telling what would happen to her now. 



+



"Has anyone heard anything yet?" Sangyeon is asking, his eyes darting around the room at everyone present. But all he got was a chorus of shaken heads and no's. 

Everyone back at the apartment was feeling rather lost waiting for Solar and the news on what the big deal with the phone was. The only one in the apartment they hadn't checked in with was Jungkook, as he seemed rather busy. When Jimin tried to bother him, he shooed him away, insisting he was very occupied at the moment. The best friend of the girl in question had rolled his eyes at his younger friend, figuring it had something to do with his relationship with Soomi, but quite frankly - he didn't think it was a good time for something like that. 

Still, there would be no arguing with Jeon Jungkook. So all he could do was leave and give the others a shrug, "he's not gonna talk to me."

So, all they could do was wait until the brunette had bursted down the door. The sight of her excited everyone, most of them rising to their feet. 

But when they all saw the state she was in, their emotions quickly fluctuated. From there, they knew whatever she found wasn't good.

Chanhee rushes towards the girl, who looked like she was melting under his tight hug, "Sol, Sol, you're okay - calm down, breathe, whatever it is - it's not worth destroying you, you understand me?" He had been giving her one of her lectures as he soothes her, patting her head as if she was a child. But Solar looked like she couldn't hear him, she looked like she was stuck like a broken record. Eyes wide, staring ahead into nothingness, she was watching the same scene on repeat, unable to hear anything going on around her. 

Jacob, Kevin, and Changmin crowd around her before anyone else could, trying to bring her back to reality. But as they looked at her, they realized why she would be in such a hypnotic state to begin with. 

Chanhee already knew, but it was news to the other three friends. But as they all exchanged knowing glances, they knew they were all thinking the same thing.

"Jungkook..." she whispers out, weakly at first. "Jungkook... I have to talk to Jungkook..." she calls out again, much louder this time. Chanhee opens his mouth to try to talk to her, try to ask what it was that had to do with Jungkook, but before he could - the girl rips out of his grasp, bolting down the hallway like she was running from a tornado. 

Everyone was left there in the living room in awe. 

"A scandal," Jimin is the first to blurt, causing everyone's eyes to jolt to him, "a scandal happened. That's what was on the phone. I don't know how - but..."

Jaehyun had cut him off by turning on the television. His fingers are quick as he flips through the guide, finding some local gossip news channel and clicking on it. 

It didn't take long for them to find what they were searching for.

"An anonymous tip was sent in yesterday regarding two of the world's biggest idols, Jeon Jungkook of boy group BTS and Kim Soomi, Korea's top solo artist as of last year," the news spoke person was saying in that obnoxious tone they always used. Everyone could only watch with horrified eyes as the pictures flashed on the screen. There were several, Solar and Jungkook leaving the hospital, them together in his car, Jungkook comforting her when she was crying. The amount of pictures left them feeling uneasy, realizing how long they have been being watched. 

"It appears the two are back together after their first scandal back in 2018, which lead to intense media hatred for Soomi, leaving us to wonder, why would she risk it again?"

"Jungkook!" Solar practically screams at the top of her lungs as she runs into the bedroom they'd been sharing for weeks now. 

The boy jumps at her sudden yell, spinning around from where he'd been working on his "project." Solar opened her mouth to hurriedly tell him the news, when her green eyes are left to dangle across the display now in their bedroom. It was a cliché but pretty sight, with flowers scattered around the floor, candles lit, and fairy lights hanging from the walls. Jungkook was sitting there in the middle of the bed in the dim lighting, guitar sitting in his lap, and a goofy grin on his face as he looks to her shocked expression. With the lighting, he couldn't really tell what state she was truly in. The boy was too eager to get what he planned out already. 

"Mi!" he cheerily calls, "hey, I've been waiting for you."

"Jungkook..." she calls again, but his name comes out as a whisper. Her feet trail across the carpet until she falls onto the soft mattress beside him. His brown doe eyes peer into hers through the soft light that surrounded them, seeming to tell her he had something special planned. "I-I have to..."

"...Wait, let me play you this song," he cuts her off, unable to contain his excitement, "I promise you'll love it, Mi. I've been waiting a long time for this, okay?"

She's left to stare at him, feeling an ache in her chest. Solar didn't feel well at all. The drugs were wearing off, she was feeling the cravings, she was feeling them bad. They would numb it, make it all okay. But she knew she couldn't in front of Jungkook. She knew she had to be smart. But it was killing her. This was all killing her. There was no telling what the hell was gonna happen to her now. 

But she couldn't say no.

"S-Sure, Kook," she breathes. 

The boy practically shakes with excitement. Taking a deep breathe, he closes his eyes as his fingers begin to softly strum the guitar. Jungkook made it look so effortless, like he didn't have to even think, the music just played itself. She wished that this was happening under better circumstances, that she could mean it when she smiled at him. She wished that maybe just once, they could be normal people, normal people who were allowed to see each other freely, were allowed to do whatever the hell they wanted to do without worrying if someone would see. 

She wished it could be like that, just for one day. 

I could make it better...

I could hold you tighter...

This eternal night with no end in sight...

It's you who gifted me the morning...

Can I now hold that hand?

I can make it right...

Maybe one day, it could be that way. Maybe when they retire, when they get too old that all the fans suddenly forget who they are. Maybe then, they could find themselves feeling free, maybe then, there would be no worry. Maybe then, she could look at him and laugh, thinking to herself that this was the life she envisioned for herself all along. 

A little taller than I was before,

With a voice a little more solid,

All of this to go back to you,

Now I'll spread a wide map called you,

Let's go back to that time

All of this couldn't last forever. It would all end, someday. 

You are still beautiful

Just hold me silently like you did that day, that time

The reason I survived in hell

It's for you, it was not for me

If you know, don't hesitate and please save my life

I'm thirsty wandering this desert without you

So hurry and grab me quickly

But maybe that day was closer than they thought. As Jungkook strummed the last chord before he stopped, his voice softening as he sung the last word. His eyes slowly fluttered open, and he found himself eager to finish what he planned. He found himself wanting to live the carefree life right now. He didn't want to wait anymore. 

"Mi, I love you," he confesses, pulling something from behind him. His eyes sparkle in the light as he holds it out to her. "I want to make you feel how special you are, I want you to know how special you are."

She was crying, she was crying so hard her tears flooded the two pieces of paper as she took them from his hands. Her eyes squint through the tears to see what was written on them. And when she did, it was like a straight knife to the heart.

Two tickets to Bora Bora.

"Mi, it's not as special as I wanted it to be... but... will you be my girlfriend again, no lies this time, just us?" 

A throaty sob leaves her lips, and she buries her face in her hands. She couldn't believe this was happening. It was such a pretty moment, a moment that would be ruined. Jungkook stares at her expectantly, knowing she would say yes. He thought she was just crying tears of joy, tears because of how beautiful the moment felt.

But when she looked up at him, he realized it wasn't that at all. He saw the pain piercing her irises, he saw the hurt in her face. 

"Mi..." he stutters out in concern, "hey, what's wrong?"

"Jungkook... they know..." she manages to get out through her sobs. He stares at her in confusion, brows creased downward almost angrily as he tries to understand. "The whole world k-knows... it's happening all o-over again..."

That's when he realized just what she was talking about.

As if on queue, their phones begin to ring angrily. The call sound echoes through the dead silent room, as Jungkook sat there, trying to process this. He wasn't an idiot to guess that it had something to do with the phone she found. 

And he had no idea what this meant for either of them. 


168:00 | a big turn

It was chaos.

"Fuck..." Solar is cursing, the stress coming over like a tidal wave as she stares down at her phone. At first, she had been getting calls from her manager, but now her publicist was spamming her phone. Her hand tangles itself into her hair, pulling in frustration, as she burns holes into the screen. Yet, she didn't have the guts to answer the call. How could she? She was fucking horrified of what she would hear. "Fuck... what am I gonna do?" 

It was quite literally the worst time for this to happen. But yet, they all knew it would. 

"Sol!" Chanhee is calling from the other side of the bedroom door, knocking a few times before he lets himself in. "Sol, Hyunjoon and some of the others headed out, they're going to kidnap who's left of his family and get some answers as to where he's currently located."

She was too busy panicking to even respond to anything her best friend said. 

Meanwhile, Jungkook had answered his phone to talk to his own publicist. Chanhee heaved out a tired yet annoyed sigh as he looked to the boy who was in the corner of the bedroom on the phone, before he focused back on his best friend. He saw the decorations in the bedroom, he knew well what was probably going down before this. The pink haired boy didn't have an issue with Jungkook, honestly, he was the best one for Solar out of the other two if you asked him. He wasn't an asshole, but he wasn't a pansy either. Chanhee actually liked Jungkook. 

For that reason, he felt bad for the both of them.

"Sol, look at me, look at me," he demands, putting a hand on each of her shoulders so she would finally pay attention to him, "you are fine. This is not as bad as your brain is making it out to be, do you understand me?"

But the girl only angrily shakes her head. "No, Hee, you don't get it," she cries out weakly, "you don't get how it is here. They're going to rip me apart, Hee... it's over for me..." her voice cracks, and she fell forward into her best friend's embrace as she sobbed. 

Chanhee sighed, not knowing what he should do in this situation. With this happening, he didn't know how he would ever begin to tell her about Sunwoo's current condition - and he also didn't know how he would ever approach her with concern for her drug problem. 

All he could do was comfort her. 

Jungkook had been concentrating on his phone call, but he was talking rather hushed. Chanhee tried to listen in to the best of his ability, but he couldn't get much other than some ok's and mhm's. Solar's phone kept going off over and over in the meantime, and when it wasn't ringing, it would ding with texts. There was no telling what her social media was looking like right now, and she didn't even want to imagine.

The hate she had gotten in 2018 was a nightmare. How the hell was she gonna get through that again? Though this time around, she hoped that her own fans would stick by her side. 

They would, right?

Her brain was too foggy to think rationally. It was too jumbled, as the thoughts of the harsh reality she was living in mixed with the cravings. It felt like a horror movie, like her worst nightmare coming to life, like her body was crumbling beneath her. 

She couldn't control her body movements, she felt like she was twitching, like she was beginning to tremble. Chanhee was massaging her shoulders from behind her, glancing at Jungkook every now and then as he tried to eavesdrop. The poor best friend exhales, feeling more than stressed. This was not what they needed right now. Honestly, he was so fed up with this ridiculous joke that he was about ready to go find this guy himself. 

He had to admit, and he would only do so to himself, but he missed Sunwoo. 

The bedroom door had burst open, and in came a giddy Hyunjoon. The boys fringe is in his eyes, hiding the devious look in them as he pulls Solar into a large hug, "oh, honey, you poor thing. You're overreacting, you know? There's absolutely no way they could be mad. You two make such a cute couple!" He's gushing out as if he was suddenly the most comforting human on Earth, before he leans his head down to whisper in her ear, "I'll leave a little something more for you in the bathroom, dear." 

She was grateful. That might be the only thing that would get her through the whirlwind she was about to experience. 

"I'll be right back," she mutters as she'd pulled away from the hug. Chanhee's eyes narrow at not only her, but Hyunjoon, as he watches her leave the room. 

Once he was sure she was gone, his eyes stab darts into the gang member. Since Jungkook was preoccupied and likely not listening, he finds himself snapping in annoyance, "you really have some nerve doing this to her, as if she needs more on her plate!"

Hyunjoon had been pacing around the carpeted room, deep in momentary thought, but stops once he hears the words of Chanhee. Pursing his lips, he shrugs, clearly not seeing the big issue in his actions, "What? You mean saving her? Oh, please, we both know she would be a hundred times worse if I didn't supply her." 

Chanhee scoffs, "You mean she wouldn't be an addict again? I don't see how that's worse."

"Come on now, still guarding her like a parent? Why don't you let it go? Solar's an adult now, she can make her own decisions." 

His fists ball at his sides from how annoyed he was at this. The fact that they knew how much she used to struggle, how drugs ruined her life for so long, and they had the audacity to hook her again. He felt exactly how he used to when they were in high school, like he was watching her destroy herself and there was nothing he could do about it but watch. He was watching the ticking time bomb that was his best friend and praying that she wouldn't blow up anytime soon. 

And it was their fault. 

Before he could say anymore, the door opened behind them, and in came Solar. She had been on her phone, and a deep sigh left her lips as she entered her bedroom, falling onto the plush mattress. 

"I have a meeting with the CEO tomorrow," she informs them, "the staff member didn't say much on the phone, so I don't really know what to expect," her voice gets quieter as she says each word, "let's just hope I don't lose my job..." She finishes with a shaky breathe. It was clear she had been crying, yet she seemed as if she had abruptly put herself together - and it wasn't hard to know how. 

"Honey, I'm sure it'll be okay," Hyunjoon assures her as he plops beside her, wrapping an arm around her shoulders and pulling her in for a hug, "For real, there's no way people could really be mad about you dating someone, that's absurd."

"You have no idea," she shakes her head, "just google me and Jungkook's name with 2018 attached."

A sigh was heard from behind them suddenly, before Jungkook had walked to the side of the room where they were. "Mi, everything is okay, alright?" he says to her sternly, but there was something in his eyes that was saying the opposite. "I just got off the phone with my CEO, he said he'd have to be silly to hate that I have a life outside of my career. The industry's changing, Mi, we'll be okay."

But the girl only shook her head. Something in her was too traumatized, all she could see in her head was the flashing cameras, the microphones being shoved in her face, the screams of the paparazzi demanding to know all the details, the vulgar comments that spread through out her socials like a wild fire. It was horrible. She had never seen anyone conjure so much hate so quickly. 

It was understandable that Jungkook was okay. He was the big star, it would be idiotic of the fans to get mad at him. The one they would be mad at was her. Perhaps it would be different because she was a bigger name now.

But there was only one way to find out. 

"Okay," she finally sighs, sniffling the rest of her worries away. Solar closes her eyes, letting in and out deep breathes. She had ways to get through this, even if they weren't healthy. No matter what, she would get through it.

"I'm fine," she announces as she opens her eyes again. Everyone stared at her in confusion and concern, except for Hyunjoon.

The gang member, on the other hand, had grinned ear to ear, proudly, "I knew you would be, honey!"



+



The sight of the YGE building made her feel sick to her stomach. It didn't matter how many drugs she took, how many times she gave herself a pep talk, she would always feel ill at the sight of her company building. Too many things had happened here, too many horrible things that she couldn't forget even if she was addicted to heroin.

There was no telling what today would bring her. Her mind kept yelling at her, telling her to find some sort of comfort, something that would take her back to a time when it was easier, a time when she wasn't aware of the psycho stalker, a time when the biggest worry on her mind was someone finding out about her secret hookup. 

She missed those times. 

Jinhwan was like a wildfire in her brain, something she never allowed herself to think about. The drugs tended to keep away thoughts of him, tended to keep away the thousands of memories with him that were stored so deeply inside of her. He was a part of her she was fighting to forget. 

But maybe she'd be fighting to forget YGE altogether soon. 

The doors slammed behind her as she entered, her eyes glancing every which way. Every employee in the lobby seemed to stop and stare at her, exchanging nervous glances. There was no doubt in her mind that every single one of them heard the news. She was lucky there was so much security outside that kept the paparazzi from getting near the company. 

She didn't care enough to offer greetings to any of them as she entered the elevator. Pushing the button to the top floor where the CEO's office was located, she found herself closing her eyes as she took in what she was about to go through.

To think the last time she spoke to him was when she was talking about taking a break. Now, here she was, being called into a meeting because another horrible scandal had broke out with her as the main star. And really, she was the only one to blame. 

Technically, she could've prevented all of this. It was her own dumb decisions that got her where she was. 

A ding sounded, and the doors before her opened. The security guard outside of the elevator checked her ID to make sure she was allowed to be there, before motioning for her to go forward. Solar offers him a friendly nod as she does so, eyeing the large double doors at the end of the hallway that lead to her demise.

It'll be okay, she assures herself, what's the worst that could happen?

She knew damn well what that was. 

Once she was standing before them, she let out one deep last exhale to prepare herself. Then, forcing a smile on her face, she placed both her hands on either handle and pulled them open. 

The CEO's office was large, everything about it was large. The entire room just screamed how rich he was thanks to music like hers. There he was, sitting at his desk in that rolly chair, spinning around to face her. Though, shockingly, he didn't look as angry as she thought he would.

Once his eyes were on her, she hurriedly bows to show respect. "CEO-nim, nice to see you, as always," she clears her throat, taking a seat in one of the chairs across from the desk.

He stares at her for a minute, before leaning forward in his chair. "I assume you know why I called you here today, Soomi-ah," he sighs, "it feels like a bit of deja-vu, doesn't it? I thought you promised me another one of these wouldn't occur."

"I'm sorry," she bows her head again, "I'll admit I got careless with being in public with - uh - my friend, I didn't plan for this to happen at all..." her voice trails off, "with the recent events, I have been very stressed with everything."

The CEO purses his lips as he takes in her words. Folding his hands together, he tapped his foot on the ground, causing a slight sound to echo through the otherwise dead silent room. Solar swallowed the lump in her throat as she watched him, wondering what her fate held. 

"YGE greatly values you as an artist, Soomi," he finally hums, "the last thing I would ever want to do is let you go. Upon seeing the scandal, I was heavily concerned as to the backlash you would receive, but shockingly, there hasn't been much negativity." Her eyes widened from what she was hearing, not seeming to believe it at all, but he went on anyway despite her bewildered face, "it appears that with you and Jungkook's popularity, the fans enjoy you together. YGE will be able to recover from the minimal backlash, but it has me thinking of a possible collaboration with him. Say, would that interest you, Soomi?"

There was no fucking way this was happening. She came in here thinking her career was over, and now she was hearing that three years later, people suddenly love the idea of her and Jungkook being an item? 

Holy shit.

"Um..." she trails off, feeling rather speechless, "I-I think that would be fine with me - if it is something you would like to do." 

"Absolutely," he nods, "I think it could be a great future project. I have our publicists doing minimal damage control, we've scheduled you for several more music shows within the next couple of weeks, and a commercial that will air next month," he goes on, flipping through some pages on his desk, "all I ask of you, Soomi-ah, is to try to stay away from any pap's, and keep your social life exposure to a minimal, yes?"

Solar hurriedly nods, "Uh, yes, yes - absolutely!"

"Wonderful, thank you, Soomi," he finishes, "you may be dismissed."


169:00 | wrath of withdrawal

So Solar's promotions continued like nothing ever changed, but so did her drug usage. She couldn't think of the last time she went to see Sunwoo because her brain had pushed it so far back out of her line of thinking. She was so distracted by being in the clouds that it had everyone she knew confused and concerned, as if she hadn't just been thrown into another scandal. 

But after her CEO insisted that everything was fine and he was even interested in them working together in the future, she could careless. There was no doubt that she was receiving hate and backlash from plenty of Jungkook's fans, but she figured so what? She had plenty of her own fans now that she didn't care. 

Or maybe it was just the heroine talking. 

The problem was, the more she used, the higher her tolerance got, meaning the more she craved higher doses. She had been using more than she ever planned to, but she would never admit that it was getting out of hand. Solar was so naive with her usage, she didn't even think Chanhee or anyone had figured it out yet. 

Her song promotions were a walk in the park - only until she began to get cravings. She had been breathing a bit abnormally as she entered her dressing room after her performance, beads of sweat dripping from her forehead. Chanhee and Changmin were the ones waiting there for her in her dressing room, and they had been having a rather hushed conversation when she entered, causing them to slam their mouths shut. 

But the brunette hadn't noticed the weird conversation they'd been having, because her brain could only focus on one thing. She usually kept a stash in here now for when she needed an emergency pickup. All she had to do was get it discreetly, and excuse herself to the bathroom. 

Little did she know, as she offered them a brief greeting before searching around her dressing room, exactly what was in Chanhee's hand. 

"I don't know if this is a good idea!" Changmin had been worriedly whispering to Chanhee, feeling the anxiety tickle his spine. 

But Chanhee was tired, he knew he had to do something. He only rolled his eyes at the boy, shaking his head, before he looks back to where his beloved friend was digging around her makeup table as if her life depended on it. He had to do something. It was getting out of hand, she was back to where she was when she was 18 - and everyone knew how bad that was. 

"Solar," he snaps abruptly, his voice booming through the otherwise quiet dressing room, but the girl had ignored him. It was almost like she hadn't heard a word he said. Feeling impatient, he stomps his foot on the ground as he yells, "Solar!" 

Finally deciding to acknowledge him, the girl heaved out a frustrated sigh as she stood up straight, "What do you-" 

She cut herself off when she saw the bag that was hanging there in between her best friend's fingers. A lump formed in her throat, her mouth watering at the sight of what she needed so badly. Several options flashed in her mind in that moment, but none of them were good ones. She just knew she had to get her hands on it if it was the last thing she did. And she also knew Hee didn't want to allow that. 

Chanhee saw it. He saw the familiar, lifeless look in her eyes. And he remembered the way it made her, he remembered what she would do for it, as the image of her fist flying towards his face entered his mind. But he knew he had to stand up firm to her if he wanted to help her, he knew that now, there was more on the line, they weren't just high schoolers anymore. 

"You can't do this to yourself, Sol," he asserts sternly, "Do you remember how this went last time? Do you remember the consequences of your usage?" he lectured, shaking his head as he tried to keep himself together, "Do you remember how it tore everything apart - you, your family, your friends, your relationship?"

Solar was already taking slow steps towards where he and Changmin were standing. She was still in full makeup, full outfit from her stage, it made it weird to see her looking so sinister when she was previously brightly smiling in front of thousands of people. Her breath was ragged as she hoarsely sputtered, "It's different now, Hee, it's helping me get through everything that's happened. Do you think I would be okay without it?" she closes her eyes, trying not to allow any of the memories slip in. But they did. 

"Can you think of what I've gone through? A broken engagement, two scandals, being stalked and harassed for someone's own personal gain - I lost the first boy I ever loved, the boy I grew up with, got shot right in front of my eyes..." she trailed off, tears streaming down her face as she coughed a bit on her own words, "someone I put my trust in spat it right back in my face. I live day to day not knowing if I'll make it to the next, not knowing what this person's next move is going to be - can't you see that?" 

Chanhee could feel his own emotions wanting to act up. He knew everything Solar had been through. He knew how hard it was on her. But he also knew she was just trying to manipulate him. 

"None of that makes drugs the answer, Sol," he deadpans, "I stood by and watched you destroy yourself once - but I won't do it again." 

"You can't do that!" she suddenly screamed at the top of her lungs, her voice so high pitched the boys were shocked it didn't break any mirrors. Her fists clenched at her sides, her face growing bright red even with the makeup. Her body began to tremble with anger, and they knew what was happening. She was going to burst, her time bomb was running out of time. 

"Sol... please calm down," Changmin nervously speaks from beside Chanhee. 

"CALM DOWN?!" she cries in exasperation, "YOU WANT ME TO CALM DOWN?!" she laughed, laughed like a maniac. Before taking another step forward, lowering her voice again, "How about you give me what's mine, Chanhee, and we can all go home calmly?"

Chanhee didn't want to admit it. But Solar was scary when she was like this. It was horrifying to see what drug addiction could do to someone, the way it changed who they were. There was no telling the kind of heights they would go to just to get what they felt they needed, and he was terrified. 

"I'm doing this for you, Sol, because I care about you - as your real friend," he insists firmly, "I'm not just some gang member that doesn't care about your health, I'm your best friend that's been with you since we were three years old..." he exhaled deeply, his eyes watering for what felt like the first time in a decade, "I'm your best friend that will not lose you again, especially not to drugs."

His words were sentimental, they were something that was special to hear out of Chanhee. Chanhee, who was hard headed, who typically preferred to make sarcastic remarks - but that still always looked out for Solar and his friends, and who swore after what happened that he would never lose another one of them again - no matter what. And he would stick to that, even if it meant he had to take a fist to the face.

But Solar, poor Solar was so out of it that she couldn't think about what she was doing. She remembered a gun that she had tucked away in the couch for safety, and the last thing on her mind was rationality and she pulled it out from between the cushions. 

And held it up to her best friend.

"Hand it over, Chanhee, now!" she snarls, "I don't want to have to fucking tell you again, okay?! Just listen to me, do it for me!"

Chanhee was in awe. 

He couldn't fathom that his best friend would ever hold up a live weapon to his face. He knew it wasn't really her, he knew it wasn't her fault, but it hurt. 

"Solar, you know you wouldn't really do anything to hurt Hee," Changmin tries to reason with her, but there wasn't a sign of life in her eyes. 

"Do you wanna fucking bet on that, Changmin?!" her booming words made the poor boy tremble with fear. She was gone, she was further out of it than either of them had ever seen her.

And Sunwoo wasn't there to save her this time.

Solar wasn't sure what would've happened if someone hadn't stopped her. She wasn't sure if some part of her, some irrational crazy part of her, would have really had the guts to pull that trigger, to shoot her best friend. There were many insane things that drugs had made her do, that withdrawal had made her do, but she never wanted to say that shooting her best friend was one of them. 

And she wouldn't have to, because two arms had wrapped around her, trapping her arms under them and causing her to drop the gun on the ground. It hit the tiled floor with a click, and Chanhee used that as an opportunity to step forward, kicking it away where she wouldn't be able to reach it anymore.

They were worried about how she would react to being restrained. But she didn't move, as the person held her there tightly, she just broke down. Her head hung, hair falling over her face so no one could see, and she sobbed. 

A breathe Chanhee didn't know he'd been holding in left his lips. The boy turned away, unable to look at his best friend in that moment, holding a hand to his head. Looking to Changmin, he exhaled as he mutters out, "Let's go, Changmin, I trust he'll take care of her - I just - I need a minute to think." 

Changmin only nods, following Chanhee because he wasn't sure what else to do. "We love you, Solar," he whispers to the weak girl, but he knew Hee was probably right that they should leave her alone for a while. 

"Take care of her," Hee sternly demands to the boy that was holding her. 

And then he was gone. 

Solar wasn't conscious enough to process just who was holding her. Her body was still shaking, and she felt like she was on fire. Her heart was beating out of her chest even though she didn't know why. Everything inside of her felt like it was falling apart, and while she thought it was just because she needed more - the problem was that she needed it in the first place. 

She didn't know what to do. She just stood there, until her legs felt weak, but she didn't stop her body from falling, the person only caught her, leading her over to the sofa where he carefully laid her back. 

"Soo..." he whispers out gently, falling onto the cushion beside her, "Hey, I'm here, okay? I need you to breathe, come back to reality slowly, alright? Everything is fine, I promise."

The boys voice was like silk, like a soft velvet blanket wrapping around her and securing her in the warmth of safety. She wasn't well enough to process her feelings, to process what was even going on. All she did was lay there, her head on the back of the couch as her eyes blinked up at the ceiling.

Jinhwan reached over, placing a hand on her cheek for a brief second. "Oh, Soo, you're burning up," he mumbles, trying to think quickly as he rose to his feet. He padded across the room to the mini fridge, opening it and taking out a cold bottle of water before placing it against her forehead and holding it there. "Just keep breathing, okay? Focus on your breathing, nothing else."

She didn't say a word. 

Jinhwan didn't have a lot of understanding as to what was going on. He knew about her old drug problem, but he had no idea it had resurfaced like this. He knew that she'd been acting a bit out of place, as in she was suddenly weirdly okay, but he never really imagined she would go back to something like that. He never thought Jungkook would've allowed that to happen.

But he guessed he couldn't be surprised. 

His phone buzzed in his pocket, snapping his attention away. He used one hand to keep the bottle on her head while he pulled it out with the other.



chanhee: jinhwan, i appreciate the help. i suspected solar's drug problem resurfaced a while after the sunwoo incident, but i was an idiot and didn't do anything about it because i thought it would be the best for her at the time if i left it alone.

chanhee: i was worried about the emotional wreck she would become if she was forced to come back to reality, but i can see now it was the worst decision i could've made. i've really let her down as her best friend again. 

chanhee: she's experiencing extreme withdrawal. she's going to be a little out of it for a while. she's gone through this before, she tends to act impulsively because the cravings overcome her. it's not her fault, please tell her that when she's fully conscious. 

chanhee: maybe wait a day or so before bringing her back to her apartment. i need to have a word with the member that is supplying her and put a stop to this once and for all. and i can't have her scavenging for more while i do that. 

chanhee: and lastly, this is difficult for me to say, but i forgive you for what you've done to her. i saw it a long time ago that i saw something in her when she looks at you i've never seen in her before ever, and i know you didn't mean what happened.

chanhee: but if you mess up again, it will be hell. 

jinhwan: oh wow, thank you, chanhee. that means a lot coming from you.

jinhwan: i'll take her back to my apartment. she definitely seems to have a bit of a fever. 

chanhee: ugh please don't mention it. i am already debating my decision. just take care of her. i'll update everyone on what happened.

chanhee: and keep jungkook away. know you two aren't the closest.



"Nani?" a small voice called out to her, causing his eyes to jerk away from his phone and to the poor girl sitting next to him, "Nani... I'm tired..." was all she muttered out, before her head abruptly fell on his shoulder. He found himself peering down at her in awe, she was out like a light. 

He didn't know anything about drug withdrawals, but he was assuming everything going on with her was a symptom of it. Rising to his feet, he sent a text to Junhoe, who should still be in their dressing room along with the others, letting him know to come to her dressing room so he could help him take her out to the car. 

The problem would be doing it discreetly. 

Minutes later, a couple knocks sounded on the door. Jinhwan jumped at the sound, worried it might be one of the staff members. But seconds later, the door swung open, and in came a loud boy calling, "Hello, what'd you do this time?! God, you really can't take a hint, huh - I told you to just leave her alo- Oh." Junhoe had cut himself off as he stopped dead in his tracks once he turned the corner into the dressing room and was able to see what was going on.

He seemed to process for a minute, as his eyes went from the passed out Solar to the boy that was standing beside the couch, "Uh, what the fuck is going on?"

Jinhwan heaved out an impatient sigh, "No time to explain right now, June-ya, I just need you to help me take her to the car."

He was looking at the oldest as if he was insane. "Oh, sure, because that's gonna be a walk in the park," he retorts, but Jinhwan just kept glaring at him. "Alright, fuck, calm down - we can just go out the fire exit or something."




170:00 | she found out

"Withdrawal?" Hyunjoon had raised a brow, not appearing as concerned as Chanhee wanted him to be - yet, he wasn't surprised at the carelessness of a monster like him, "Well, dear, that is typically what happens with something like heroine. It's a shame it's poor Solar's favorite, you know? It's hard to say no to her little face..." he trails off as he looks to the ceiling, twirling that famous knife of his around his fingers as he shakes his head longingly.

The look on Chanhee's face was raw annoyance. He didn't know what would have to happen to get it through this guy's head that supplying her was not the answer to anything. "Don't give her anymore," he snaps, pointing a finger at the boy, who had finally boredly looked back to him, "it's ruining her, she has too much to lose now."

Hyunjoon rolls his eyes. "Look at it from her point of view, she's already lost so much, and now you're going to take away the only thing that makes her feel better?"

"I mean it, Hyunjoon," Chanhee snarls, not caring for the excuses anymore, "for her health, this has to end now."

Deciding he was tired of the conversation, he shrugs, "Alright, I suppose I can cut her off for a while."

A breathe of relief left the best friend's lips. He thought that maybe he could catch his breathe for a second, but he should've known that wouldn't be happening - especially not until he spoke to Jungkook. The boy had come waltzing out of Solar's bedroom, his eyes bolting everywhere as he was in search of her. 

The doe eyes of Jungkook fall onto Chanhee, and they lit up for a moment, likely expecting the girl to be with him. When he doesn't see her anywhere, confusion spreads over his expression, and he walks deeper into the living room where everyone sat expectantly, clearing his throat, "hey, uh, where's Mi?"

Chanhee heaved out a sigh as he turned to Jungkook. God, he did not want to deal with this right now. But, there was no avoiding the inevitable, "she's with Jinhwan."

Jungkook looked like he'd just called him something vulgar. He was in a state of shock for a moment, trying to figure out if he actually just heard him correctly. Last he checked, they all collectively hated Jinhwan. Why the hell would he allow her anywhere near him? He was livid, as rage filled his eyes and he clenched his fists by his sides, before letting out a deep breathe. He knew he couldn't get mad at Solar's closest friend, but he sure wanted to.

"Jinhwan?" his voice is low, as he tries his hardest to keep his composure, "We know what Jinhwan did to her, how is she going to be safe anywhere around him?!"

"Calm down, Jungkook," Chanhee disapprovingly exhales, walking past him to fall onto one of the sofa's, "she'll be back tomorrow, it was just so I could make sure she no longer has access to any drugs."

Jungkook was one of the only ones who had been unaware of Solar's resurfaced drug problem. Chanhee realized that fact too late, as the boy's face fell. A look of confusion and surprise spread over his expression, and he turned to look to the gang members around him, wondering if anyone would speak up and confirm the words he just heard. 

"She's been having some problems for a while," Changmin mutters from beside Chanhee, "we all suspected it, we just didn't know what to do."

"But it was getting too bad," Chanhee goes on with a sigh, "Solar's never handled withdrawal well, she likely has plenty of stashes hidden around this apartment. So, until we find them all and get rid of them, I can't allow her back into this apartment."

Jungkook's mouth hung open a little as he was trying to understand this. To think, he was so blind, that she had been hurting like that right in front of him - so much so that she turned to drugs. And he let it happen.

He felt horrible.

"I suggest you get searching, Jungkook."




+




"Take care of her, okay?" Junhoe looks to Jinhwan expectantly, standing by the open bedroom door and leaning against the frame. Weirdly enough, his words were oddly demanding, "and just try to be less of a dumb ass this time - you know, you're lucky that guy allowed you near her."

Jinhwan had been focusing on setting her down on the bed, placing a wet cloth over her forehead and making sure she was comfortable with a pillow. Once he was satisfied, he stood back up straight, turning to his tall friend. "I know that, June-ya," he scoffs, "it's too late to speak, though, I don't know if she'll ever forgive me..." his voice trails off as he looks back to the sleeping beauty.

Junhoe purses his lips in thought for a moment, until he decided the emotional conversation was just too much for him. Clearing his throat, he spun on his heel as he calls back, "anyway, I'm gonna go fill in the others."

And then he left Jinhwan there. The short boy had shook his head is disbelief at how much of an idiot he was, before closing his bedroom door. The sight of Soo in his bed was something he hadn't seen in so long, it pulled at his heart strings as he slowly walked over to the other side of the bed, sitting next to her on the plush mattress. 

There were many things running through his head. He was trying to figure out what the right words were to say, how he should say what he needed to say, what he wanted to say. He felt as if he'd had so much time to think about it, but yet, he still had no idea what words would make it better.

The unfortunate truth was nothing could take back what he'd done to her. The truth that Jinhwan was ashamed of, so much so that he tried to lie, tried to trick everyone, only until he began to trick himself, too. At the end of the day, he had to face what he'd done. Furthermore, he had to face the hateful words he'd spouted to the love of his life, the words he could never take back. 

All he could do was show her how sorry he was.

His phone had abruptly buzzed in his back pocket, cutting his thoughts off. 


chanhee: is solar still asleep?

jinhwan: yeah nothing yet

chanhee: i wanted to warn you, she's going to be very antsy for a while. the withdrawal will make her irritable though i'm sure you already guessed that

chanhee: whatever you do, don't set her off. 

jinhwan: noted, i will try my best


"Nani?" 

He practically chucked his phone across his room, sitting up straight from previously leaning against the headboard. Swallowing the lump in his throat, his eyes are full of concern and nerves as he looks to the drowsy girl struggling to sit up herself, as she'd reached up to rub her eyes slowly. 

"Soo," he blurts out, "hey, how are you feeling? Let me see if your fever has gotten any better." He hesitantly scoots closer to her, reaching up a hand to hold it against her forehead. It definitely had been getting better, much to his relief. 

Solar had slowly sat up fully, though her body seemed to be shaky, almost like she was having spasms. Her eyes looked around, trying to process her surroundings, before they fall onto Jinhwan. She stared at him for a minute, looking like she couldn't seem to find any thoughts, like her head was empty.

He was worried, he didn't know what she would say. Until she finally weakly mutters, "I really need it, Nani..." she shakes her head, sniffling as she looks to her hands that sat in her lap, "I don't feel good without it... I don't feel good..." she repeated until her voice grew to a whisper.

She seemed calmer than he expected her to be. More than anything, she appeared tired, as if she hadn't just been sleeping for hours. 

"I know, Soo, I'm sorry you're feeling that way," he slowly speaks up, "but... I promise you'll be better without it. I know there's so much going through your head that your scared to face, but I know you can do it, Soo. You're the strongest person I know."

The girl looked defeated. "Not anymore, Nani," she shook his words away, "I'm nothing without it."

Jinhwan felt horrible seeing how lost she looked. Memories of when they first met flashed through his head, of all the times he helped her when she was drunk, all the times he took the bottles away from her, hid them where she wouldn't have access anymore. They were so fresh in his mind, he'd made it his solemn goal to save her from alcoholism back then. It became his life purpose, to make sure she could function without it, to make sure she could be strong on her own.

He had done it before, he had saved her from something of the sort before. And he knew he could do it again, even though it was so much more serious this time, he would do it again. 

"Cheer up, Soo," he insists, bumping her shoulder with his and goofily grinning at her, "you're everything without it, Soo, look at you. Look at everything you've accomplished without it, everything you've become."

"It hurts too much to face, Nani."

"That's why you're not alone," he urges, and before he could stop himself, he reached over to grab her trembling hand, "you're never alone, Soo, I'll always be here."

But Solar looked like she was in a different universe. Her pupils were dilated, her head was spinning, and she had goosebumps up her entire body. He didn't know what it would take to get through her head, all he knew was to keep assuring her.

"Following everyday is a better tomorrow, Soo."






+




"Sunwoo!" the girl is screaming, her footsteps frantic as they slam against the tiled floors. The poor girl was dazzled with confusion, and she was screeching out the boy's name as if it was the last thing she'd ever do. It caused a lot of heads to turn as she pushed through the crowds of students in the hallways, who all turned to whisper to each other about what could be wrong with her.

She wasn't stupid. She'd heard the talk around the school, something about 'goody two shoes Solar' being head over heels for 'bad boy Sunwoo.' Everyone was lost at the fact the two were so weirdly close, because they never really hung out together at school until recently, despite the fact they'd known each other for so long. 

Though she didn't enjoy being the talk of the school, she had bigger things on her mind, such as figuring out what was wrong with Sunwoo. He had been so oddly irritable today, all for a reason she didn't know. It was making her feel like she'd done something wrong. Last she thought, they were doing good. 

Unfortunately for Solar, she was just too young to understand fully what Sunwoo went through. Of course, she knew the basics of how difficult his life was and how abnormal it was for a freshmen boy to be living, but she still didn't fully know or have the capability to understand the types of things his "job" required him to do. 

"Sunwoo!" she yells again as she burst through the back doors of the school. Her breathing was a bit ragged, and a sigh of relief left her lips when she saw the boy sitting on the steps in front of her. "Hey, what's up with you?! Are you okay?!" she's quick to throw questions at him as she rushes to his side, sitting on the step next to him.

He didn't say anything. Sunwoo didn't even look at her. His dark eyes were staring ahead, into nothingness, like there wasn't a thought behind them. He was angrily tapping his foot on the concrete, as if he was impatient, as if he was waiting for something he knew wouldn't come. She couldn't notice it then, but his pulse was abnormal, his pupils dilated, and goosebumps would run up his arms every few minutes. 

"Um... hey," she nervously spouts, reaching out to touch his shoulder. The second her fingers graze his leather jacket, his eyes bolt to her abruptly. The sight caused her to jump, not expecting the action, and she swallowed the lump in her throat as she asked again, "what's up with you, Sunwoo?"

He seemed to come to his senses, likely realizing that he couldn't keep her in the dark forever. More than anything, Sunwoo never wanted to involve Solar in the reality that was his life. The world he lived in was dark, and it had been ever since he was a kid and his father began training him for his "life purpose." When he met Solar, when he fell in love with her, when they started dating even as kids, he told himself he'd never let her get caught up in the vulgar life he lived.

The problem with that was that she already had been caught up in it, from the second their parents had met, from the second his father thought it would be a good idea to mess with the Lee family. 

So, heaving out a sigh, he broke their eye contact as he deadpanned, "I'm just having some problems, Sol, nothing you need to worry about."

She blinked at him, not seeming to understand. "What are the problems, Sunwoo? You know, you can talk to me, that's what I'm here for."

Feeling stressed, Sunwoo buried his face in his hands, groaning a bit out of frustration as he ran his hands down his face before they fell back into his lap. There was a silence that fell over them as he leaned to pull a cigarette and a lighter out of his pocket, lighting it and taking a hit, blowing the smoke out into the air in front of them. Only then, did he clear his throat to dully explain, "I'm just having some rough withdrawal right now, Sol. My father cut us off for a while, said we were using more than we were selling," pause, took another hit, "said in order to keep the business healthy, we have to go rob someone else's inventory to make back what we used."

Solar was naïve. She tried to process what she was hearing, but there were too many things she didn't know. One of them, was the fact that Sunwoo had been an avid drug user. 

"Withdrawal from what?" 

Sunwoo chuckled, shaking his head in awe at the fact she was so slow sometimes. Solar was innocent, she was like a delicate flower in the middle of a plowed field. A delicate flower he swore to protect, and he hated the fact he was caught into telling her. "Drugs, Sol, hard drugs... heroine, the kind that goes straight to the vein," he chucked his cigarette on the step below them, extending his leg to step on it, "it started as a little at first. The guys have always been users, but I tried to keep away, they told me it would help with doing jobs, make them easier... quicker..." he trailed off, thinking for a second on his words, "but - like they teach us in school, once you start, you can't stop - not without a little difficulty here and there. But I'm good, alright? A little withdrawal is nothing."

Solar stared at him for a long while. She didn't know anything about drugs, she didn't know what they did, how they felt. The most she had ever known about drugs was only from TV shows or movies - or what they taught you in school, like Sunwoo said. She knew that he dealt with them, but really, he never spoke about it. 

"Heroine..." she mumbles under her breathe. She couldn't imagine what it would be like to inject something like that into your own bloodstream, it made her wince. "What's it - oh, Sunwoo, you're shaking," her eyes widen as she blurts, having had reached out to touch his hand only to find it was trembling every few seconds, as if he was having spasms.

Sunwoo had jerked his hand away for the first time, seeming embarrassed. "I'm fine, Sol, just a little chilly."

"What's it like?" she blurts. 

His brown eyes slowly look to her, shocked she would even ask him such a thing. Though, he could only expect such a question from an innocent girl like her. 

"I hope you never find out, Sol."


171:00 | open hearts

"Well, that's great, Soo!" Donghyuk had been cheerily crying from where he sat in the living room, "It's relieving that the CEO is more supportive of artists having relationships now."

"You don't look too thrilled, though," Yunhyeong noted, "do you feel alright? You want me to get you some water?"

Solar only shook her head no, feeling a bit drowsy all of a sudden. It felt weird being back at their apartment. Everywhere she looked, it felt like she was drowning in memories. She still had yet to really talk to Jinhwan, and she didn't know if she would have the strength to. But if she didn't, it meant they'd continue feeling awkward. After all, it was the first time they had really been around each other since their fight. She only saw him at the company for a split second when he gave her the phone, but she hadn't cared to have a conversation with him then.

Her head hurt. She was trying her best to fight off the cravings as time passed, but it felt like her body was eating itself from the lack thereof. She was consciously aware of what she almost did to her best friend, and she already knew he was never going to let her live it up. 

Solar wasn't surprised by her actions. She had been through this before. She knew the kind of things she did when she was going through withdrawal, the problem was that she couldn't help it, she couldn't control it. They just happened. 

She knew her friends were right when they said she needed to get off them. She knew it was dangerous and unhealthy - that it would never get her anywhere good being an addict again. But she just wanted them so bad. Whether it was good for her or not, they sure made the hurt go away, they made reality go away, and it felt like what she needed to get through the horror of what her life was becoming. 

Without them, she was forced to face and process everything that was happening, and it was hitting her like a truck. 

"It's not over yet..." she only mumbles, catching the boys attention as they figured she didn't want to talk anymore, "it's not over until they ruin me..."

"Soo, don't say that," Hanbin lectures, "they'll find him before he pulls anything else, alright? You just have to keep your head up."

The helpless brunette only shook her head vigorously, burying her face in between her knees that were pulled to her chest. There, she sat, looking like a lost animal, as her breathing was ragged and she would tremble every few seconds. The boys exchange glances, not seeming to know what to do to help. They all hadn't gotten to talk to her much at all, and now that they had seen the state she was in, they were shocked and confused. They'd never seen Kim Soomi give up like this. 

Solar was in a daze. Her mind was just thinking about what would happen next, about Sunwoo being gone, all of it. It was all replaying in her head like a broken record until she was crying into her knees, shaking with utter fear as the scene of the boy that deemed to protect her with his life getting shot replayed over and over. 

"Hey, Soo, come on, let's go back to my room, okay?" Jinhwan had appeared from the hallway, gently grabbing her arm and giving it a bit of a tug to urge her to get up. 

But she didn't move, she didn't even acknowledge that he said anything. 

Jinhwan pouted, realizing how bad of a state she was in, before looking to his friends. They all shrug their shoulders, mouthing that they didn't know what to do, either. With a sigh, the short boy leaned over to pick her up himself. 

The trembling girl didn't say anything as he carried her through the hall and back into the safety of his bedroom. It was only when he laid her down on the plush mattress, before sitting next to her, that she weakly sputtered, "I have to go see him, Nani, I have to go check on him."

It didn't take him long to know who she was speaking of. But he didn't get a chance to respond when she whispered into the air, her voice cracking more with each word, "the drugs... they distracted me so much... I-I stopped visiting him... he could be in worse condition now and I have no idea..."

He thought for a moment, bouncing his leg up and down against the carpet. "well, your siblings or whoever still checks on him, right?" he hesitantly asks, "Do they not update you on his condition?"

She looked as if she was in deep thought - and she was. Solar was having trouble recollecting her recent memories, though, until she finally shrugged, "I'm not sure..."

"You can ask them when you get back, okay?" he soothes her with a small smile, "and if you want to go visit him after that, I would be happy to take you."

Her green eyes slowly looked up to fall on him. Jinhwan's caramel locks were falling in his face, the same dangly earring he wore in his ear, that same mole under his left eye, and the same twinkle in his brown irises when he softly met hers. She found herself breaking their eye contact due to the nerves that were tickling up her spine, as the memory of the gifts he'd given her replay in her mind, as the memory of when she heard a snippet of the song he'd wrote and performed at music shows tickle at her brain. 

She knew she wouldn't be able to handle hearing that song now, that was for sure. It would rip her apart. 

"Soo..." Jinhwan abruptly clears his throat, "I-I really want to tell you something - but you don't have to say anything, okay? Unless you want to say it's not a good time - I would understand..."

For the first time in a long time, she sent him a welcoming, close lipped grin, "go ahead, Nani."

"I want to be completely honest - I know I keep apologizing, and that every single time it's something different," his eyes divert to his lap, where he was fiddling with his thumbs like a nervous kid, "I thought it would be better for me to avoid telling the truth, until I was lost in my own pile of lies... the truth is that I cheated on you, Soo. I let my anger take control of me and do something that I regret to this day..." he exhaled, pausing a minute to glance up at her to see if she was still listening. When the sends him a slight nod to show she was, his head jolts to look downward again, thinking for a minute before he clears his throat again, "and after everything happened at the company... I-I don't know... I think I was too shaken up to process what really happened, what Jimin was really trying to do to you... I couldn't admit that she was crazy enough to be involved in something like that, that she likely was spinning webs of lies to me on purpose to get to you... I was embarrassed and ashamed of myself..." he shook his head in annoyance, feeling angry at himself all over again, "those things I said to you - I can never take them back, but I can tell you - I didn't mean a word that I said, and every second I've spent away from you, Soo, is another second that I learn I was in the wrong, that I was a horrible person for ever treating you that way..." Jinhwan sighed, his brown eyes finding a way to hers again, "and I'd do anything to take it back, Soo. I just want you to know that."

Solar's head fell to stare at her lap. He was unsure why, until he heard her sniffle a little. Jinhwan didn't know if it would be okay for him to touch her at the moment, so he stayed silent, wondering if she had anything to say. 

It took her a moment to gain her composure, until she sighed, "We don't really know how much she was involved in or how deep she was, you know? I don't enjoy doing things like that, Nani," her voice grew to a whisper as she was likely replaying it in her head, "but I knew I had to defend myself. I knew I couldn't lose the fight there, especially not when people I loved were in danger all because of me."

"I know, Soo, I don't blame you for what you had to do," he hurriedly assures her, "I'm proud of you. I'm proud of you for being so strong, for standing up for yourself, for even listening to me right now... it means a lot to me, Soomi."

"I could never really hate you, Jinhwan... I could try... but there's not a bone in my body capable of it."

"I want to help you figure this all out," he blurted abruptly, feeling his heart on his sleeve and the adrenaline from hearing her say those words pumping through his veins. He sat up straight, turning to her with a look of determination, "I want to work with Sunwoo's members, help them get to the bottom of this... anything I can do, Soo, I want to do it."

Solar chuckled at his sudden eagerness, "Alright, calm down, we'll have to talk to them and see what they're up to. I'll admit I've been slacking a little bit..."

"That's understandable, it's a lot for you to handle - but really, thank you for hearing me out, Soo."

She never really thought she would be able to smile at him again. But she did, because there was something about the way the corners of his eyes would crinkle when he looked at her, how he would lean his shoulders forward ever so slightly. There was something about the way he was still the boy she'd been so head over heels for since the first time she laid eyes on him. There was something about Kim Jinhwan. 

He saved her more times than she could count. And right then, she was able to admit she had done wrongs, too. She was able to face that everyone made mistakes. And more than it all, she was able to tell him the words she never thought she'd be strong enough to say.

"I forgive you, Nani."


172:00 | the bigger picture


It would be the next day that Solar went back to her apartment, with Jinhwan following. Chanhee had texted Jinhwan informing him that he swore he had found every single stash she had, and that the member of the gang that was supplying her would surely in his right mind stop. He continued to rant that he would not allow Solar to continue taking drugs if it was the last thing he ever accomplished in his life. Jinhwan could always continue to say that she had a very caring - and strict - best friend. 

In Solar's head, she wasn't really planning to stop. Sure, she could at least lay off the heroine, but there were still other options for her that would keep the edge off. But still, she knew she had to help figure out what was going on, visit Sunwoo, and continue doing her promotions for her new EP.

Furthermore, she found herself wondering how everyone would react to seeing Jinhwan again. She liked to hope that since Chanhee forgave Jinhwan, surely everyone did, too. But she had a bad feeling about it all.

So, she'd swallowed the lump in her throat as she entered her apartment. Forcing a smile on her face, everyone had rose from the sofas in the living area to greet her, excited that she was home. The gang members had looked to be in the middle of talking, and had stopped their little meeting to welcome her. 

"Sister, feels like I haven't seen you in forever," Jaehyun had hugged her, patting her on top of the head as if she was a child, "God, have you grown?" he teased, earning a very unimpressed glare from the brunette.

"Jae, listen, I need you to -"

"- What the fuck is he doing here?!" she was cut off by her brothers angry growl. Solar resisted the urge to slam her head into the wall as she turned to see Jinhwan nervously entering the apartment, hands in his pockets and looking like he wanted to react the same way she did.

As if it could get any worse, some of the gang members simultaneously pulled out weapons, pointing them at the poor boy as if he was going to do something to harm any of them.

"Whoa - Jesus Christ, can you guys put that away and calm down?!" Solar is yelling at them, a look of disappointment clear on her expression. When the few members don't put away the guns, she narrows her eyes, "seriously, what do you think this is? Put the weapons away."

With deep sighs, they finally obeyed her. 

"We made up," she informs them all sternly, "I don't want to have any drama with any of you, for real, we have things to figure out."

"I want to help in anyway I can," Jinhwan cuts in loudly, stepping forward to stand beside her.

Everyone cautiously watched as the short boy put a hand on Solar's shoulder, grinning to them all as if he was an angel. Absolutely no one was thrilled. 

"Is this a fucking joke?!" Chaerin snapped as she had entered the room from the kitchen at the sound of her sister being home, "I was horrified enough when Hee said you were with him, but you're really gonna bring him here?!" she went on in her exaggerative tone, until she was standing right in front of the two. Her green eyes narrowed at the short boy, and she pointed one of her fingers at him as if she was ready to pounce, "Listen here, we are gonna have a long, long talk about how to treat my sister the way she should be treated." Jinhwan opened his mouth to speak, but the young sister didn't allow him to as she spun around, crossing her arms over her chest and humming, "plus, what are you gonna do about the hungry tiger in your bedroom, Solar?! You can't tell me you're gonna start switching around again."

The oldest sister groaned, not in the mood for this shit. "Chae, really, that is so irrelevant, we need to focus on the important things at hand."

The dark haired girl snorts as she falls onto one of the armchairs, "yeah, okay, easy for you to say now that you're off heroin."

Ouch.

She knew her sister didn't genuinely mean to be as rude as she was. So, instead of arguing with her, she heaves out a deep sigh, "Look, guys, I just want to figure this out once and for all, okay?"

The members exchange some glances, before shrugging. "Of course, Sol, we do, too," Sangyeon finally assures her, "here, take a seat. We'll fill you in on what we've been up to recently."




+




"We're close, Sol, closer than we've ever been. It's time for this to finally end once and for all," Sangyeon asserts in a confident tone, closing the laptop that held all the information they currently had. 

Solar sat there on one of the sofas, next to Jinhwan, trying to piece everything together in her head. Everything she had just heard was a lot of information to process. She hadn't realized how much had been going on while she was in her own euphoric land and dealing with her career. But they were serious about getting things done. 

"I didn't think you guys would kidnap that many people," she's muttering in awe, scratching the back of her head, "do I even want to know where you're keeping them at?"

"No, sweetie, you don't!" Hyunjoon speaks up with that daring, mischievous smile on his face. 

That seemed to tell her everything. It was better to not ask anymore questions after a response like that from Hyunjoon. 

"Okay, well, I want to be there when this all goes down," she announces matter-of-factly, causing everyone to look at her as if she was insane, "this is my fight still, this all started with me, I want to end it, too."

"I really don't think that's a great idea, Sol," Sangyeon was shaking his head, tapping his chin in thought, "our leverage is that fact we're holding the remainder of his family hostage, we've used his family members to send him a message to meet us there - and alone, at that. But it's unlikely he'll come alone," he explains further, "we also are aware he still has contacts inside of your company, meaning he still has people on his side... the likelihood of him actually showing up - nonetheless alone - is slim. Having you there raises a high risk, Solar, we already know he wants you dead."

But Solar didn't care, she stood her ground. "It's my fight," she insists, crossing her arms over her chest and leaning back into the plush of the couch, "I know how to handle myself, and the likelihood of something happening to me with trained gang members surrounding me is unlikely."

They all collectively sigh. She was right, but they hadn't originally worked her being present into their plan. 

"Do you guys plan to kill him?" she found herself asking, even though it was a bit of a dumb question. 

"I plan to do more than that, dear!" Hyunjoon pipes up. She knew what that meant, but still, he wasn't the one making the official orders.

"I'm unsure of the best route yet," Sangyeon admits with a deep exhale, "killing him would solve one thing, yes. But the probability that those under him have the information he's threatening to leak and will just do it for him is also high - therefore, I was thinking it would be better to hold him hostage until we have information of anyone and everyone working under him."

"But does that guarantee nothing else will be leaked?" 

"No," he deadpans, "but there's only so much we can do there. Once I have a list of everyone under him, I can do my best to hack into their devices and wipe them clean of any information they may have - but there's no telling how deep it goes."

"Oh..." Solar's voice trails off into nothingness. She had a bad feeling about all of it, a bad feeling as to what her future would bring. She should be happy that it was almost over for what should be good, but how could she be so sure? And how would it go without Sunwoo? 

"Either way, we have no more time to waste," the substitute leader had announced, "we never know when he'll act again, and for our sake and safety, we need to get it over with sooner than later."

"So, when is all of this happening?" Jinhwan cuts in.

"Seven days."




+




Seven days.

Solar stared at herself in the mirror for a long time. She wasn't sure how to feel, so much so that she didn't want to feel. Her craving for drugs was still clear, even though most of the drug was now out of her system. She still couldn't stop thinking about how bad she wanted to feel carefree again, because the nerves were getting the best of her at the moment. 

But much to her dismay, Chanhee had shockingly found the majority of her stashes. He really was serious when he said he would put a stop to it, and she admired him for that. But unfortunately, she just couldn't help herself.

Which was why when she found one, tucked behind the mirror in her bathroom, that he'd missed - she immediately jumped in excitement. Turning on the water to minimize the noise from her snorting, she used a makeup palette to do so. 

Setting it back on the counter, she let out a deep breathe as she felt it enter her lungs. Maybe she would at least feel a little less nervous now. 

Lucky for her, she didn't have anymore schedules until later on in the week. It meant she had time to focus on the important things she'd been ignoring. She just had to make sure no one realized she was still doing any kind of drugs - especially not Chanhee. And that boy would be on high guard now. 

With a deep breathe, she smiled at herself in the mirror. It would be fine. 

Everything would be fine. 

"Hey, Soo..." She heard Jinhwan call from not her bedroom, but the door to her bedroom. 

And being who was in her bedroom, she wasn't planning for him to enter. Jungkook had been asleep the entire time earlier, as he seemed rather tired or maybe just had a weird sleep schedule. Either way, it had made it easier for her thus far, as it meant he hadn't seen or had to interact with Jinhwan yet. 

But she knew the knocking at the door and the sound of his voice had likely awoken the boy in her bed. 

Hurriedly, she stepped out of her bathroom in a rush, but she was too late. Jungkook had already opened her bedroom door to stare straight into the face of Kim Jinhwan.

"Wow, you really have some nerve showing up here," he was saying in awe, the rage dripping with each word. The look in his eyes was dangerous, he looked vulgar. Solar had never seen him have such aggressive body language, and it had her growing anxious even despite the drugs she'd just snorted, as she walked up behind him, hoping to calm him down.

"Jungkook, can we not-"

"-Do I? I admitted to my wrongs, I'm more than sorry for what I did, Jungkook. All I'm here to do is help Soomi," Jinhwan snarled back, "have you tried doing that? Or were you too busy while living with her to notice she was struggling so much she turned back to drugs?!"

Not a good thing to say to Jungkook right now.

"Oh, you're gonna fucking regret that..." Jungkook hissed, fists clenching at his sides. Solar reached out to grab his arm, but the boy ripped it out of her grasp as he shoved Jinhwan so hard his back flew backwards and into the wall in the hallway behind him. 

Jinhwan's back slammed into it with a clump. It was clear it hurt at least a little, but he showed no sign of pain as he stood back up straight. But Jungkook was already walking forward, "I'm tired of your high strung attitude, you do not deserve to think you're the best option for Soomi after what you did to her, you're fucking pathetic." He was right in the boy's face, being much taller than him.

But the shorter boy didn't care. He knew he had to stand up for himself, even as the words of Jimin repeated in his head about how Jungkook wasn't afraid to get his hands dirty. He didn't care. He was tired of the same game. How was he still focused on this when there were so many bigger things at hand? 

"Jungkook, the only one with a high strung attitude is you," he scoffed, "you're the one who waltzed back into her life after two years as if you never did anything. Look at what you put her through. Hell, you probably could've prevented this entire thing from the very beginning if you weren't such a fucking pussy."

Yikes.

That one hit hard.

Solar raised a hand to cover her mouth, she was in so much shock from the angry words Jinhwan just spouted. But more so, she was in shock from Jungkook pulling a gun out of his jeans pocket. 

"Jungkook, what the fuck?!" she's crying out, "Are you fucking crazy?! Put that away!"

Usually, Jungkook was the more rational thinker - or so he'd been claiming recently. But everyone loses control of their emotions at some point, it's just a matter of when. And the words that he'd just spouted to him was exactly what it took to send him spiraling out of control.

Shockingly enough, though, Jinhwan didn't move a muscle. He didn't put his hands up as if he'd been caught by the police, he didn't show any sign of fear on his face. The innocent boy just blankly stared back at the man holding a gun to his face, and laughed. 

"Seriously, Jungkook, is this you proving a point?" he asks, "Or are you just proving me right?"

The brown doe eyes stared back at Jinhwan, fueled with anger, as if he would pull the trigger without a second thought. At least then, Solar would truly be his, right? No. Not true. Because the truth was, Solar Lee would never belong to anyone. 

And he knew that. 

Heaving out a sigh of defeat, his arm falls back to his side. "It's not worth it," he groans, "as much as I hate you, I know it's not worth it." 

Solar let out a breathe of relief. 

"I know there's truth in what you say," he admitted in a blank tone, "but think about it, there's still truth in what I say. But the fact of the matter is there are bigger problems on our plates right now," he turned to glance at Solar, who sent him a grateful smile, before looking back to Jinhwan with an exhale, "so, with that being said - if Mi can forgive you, I guess I can attempt to."

Jinhwan stared at him for a long while. It almost felt awkward hearing words like that from Jungkook, the guy he'd been fighting with for God knew how long. 

But he sent him a small smile anyway. "I appreciate that, Jungkook."


173:00 | the one that got away

Seven days until everything went down, until everything changed. Solar was dreading what would happen in seven days. And she was dreading it happening without Sunwoo. 

The thing was, everyone else felt exactly the same. No one wanted to do it without him. The gang members felt weird still, operating as a team without their leader at the front. It felt wrong. Sunwoo was always so firm, so stern and confident with every decision he ever made. He never failed, and it was difficult to believe that after all these years, he finally had. 

No one could wrap their head around it, especially the ones who knew of his true condition. And they knew that it would be only a matter of time until Solar started asking questions.

"What are we gonna do?!" Chaerin was whisper yelling at them all.
